nodes.name: BFRND (Loïk Gomez)
nodes.bio: Self-taught composer and music director born and raised in southern France, now based in the Swiss countryside. Composer behind every Balenciaga show since Fall 2017, working closely with creative director Demna Gvasalia (his husband). Creates orchestral, industrial, tribal techno, and hardcore scores. Has since rebranded as Loki.
nodes.cv: Met Demna Gvasalia in London (March 2016); married in Switzerland (2017). Balenciaga music director (Fall 2017 – 2025). Initial breakthrough via collaboration with The Sisters of Mercy's Andrew Eldritch. Rebranded as Loki (@l0ki_92) following Demna's final Balenciaga show.

nodes.name: Michel Gaubert
nodes.bio: Paris-based music director, DJ, and sound curator — the preeminent figure in fashion show music. Began career as a record buyer at Champs Disques on the Champs-Élysées (1978), then DJed at Paris nightclub Palace. Karl Lagerfeld's exclusive Chanel music director for 30 years.
nodes.cv: Record buyer at Champs Disques (1978). DJed at Paris nightclub Palace. Chanel music director (1989–2019, under Karl Lagerfeld). Current clients: Christian Dior, Valentino, Loewe, Fendi, JW Anderson, Proenza Schouler, Sacai. Notable: 80-piece orchestra at Chanel SS2011 Grand Palais; Fendi Great Wall of China show. Cult Instagram account.

nodes.name: Music Not Diving (Scuba / Paul Rose)
nodes.bio: Music Not Diving is a weekly podcast hosted by Paul Rose, the DJ, producer, and label boss known as Scuba, founder of the influential electronic label Hotflush Recordings. Launched in January 2022 as 'Not A Diving Podcast' and later rebranded, the show brings in-depth conversations with significant figures from the world of electronic music — producers, booking agents, label bosses, PR professionals — covering careers, industry dynamics, and the evolving landscape of dance music. Rose was motivated by the lack of substantive coverage of electronic music in long-form podcast format.
nodes.cv: Host: Paul Rose (Scuba). Founded Hotflush Recordings (2003, celebrating 20 years in 2023). Broke artists including Mount Kimbie, George FitzGerald, and Or:la. Ran 'SUB:STANCE' parties at Berghain for 5 years. Released five critically acclaimed artist albums. DJ Mag Best Live Act award. Mixed for fabric, DJ Kicks, Ostgut Ton. Podcast launched January 2022; guests include Aphex Twin's booking agent, Will Saul (AUS Music), Daedelus, Patrice Bäumel, and many more. Supported by Patreon.

nodes.name: Will Clarke
nodes.bio: DJ, producer, podcaster, and Grammy-nominated songwriter raised in Bristol. Started DJing with an Ibiza residency at 17, amassing 100M+ global streams and appearances at the world's largest festivals. Known for blending house, techno, and electronica with a vocally-driven sound.
nodes.cv: Breakout hit 'Booty Percolatin'' (2016); nominated Best Newcomer at DJ Awards Ibiza 2016. Two Billboard Dance chart #1 remixes (U2, Rihanna). Releases on Trick, Drumcode, Factory 93, Filth on Acid. Founded techno/house imprint All We Have Is Now (2019). Hosts The Will Clarke Podcast — weekly interviews with artists and industry figures.

nodes.name: Annabelle Kline (annabelleklinee)
nodes.bio: New York-based music curator, DJ, journalist, and founder of That Good Shit — an independent music platform for playlist curation, blog interviews, and live events spotlighting underground and emerging artists. Career launched in October 2020 when TikTok videos about 'what your favorite artist says about you' went viral, leading to a flood of playlist requests. Since then she has built a platform that has toured with EARTHGANG, produced curated showcases and open mic nights coast-to-coast (Sunday Sounds with Green Tea Studios), and interviewed Doechii, Westside Gunn, Larry June, Ravyn Lenae, and Toro y Moi. Forbes 30 Under 30 honoree. Instagram: 114K+ followers.
nodes.cv: Founder, That Good Shit (@thatgoodshtmusic, 2020–present). DJ. Forbes 30 Under 30. Live events producer: Sunday Sounds (with Green Tea Studios), curated showcases. Toured with EARTHGANG. Interviewed: Doechii, Westside Gunn, Larry June, Ravyn Lenae, Toro y Moi, and more. Featured in Cherry Tape Magazine, 5 Cent Sound, Half Moon BK.

nodes.name: Assorted Tapes (AJ)
nodes.bio: Las Vegas-based music curator and self-described 'Internet's Jukebox,' known for blending new sounds with familiar ones across Instagram and TikTok. Operating as @assortedtapes, AJ has built a following of 178K+ on Instagram and 262K+ on TikTok through eclectic playlist curation, music recommendation reels, and a strong submission pipeline for independent artists. Has hosted a show on Polaroid Radio and maintains an active Bandcamp collection.
nodes.cv: Curator at Assortedtapes.com. Instagram: 178K+ followers (@assortedtapes). TikTok: 262K+ followers (@assortedtapes). Former Polaroid Radio show host (Iris Station). Maintains curated playlists and artist submission portal at assortedtapes.com. Active Bandcamp collector.

nodes.name: Christian Archer (chrstianarcher)
nodes.bio: Brooklyn-based music curator, writer, and host of Gilded Minutes — a music talk show and Substack where musicians come to his home to discuss their upcoming work and play their favorite records on his $15,000 HiFi system. A self-described personal music curator and artist advocate who listens to 10–30 new albums a week, from Grammy nominees to underground obscurities. Previously published under the name Attack & Flux (launched October 2022), rebranded to Gilded Minutes. Known on Instagram (50K+ followers) and TikTok for deep-listening music content and artist advocacy. Also hosts The Archer Podcast on Spotify.
nodes.cv: Founder/Host, Gilded Minutes (formerly Attack & Flux, 2022–present). Substack newsletter and YouTube show. Host, The Archer Podcast (Spotify). Instagram curator (@chrstianarcher, 50K+ followers). Background in hip-hop as a recording artist; works in tech as a product designer.

nodes.name: DOST (Rajid Selim)
nodes.bio: Media platform and podcast founded by Bangladeshi-American director and content strategist Rajid Selim, dedicated to amplifying the most exciting musicians from the South Asian diaspora. DOST (meaning 'friend' in several South Asian languages) features artist interviews, curation, and live experiences spotlighting desi artists across genres. Has generated over 200 million views for South Asian musicians. Active on Instagram (73K+ followers) and TikTok, and runs The DOST Podcast on Spotify. One of the most important platforms for desi representation in contemporary music media.
nodes.cv: Founder & Director, DOST (ongoing). Content strategist, director, DJ. The DOST Podcast (Spotify). 200M+ views generated for South Asian artists. Instagram: 73K+ followers (@dostmeansfriend). TikTok: @dostmusic.

nodes.name: Derrick Gee
nodes.bio: Sydney-based Chinese-Australian music personality, DJ, and content creator who self-describes as a 'professional music fan.' After a decade in TV production, Mixcloud brand partnerships, and a creative director role at 88rising, he pivoted to full-time content creation in 2022 and rapidly amassed 1.3 million+ followers across platforms. Known for his calm, knowledgeable, and humorous approach to music discovery spanning everything from Tokyo jazz to Budots.
nodes.cv: Host, Solid Air (YouTube/Patreon music show). Former Creative Director, 88rising. Former NTS Radio resident (2016–2020). Former Mixcloud brand partnerships lead. SiriusXM host. Creator in Residence, Elbphilharmonie (2024/25). Featured in New Yorker, SSENSE, Sydney Morning Herald. Live touring show: Derrick Gee's Radio Hour.

nodes.bio: 3D and multiformat visual artist and YouTuber from London. Creates immersive 3D visuals, motion graphics, and graphic identities for jungle and electronic music artists using open source software Blender. Grew up on salvaged digital parts and stop motion animation. Known for accessible tutorials and a Y2K-inflected aesthetic.

nodes.name: Jokermen (Ian Grant & Evan Laffer)
nodes.bio: Jokermen is a music podcast and Substack by Ian Grant and Evan Laffer, launched in June 2020 with an initial deep dive into the discography of Bob Dylan. The show has since expanded to cover Van Morrison, Steely Dan, Warren Zevon, David Berman, Mark E. Smith, and others, completing multi-year series on Lou Reed/John Cale/the Velvet Underground (2022–2024) and currently covering Brian Wilson and the Beach Boys. In 2023, Jokermen also spun off Never Ending Stories, a separate podcast about Dylan's Never Ending Tour, co-hosted with critic Steven Hyden. The show is beloved for its erudite yet irreverent tone, obsessive track-level analysis, and 'Jokermen Mindset' ethos.
nodes.cv: Hosts: Ian Grant & Evan Laffer. Podcast launched June 2020. 350+ episodes. Series: Bob Dylan (2020–2022), Lou Reed/John Cale/VU (2022–2024), Brian Wilson/Beach Boys (2024–present). Spin-off: Never Ending Stories (with Steven Hyden, 2023–). Substack newsletter (thousands of subscribers). Notable guests include Greil Marcus, Tim Heidecker, Laurie Anderson, Benny Safdie, Michael Imperioli, Tom Scharpling, Ezra Koenig, Josh Tillman (Father John Misty), Ira Kaplan (Yo La Tengo), Natalie Mering (Weyes Blood). Live shows and merchandise.

notableWork.label: Jokermen Substack

nodes.name: Lovie (lovie.world)
nodes.bio: Brooklyn-based DJ, curator, and radio host (real name Lindsay Zae Summers). After relocating from Washington D.C. to New York in 2020, Lovie quickly became one of the city's most beloved selectors, known for slow-groove disco, dusty '70s funk, and sumptuous soulful house music spun with deep patience and musicality. Named one of Mixmag's Top 25 Breakthrough DJs of 2024. Holds a weekly residency at the Lot Radio (Summer School Radio) and a residency at Brooklyn club Public Records. Co-founded a party series focused on Black femme lineups bridging R&B and soulful house.
nodes.cv: Weekly resident, The Lot Radio (Summer School Radio, 4+ years). Resident DJ, Public Records (Brooklyn). Mixmag Top 25 Breakthrough DJs of 2024. We Out Here Festival. Insomniac artist. Mixmag Blackout Electronic Music Roundtable participant (with Kevin Saunderson, DJ Paulette). Co-founder, Black femme-forward party series.

Originally from New Orleans, she has built a massive following (150,000+ on Instagram) through her encyclopedic musical knowledge spanning indie, dream pop, experimental, and global sounds. Caroline Polachek has called her 'the future of music journalism.'

nodes.name: RamonPang (Ramon Tumbucon)
nodes.bio: LA-based Filipino-American DJ, producer, label head, curator, A&R, visual designer, and writer. Co-head of Tabula Rasa Records, one of LA and SF's premier underground electronic labels (roster includes Machinedrum, EPROM, Matmos). Known for Instagram and TikTok videos (150K–2M views) that educate audiences on electronic music subgenres — vaporwave, jersey club, plunderphonics, UKG, and beyond. Won beat battles judged by Baauer, A-Trak, and Mura Masa in 2021. Has received an RA Podcast (RA.1034). Featured in Rolling Stone Philippines for his work connecting Filipino identity and experimental music.
nodes.cv: Co-head, Tabula Rasa Records. Producer, DJ, curator, A&R, visual designer, writer. RA Podcast RA.1034. Won Baauer Twitch beat battles (judged by A-Trak, Mura Masa, 2021). Featured: Rolling Stone Philippines, FUXWITHIT, Resident Advisor. Instagram: 61K+ followers.

nodes.name: Saint Virgil (saintvirgil.mp3)
nodes.bio: NYC-based music writer, collector, researcher, archivist, and DJ. Publishes Black Noise, a Substack newsletter described as 'a curated journey through the intersections of music, memory, and the timeless allure of physical media.' Has maintained a Notion listening log since 2016 cataloguing every piece of music ever heard. Known on Instagram (50K+ followers) for eclectic, deep-cut curation spanning continents, decades, and genres. Also a DJ with sets at The Lot Radio covering 3-step, afro house, and amapiano. Black Noise has grown to 4,300+ subscribers.
nodes.cv: Author, Black Noise (Substack, ongoing — 4,300+ subscribers). DJ, The Lot Radio (NYC). Instagram curator (@saintvirgil.mp3, 50K+ followers). Physical media collector and music archivist.

nodes.name: SoundFynd

location.raw: Washington, D.C. / New York, NY (team distributed across US)

nodes.bio: Independent, self-funded music media collective founded by Tal Kamara, a writer from Teaneck, NJ. SoundFynd operates as a blog, interview platform, and social media presence built around community and music discovery, with an editorial ethos inspired by The Village Voice, The Fader, Pitchfork, and college radio/zine culture. The 10-person team is concentrated in Washington D.C. and New York City, with a stated mission to uplift LGBTQ+, BIPOC, and working-class artists and shine a light on music industry labor issues.
nodes.cv: Founder & Editor-in-Chief: Tal Kamara (George Washington University). Editor-in-Chief of Blog: Ike Nabuife (Ohio State, Columbus). Staff includes graduates of Howard University and American University. Produces blog interviews, TikTok content, YouTube videos, and playlist curation. Accepts artist submissions. No corporate ownership or backing.

nodes.name: Tia Ho
nodes.bio: Brooklyn-based music content creator, critic, and self-described 'critical glazer.' Spent six years at KEXP in Seattle as its first-ever full-time social media hire, building the station's social channels from scratch before going independent. Since leaving KEXP, she has worked with Beggars Group, XL Recordings, Ninja Tune, and Atlantic Records, and has covered festivals including Coachella and Icelandic Airwaves. Known for her on-camera editorial presence — album reviews, personal essays, and interviews — and has been featured by TIME as a journalist and content creator. Also contributes to Pigeons & Planes.
nodes.cv: Social Media Lead, KEXP, Seattle (six years). Contributor, Pigeons & Planes. Brand/media partnerships: Coachella, Icelandic Airwaves, Beggars Group, XL Recordings, Ninja Tune, Atlantic Records. Represented by Muk Posh (from 2025). Featured in TIME magazine and The Line of Best Fit.

nodes.name: billdifferen
nodes.bio: South Louisiana-based music writer and self-described 'only Cajun funkiero on Earth,' best known for an obsessive, encyclopedic knowledge of Brazilian baile funk and its regional variants. Operates the blog 'i'm just billdifferen' (billdifferen.blogspot.com), publishing monthly favorite music lists and deep-dive scene writing with an irreverent, personality-driven voice. Has described baile funk as his 'morning coffee and nightly melatonin gummy' and approaches the genre with a scholar's depth and a fan's genuine delight. Also covers dariacore, global club music, and other niche internet sounds. Despite cult online status, has self-effacingly noted 'this music writing thing ain't for me' — yet keeps writing.
nodes.cv: Author, 'i'm just billdifferen' blog (billdifferen.blogspot.com, ongoing). Contributor, Finals music blog (finals.blog) — wrote influential baile funk roundups and scene explainers including 'WHAT IN THE HONEST FUNK? My Eye-Opening Experience with Baile Funk in 2021' and the monthly 'da billdifferen funk roundup' series. Guest on Ghost Notes Worldwide (NTS Radio, Funk Special, August 2023). Active music tastemaker on Twitter/X (@billdifferen). SoundCloud playlist curator. Self-funded, independent operation.

nodes.name: BIG: Culture & Arts Festival
nodes.bio: Grassroots multi-genre arts and music festival held annually in downtown Gainesville, Florida. Brings together live music, multimedia art, skate, fashion, cars, food, circus performance, and film across eight stages. Presented by Dion Dia Records and How Bazar. Now in its fourth year (2026), with headliners including Earl Sweatshirt, The Alchemist, and Zack Fox.
nodes.cv: Founded by Jahi Khalfani, Laila Fakhoury, and Khary Khalfani — co-founders of Dion Dia Records and How Bazar, Gainesville's independent record label and cultural hub. Evolved from The BIG SHO' (2023) into a full multi-day festival. 2026 edition drew 5,000+ participants across 140+ artists.

nodes.name: Dripping Festival
nodes.bio: Annual music and arts festival held at Camp Sacajawea in Sparta, New Jersey, exploring the connections between sound system-driven club music and the experimental avant-garde. Set across 200 acres of forest, wooded paths, and a private lake. Features four stages (Inn, Barn, Lakeside, Open Air), a Funktion-One sound system, workshops, art installations, and comedy. Debut 2023.
nodes.cv: Founded by Leo Miller and Daniel Martin-McCormick. Known for its gentle pace, community spirit, and adventurous programming mixing experimental electronic, club, and avant-garde. 2025 lineup included Juliana Huxtable, keiyA, and gyrofield. Visual production by Studio GumGum and Waldo Walle. Featured in Resident Advisor.

nodes.name: Pitti Uomo
nodes.bio: The world's most important menswear trade fair, held biannually (January and June) at Fortezza da Basso in Florence. For four days each edition, Florence becomes the global capital of men's fashion, drawing industry professionals, buyers, press, and street style photographers from around the world. Known as much for its atmosphere and cultural programming as its showrooms.
nodes.cv: Origins trace to Giovanni Battista Giorgini's First Italian High Fashion Show (1952). Officially dedicated to menswear from 1972; moved to Fortezza da Basso in 1982. Organized by Pitti Immagine. Biannual editions each draw hundreds of brands and tens of thousands of visitors. A key fixture on the global fashion calendar for over 50 years.

nodes.name: Unsound Festival
nodes.bio: Annual experimental music and arts festival based in Kraków, Poland, dealing with evolving and mutating forms of music and related visual arts. Founded 2003, each edition is built around a central theme. Also stages editions in New York, London, Adelaide, Toronto, Osaka, and other cities. One of the most respected experimental music platforms in the world.
nodes.cv: Founded 2003 by Mat Schulz (Australian expat) and Stephen Berkley. Run by the non-profit Fundacja Tone. 2021 hybrid edition won Poland's most prestigious cultural award, Paszport Polityki. Annual themes have included Horror (2010), Future Shock (2011), DADA (2023), and WEB (2025). 72K Instagram followers.

nodes.bio: MoMA PS1's Warm Up is one of the longest-running music programs housed within a museum, presenting experimental and avant-garde music every summer since 1998 in the outdoor courtyard. nodes.name: Alphonse Pierre
nodes.bio: New York-based senior writer at Pitchfork and one of the most respected voices in contemporary hip-hop criticism. Born and raised in New York, he joined Pitchfork in 2018 and was promoted to Senior Writer in 2023. Known for his Off the Dome column, which blends criticism, cultural commentary, and street-level rap journalism. Winner of the 2025 ASME Next Award.
nodes.cv: Senior Writer, Pitchfork (2018–present). Author of the 'Off the Dome' column. Previous contributor: Noisey, VICE, Passion of the Weiss. 2025 ASME Next Award honoree (American Society of Magazine Editors). nodes.name: Amanda Petrusich
nodes.bio: One of the most acclaimed music critics and cultural journalists working today. A staff writer and pop music critic at The New Yorker since joining Pitchfork in 2003, Petrusich writes with uncommon literary grace about the full sweep of popular music — from 78rpm blues collectors to Phish, Neil Peart, Patti Smith, and D'Angelo. She grew up in the Hudson Valley, studied at the College of William & Mary (B.A., English and Film Studies, 2000) and Columbia University (M.F.A., nonfiction, 2003), and has become one of the most essential voices on the intersection of music, obsession, memory, and American identity. Named a 'Hero of 2024' by Mother Jones.
nodes.cv: Staff Writer & Pop Music Critic, The New Yorker (current). Contributing editor, Oxford American. Associate Professor / Writer-in-Residence, NYU Gallatin School of Individualized Study (since 2010). Former staff writer, Pitchfork (from 2003). Commissioning editor, Bloomsbury 33 1/3 series. Author: Pink Moon (33 1/3, 2007); It Still Moves: Lost Songs, Lost Highways, and the Search for the Next American Music (Faber, 2008); Do Not Sell at Any Price: The Wild, Obsessive Hunt for the World's Rarest 78rpm Records (Scribner, 2014). 2016 Guggenheim Fellowship (nonfiction). 2019 Grammy nomination (Best Album Notes, Bob Dylan's Trouble No More). 2016 Staige D. Blackford Prize for Nonfiction. 2016 Brooklyn Magazine '100 Most Influential People in Brooklyn Culture.' 2014 NY State Foundation for the Arts fellow. 2015 MacDowell Colony fellow.

notableWork.label: New Yorker contributor page

nodes.name: Cassidy George
nodes.bio: Berlin-based culture writer and Managing Editor at 032c. Writes about music, fashion, and culture for The New York Times, The New Yorker, Vogue, GQ, Rolling Stone, W, The Cut, Dazed, and the Wall Street Journal. Began her career at SLEEK magazine before joining 032c's editorial team in 2021. Known for major longform cover features including a 40-page Nine Inch Nails dossier and a 32-page Drain Gang deep dive. In 2023, launched the YouTube channel Hausverbot, featuring candid conversations with Berlin's best DJs. Has interviewed Travis Scott, Dua Lipa, Doechii, Drain Gang, Tyla, Tems, Yeat, and more.
nodes.cv: Managing Editor, 032c (2021–present). Founder/Host, Hausverbot YouTube channel (2023–present). Former writer, SLEEK magazine. Contributor: New York Times, The New Yorker, Vogue, GQ, Rolling Stone, W, The Cut, Dazed, Wall Street Journal, Resident Advisor.

nodes.name: Jeff Weiss
nodes.bio: LA-based music journalist, critic, editor, label founder, and DJ widely regarded as one of the finest music writers working today. Best known for his authoritative coverage of West Coast underground rap, longform cultural criticism, and tireless advocacy for artists like Drakeo the Ruler. Founded the pioneering hip-hop blog Passion of the Weiss in 2005. His first novel, Waiting for Britney Spears, was published in 2025.
nodes.cv: Founder/Editor-in-Chief, Passion of the Weiss (2005–present). Founder, POW Recordings. Co-founder, The LAnd magazine. Former columnist, LA Weekly. Contributor to Washington Post, LA Times, Pitchfork, Rolling Stone, The FADER, Esquire, Variety, Billboard, NME, Hollywood Reporter, Tablet, The Face, and more. Co-host, Shots Fired podcast. Included in 2009 Best Music Writing Anthology. Published novelist (2025).

nodes.name: Joshua Minsoo Kim
nodes.bio: Chicago-based culture writer, film programmer, and high school science teacher whose criticism spans experimental music, avant-garde film, and global pop. Founded Tone Glow in 2015 — an independent publication covering experimental music through longform Q&A interviews — which moved to Substack in 2019 and now hosts tens of thousands of subscribers. Known for meticulous, empathetic interview work that treats artists as full human beings first. Began writing publicly in 2011 on Tumblr. Currently writing a book about music, sound, and silence in avant-garde film for Repeater Books. Publicly called out Rolling Stone in 2025 for editorial pressure to soften a negative review, drawing wide attention to critical integrity issues.
nodes.cv: Founder & Editor-in-Chief, Tone Glow (2015–present). High school science teacher (Chicago). Film programmer (hosts in-person screenings of independent/avant-garde film on 16mm, 8mm, super 8mm). Contributor: Pitchfork, The Wire, Rolling Stone, NPR, BOMB Magazine, Stereogum, Billboard, Bandcamp Daily, Resident Advisor, Chicago Reader, The Quietus, Cinema Scope, MUBI Notebook, Metrograph, Slant Magazine, Musicworks. Writer & editor, The Singles Jukebox. Forthcoming book: music, sound, and silence in avant-garde film (Repeater Books).

nodes.name: Matthew Belloni (The Town / Puck)
nodes.bio: LA-based entertainment journalist, podcast host, and founding partner at Puck. The definitive Hollywood insider voice — his twice-weekly Puck newsletter 'What I'm Hearing' and podcast The Town (on The Ringer) cover the business of entertainment: streaming wars, dealmaking, studio politics, and the forces shaping what gets made. Spent 14 years at The Hollywood Reporter, including as editor-in-chief, and five years before that as an entertainment lawyer. Covers the music industry as it intersects with Hollywood — streaming economics, label deals, artist rights, and the broader entertainment business.

nodes.name: Somewhere Soul (Josh Mason-Quinn)
nodes.bio: London-based music curator, DJ, A&R, and event programmer behind Somewhere Soul — one of the UK's most followed underground music discovery platforms. Founded by Josh Mason-Quinn after university, Somewhere Soul has grown from a passion project into a tastemaking powerhouse with 1M+ followers across Instagram and TikTok, known for Josh's signature head-nod videos championing jazz, soul, funk, and electronic music from around the world. In early 2024 his videos went viral, reaching regular six- and seven-figure views. Also runs live events ('Music to Expand Your Horizons'), sold-out shows at Jazz Cafe and 91 Living Room, and released Rituals Volume 01 — a double-vinyl compilation on Warm Agency featuring Allysha Joy, Blue Lab Beats, Chip Wickham, and others.

nodes.name: Trapital (Dan Runcie)
nodes.bio: Independent media company and newsletter covering the business of hip-hop, founded in 2018 by Dan Runcie. Trapital bridges music industry strategy and culture — examining deals, streaming economics, label structures, artist ownership, and the business moves shaping rap and R&B. nodes.bio: Independent online radio platform founded in 2011 in Hackney, East London by Femi Adeyemi. Streams 24/7, prioritizing niche and underground artists across every genre. Inspired by pirate radio, MTV2, WFMU, and the community around Plastic People nightclub. Now a global platform with studios in London, LA, Manchester, and Shanghai.
nodes.cv: Founded 2011 by Femi Adeyemi with £5,000. Incorporated as NTS Live Ltd in 2013. Hosts include globally recognized DJs, producers, and artists. One of the most influential independent music streaming platforms in the world.

nodes.name: Rinse FM
nodes.bio: London-based community radio station founded in 1994 as a pirate station. Pivotal in launching and promoting grime and dubstep to global audiences. Awarded a broadcast licence in 2010, now operating on FM 106.8 MHz and DAB in London. Programming covers garage, grime, dubstep, house, jungle, UK funky, and dance music.
nodes.cv: Founded 1994 by Geeneus (Gordon Warren) as a pirate station. Licensed June 2010. Celebrated 30th anniversary in 2024 with Dazed oral history feature. 234K Instagram followers.

nodes.name: Song Exploder
nodes.bio: Music podcast created and hosted by Hrishikesh Hirway in which musicians break down a single song piece by piece, explaining every production and lyrical decision. Debuted January 2014 and adapted into a Netflix original series in 2020. Part of the Radiotopia network.
nodes.cv: Founded 2014 by Hrishikesh Hirway (b. Peabody, MA; Yale grad). Podcast of the Year — Quartz (2015); Best of iTunes (2015); Best Music Podcast — Academy of Podcasters (2017, 2018). Netflix series (2020). Guests include Billie Eilish, Solange, Metallica, Bon Iver, Fleetwood Mac, Lorde, Yo-Yo Ma, and hundreds more.

nodes.name: The Lot Radio
nodes.bio: Online radio station streaming 24/7 from a repurposed shipping container on an empty lot in Greenpoint, Brooklyn. Founded 2015 by François Vaxelaire, focusing on electronic music, disco, and soul. One of NYC's most beloved independent music institutions.
nodes.cv: Founded 2015 by François Vaxelaire and Pauline Le Mell. Programme directors Chris Cherry and Lloyd Harris. Winner Best Online Radio Station, North America (2016). 298K Instagram followers. Celebrated 10th anniversary in 2025.

nodes.name: 032c
nodes.bio: Biannual contemporary culture magazine and fashion brand founded in Berlin in 2000 by Joerg Koch. Covers art, fashion, and politics in English. Named after a Pantone color reference. Has evolved from a magazine into a full fashion brand with global ready-to-wear collections.
nodes.cv: Founded 2000 by Joerg Koch (journalist) and Maria Koch (womenswear designer; former Jil Sander, Yeezy consultant). Biannual English-language publication. Expanded into fashion brand with retail and community events globally. Columnist Brenda Weischer (brendahashtag) writes the 'Brenda's Business' column.

nodes.name: Apartamento Magazine
nodes.bio: Biannual interiors magazine founded in Barcelona in 2008 by Nacho Alegre, Omar Sosa, and Marco Velardi. A reaction against sterile, impersonal interior design publications — Apartamento showcases lived-in homes filled with character and personal expression. First issue of 5,000 copies sold out immediately.
nodes.cv: Founded 2008 by Nacho Alegre, Omar Sosa, and Marco Velardi in Barcelona; now operates from Berlin via Apartamento Studios creative consultancy. Biannual. Celebrated 10-year anniversary with major monograph.

nodes.name: Aquarium Drunkard
nodes.bio: One of the most enduring and beloved independent music publications on the internet, founded in 2005 by Justin Gage — named after a lyric from Wilco's Yankee Hotel Foxtrot. What began as a way to share music with friends grew within a year into a globally-read music journal. AD covers an extraordinarily eclectic range of sounds: psych, jazz, avant-garde, folk, soul, Afrobeat, krautrock, gospel, world music, and beyond — anything with something 'oblique and bent' about it. Nick Hornby named it one of his six favorite music blogs in 2009. The publication has since evolved into what Gage calls an 'independent media conglomerate.'
nodes.cv: Founded 2005 by Justin Gage (Atlanta/LA). Grew to include 7+ regular writers and guest contributors. Launched Autumn Tone Records. Weekly show on SIRIUS/XMU. Co-founded Gold Diggers (recording studio/bar/hotel, Hollywood). Launched 'Lagniappe Sessions' (2011) — vinyl released with Light In The Attic (2016). 'Radio Free' pirate radio stream (2020). Aquarium Drunkard Picture Show aired on Adult Swim simulcast (2020). 2009 LA Weekly Best Music Blog award. Helped break Alabama Shakes via a demo post.

nodes.name: ArtReview
nodes.bio: International contemporary art magazine founded in 1949, one of the world's leading publications dedicated to expanding contemporary art's audience. Features criticism, reviews, commentary, analysis, and commissioned artist projects. Also publishes ArtReview Asia (launched 2013) and the annual Power 100 list.
nodes.cv: Founded 1949. One of the oldest continuous contemporary art magazines. 274K Instagram followers. Website artreview.com features news, criticism, video, and podcasts alongside print issues.

nodes.name: Artforum
nodes.bio: International monthly contemporary art magazine founded in 1962 by John P. Irwin Jr. Distinguished by its square 10.5" × 10.5" format and artist-devoted covers. Moved from LA to NY in 1967. The definitive publication of the contemporary art world for over 60 years. Now owned by Penske Media Corporation.
nodes.cv: Founded 1962 by John P. Irwin Jr. Moved to NY 1967. Co-editors: Rachel Wetzler and Daniel Wenger (2026–). 1M Instagram followers. Owned by Artforum Media LLC (subsidiary of Penske Media Corporation).

nodes.name: Crack Magazine
nodes.bio: Independent monthly music and culture magazine, the biggest independent free monthly magazine in Europe. Founded 2009 in Bristol by Jake Applebee and Thomas Frost. Distributed across Europe. Covers music, art, nightlife, and culture with a focus on underground and emerging scenes.
nodes.cv: Founded 2009 by Jake Applebee and Thomas Frost. First independent music magazine interview with Flying Lotus (2012). Issue 100 with Thom Yorke (May 2019). Launched creative agency CC Co (Crack Creative Company) in 2021, led by head of content Duncan Harrison.

nodes.name: Dazed
nodes.bio: Independent British fashion, culture, and arts magazine founded in 1991 by Jefferson Hack and Rankin while studying at London College of Printing. Covers music, fashion, film, art, and literature. Published as Dazed & Confused until 2014. Part of Dazed Media alongside AnOther, Another Man, Nowness, and Dazed Studio.
nodes.cv: Founded 1991 by Jefferson Hack and Rankin. Renamed from Dazed & Confused to Dazed (Feb 2014). 2M Instagram followers. Parent company Dazed Media. Launched Dazed Beauty (2020).

nodes.bio: Independent biannual culture, arts, and fashion magazine founded in New York in 2012. Published in book format and distributed globally, featuring prominent voices from art, literature, and fashion. Editor-in-chief and creative director: Nick Vogelson.
nodes.cv: Founded 2012. Biannual (spring/fall). 213K+ Instagram followers. Known for art-directed photography and long-form cultural writing.

nodes.bio: Quarterly art and visual culture magazine founded in London in 2009 by Marc Valli (co-founder of art book shop Magma). Takes a non-hierarchical, artist-led approach to contemporary art coverage, spotting communities and personalities from cities across all continents. Now part of the Colart Group.
nodes.cv: Founded 2009 by Marc Valli. Quarterly (March, June, September, December). Acquired by Colart (2017). 128K Instagram followers. Website elephant.art publishes ongoing criticism and features.

nodes.cv: Founded 2005 by Gert Jonkers (editor-in-chief) and Jop van Bennekom (creative director). D&AD Best Magazine & Newspaper Design (2008). Launched website with daily content (2009). Sister publication The Gentlewoman (2010).

nodes.name: Frieze
nodes.bio: International contemporary art magazine and art fair founded in London in 1991 by Amanda Sharp, Matthew Slotover, and Tom Gidley. Published eight times a year. Publishes essays, profiles, interviews, and reviews by leading writers, artists, and curators. Expanded to global art fairs in London, NY, LA, Seoul, and beyond.
nodes.cv: Founded 1991 by Amanda Sharp, Matthew Slotover, and Tom Gidley. Sharp and Slotover appointed OBE (2012) for services to the visual arts. 1M Instagram followers. Frieze Art Fairs now staged globally.

nodes.bio: International quarterly magazine of contemporary art and culture founded in Milan in 2009 by Alessio Ascari. Distributed worldwide on a seasonal basis. Known for its interdisciplinary and unconventional approach, Kaleidoscope covers visual art, fashion, film, music, and performance.
nodes.cv: Founded 2009 by Alessio Ascari (founder and creative director). Art direction by London studio OK-RM (from Winter 2011/12). Quarterly. Self-described: 'THE AVANT-GARDE, ETC.'

nodes.name: Maggot Brain Magazine
nodes.bio: Quarterly print music magazine published by Third Man Records, launched in 2019. Named after the classic 1971 Funkadelic album. Edited by Mike McGonigal, featuring an A-list cast of writers covering rock, punk, jazz, and roots music alongside literary and cultural writing. Print-only and analog-first.
nodes.cv: Launched 2019 by Third Man Records (Jack White). Editor Mike McGonigal (formerly of zines Chemical Imbalance 1984–1994 and Yeti 2000–2013). Quarterly. Now past issue #20.

nodes.name: Marfa Journal
nodes.bio: Biannual arts and culture magazine founded by Alexandra Gordienko, launched May 2013 as a Central Saint Martins graduate project. 'By artists for artists' philosophy. Features cultural figures including Chloé Sevigny, Lindsay Lohan, and Lynda Benglis.
nodes.cv: Founded by Alexandra Gordienko (Yekaterinburg, Russia). MA graduate project at Central Saint Martins, launched May 2013. Expanded to MARfamily spinoff, art books, and MARFOFFICE creative bureau.

nodes.name: Metal Magazine
nodes.bio: Independent biannual fashion, photography, and art magazine born in Barcelona in June 2006. Available in more than 25 countries. A heady mix of fashion, photography, and art with a curious eye and international spirit. Also publishes exclusive online content.
nodes.cv: Founded June 2006 in Barcelona. Editors-in-chief and creative directors: Yolanda Muelas and Poncho Paradela. Biannual print + online. 238K Instagram followers. Distributed in 25+ countries.

nodes.name: Pigeons & Planes
nodes.bio: One of the most influential music discovery publications of the blog era, founded in 2008 by Jacob Moore. P&P became known for breaking artists early — including Lorde's first US interview, early coverage of Chance the Rapper (who shouted them out on Acid Rap), and first interviews with Smino, Clairo, and Young Thug. Acquired by Complex in 2011, then BuzzFeed/Complex Networks in 2021, and NTWRK in 2024. The standalone website went dark for over six years before relaunching in early 2026 with original editorial, artist profiles, and music discovery coverage.
nodes.cv: Founded 2008 by Jacob Moore (@CONFUSIONisDEAD). Acquired by Complex (2011), BuzzFeed/Complex Networks (2021), NTWRK (2024). Website relaunched January 2026. Founder/GM: Jacob Moore. Contributing Editor: Joe Price. Known for breaking Lorde (first US interview), Chance the Rapper, Smino, Clairo, Young Thug.

nodes.name: Purple Magazine
nodes.bio: Biannual art and fashion magazine founded in Paris in 1992 by Olivier Zahm and Elein Fleiss as a fanzine-style reaction against 1980s superficial glamour. After Fleiss departed in 2002, Zahm shifted the focus toward fashion. Bridging art and fashion for over 34 years as an independent publisher.
nodes.cv: Founded 1992 by Olivier Zahm and Elein Fleiss. Fleiss departed 2002; Zahm continues as editor-in-chief, art director, and photographer. 366K Instagram followers. Zahm also active as curator and art critic.

nodes.name: Spike Art Magazine
nodes.bio: Independent quarterly contemporary art magazine founded in Vienna in 2004 by artist Rita Vitorelli. Situated between art theory and practice, mixing rigorous criticism with unconventional ideas on contemporary art. Expanded to Berlin in 2014 with a second editorial office and event space.
nodes.cv: Founded 2004 by Rita Vitorelli (Vienna). Second office and non-commercial art space in Berlin (2014). Quarterly print + weekly online content. Contributors include Dean Kissick, Hans-Ulrich Obrist, Karen Archey. 77K+ Instagram followers. One of the last remaining independent art magazines.

nodes.name: System Magazine
nodes.bio: Biannual Anglo-French fashion publication founded spring 2013. Documents the people, dialogues, and culture at the heart of the fashion industry through exclusive long-format conversations and image portfolios created by leading image-makers.
nodes.cv: Founded 2013 by Elizabeth von Guttman, Thomas Lenthal, and Jonathan Wingfield. Sold to Mike Obenson (2020); reacquired by original founders (Dec 2024). Launched System Beauty (2022) and System Collections (May 2025). Notable covers: Pharrell Williams (Issue 22), Lotta Volkova (Issue 23).

Published in both German and English (since 2006). Rigorously theoretical, combining criticism with sociological analysis of the art world.

Also operates e-flux architecture, announcements, directory, and education.

nodes.name: i-D Magazine
nodes.bio: British fashion, music, art, and youth culture magazine founded in 1980 by former Vogue art director Terry Jones as a punk-era street style fanzine. One of the first magazines to cover street fashion. Iconic for its winking cover pose. Acquired by Bedford Media in November 2023.
nodes.cv: Founded 1980 by Terry Jones. Sold to Vice Media (2012); acquired by Bedford Media (Nov 2023). 2M+ Instagram followers. Key platform for emerging talent across fashion, music, and culture for over 40 years.

nodes.name: Arcana: Books on the Arts
nodes.bio: Specialist bookstore in Culver City devoted to new, out-of-print, and rare books on photography, art, fashion, design, architecture, film, and music. Operating since 1984 with over 100,000 items in a 5,000 sq ft space. Open Wednesday–Sunday, 11am–7pm.
nodes.cv: Founded 1984. Located in Culver City, CA. 5,000 sq ft. 100,000+ items. Specializes in visual art, architecture, design, photography, film, fashion, and music publications.

nodes.name: C'H'C'M'
nodes.bio: Multi-brand menswear boutique established online in 2008, opened physical space in 2010 at 2 Bond Street, NoHo NYC. Launched in-house label in 2014, manufactured in the UK, France, Italy, Japan, and USA. Specializes in curated projects with designers and manufacturers.
nodes.cv: Founded 2008 (online). Physical store opened 2010 at 2 Bond Street, NoHo NYC. In-house line launched 2014. Manufacturing in UK, France, Italy, Japan, USA.

nodes.name: Dashwood Books
nodes.bio: New York's only independent bookstore devoted entirely to photography books, founded in 2005 by David Strettell (formerly Cultural Director of Magnum Photos). Specializes in rare, imported, limited-edition, and self-published photobooks from international publishers.
nodes.cv: Founded 2005 by David Strettell (ex-Magnum Photos Cultural Director). Located at 33 Bond St, NoHo NYC. Imports from Japan, Germany, Netherlands. Publishes limited-edition monographs.

nodes.name: Dover Street Market
nodes.bio: Multi-brand luxury concept retail space conceived by Rei Kawakubo and Adrian Joffe of Comme des Garçons. Known for its 'beautiful chaos' philosophy merging fashion, art, and culture. Flagship opened London 2004; locations in New York, Tokyo, Singapore, Paris, Beijing, and Los Angeles.
nodes.cv: Founded by Rei Kawakubo and Adrian Joffe (Comme des Garçons), 2004. Locations: London, New York, Tokyo, Singapore, Paris, Beijing, Los Angeles.

nodes.name: The Broken Arm
nodes.bio: High-fashion concept store, café, and gift shop in Le Marais, Paris, founded in 2013. Offers a curated selection of international designers alongside the Dreamin Man Café. A key destination for fashion-forward retail in Paris.
nodes.cv: Opened 2013 at 12 Rue Perrée, 3rd arrondissement, Le Marais. Includes boutique, The Broken Arm Gift Shop, and Dreamin Man Café.

nodes.name: printings
nodes.bio: Japanese art book publisher and online shop specializing in archive books, photography publications, and art goods. Ships internationally. Known for limited-edition archive books and collaborations with photographers and artists.
nodes.cv: Japan-based art book publisher and shop. Publishes 'Archive Books' and curates 'Book Archive' items. Ships worldwide.

nodes.name: 180 Studios
nodes.bio: London's leading creative cultural centre at 180 The Strand, a landmark Brutalist building transformed into a hub for exhibitions, production studios, dining, and a community of artists and innovators. Home to 45+ companies across fashion, art, technology, design, music, journalism, and food.
nodes.cv: Located at 180 The Strand, WC2R 1EA, London. Brutalist landmark converted to creative hub. 45+ resident companies. World-class exhibitions and production studios.

nodes.name: Colbo
nodes.bio: Multi-disciplinary store, in-house fashion label, and cultural space on the Lower East Side of Manhattan. Offers original garments, independent brands, vintage clothing, home goods, vinyl records, a café, and an event/exhibition space. Open daily 11am–7pm.
nodes.cv: Based on the Lower East Side, NYC. Multi-brand store with in-house label. Hosts events, exhibitions, and a café.

nodes.name: Devon Ojas (Devon Turnbull)
nodes.bio: Brooklyn-based artist and audio engineer operating as OJAS. Handcrafts high-fidelity audio systems — monolithic functional sculptures that express the emotional essence of music. Installations at SFMOMA, Cooper Hewitt, Lisson Gallery, Public Records, Nine Orchard Hotel, and Supreme stores worldwide. Background in graffiti, DJing, and graphic design.
nodes.cv: Born 1979, Smithtown NY. Audio Engineering background. Cofounded menswear label Nom de Guerre (2003). OJAS systems exhibited at SFMOMA, Cooper Hewitt, and Lisson Gallery. Built listening rooms for Public Records Brooklyn, Nine Orchard Hotel, and Patina Osaka.

nodes.name: In Sheep's Clothing HiFi
nodes.bio: Vinyl listening bar, record store, blog, and HiFi record club founded in LA's Arts District. Originally a Japanese whisky and vinyl listening bar; pivoted during COVID into an online platform and record club. Known for curating obscure deep cuts, overlooked reissues, and underappreciated records. Monthly HiFi Record Club sends unique selections to each subscriber — no two members receive the same records.
nodes.cv: Founded by brothers Aneesh and Amit. Listening bar in LA Arts District (pre-COVID). Record shop in Fairfax district. HiFi Record Club launched 2021. 68K+ Instagram followers. Key contributors include Randall Roberts (formerly LA Times).

nodes.name: Public Records
nodes.bio: Music venue, bar, and eatery in Gowanus, Brooklyn occupying 6,000 sqft of a 1910s former ASPCA building. Five spaces: The Atrium (restaurant/music lounge), Sound Room (sonically-immersive live/club venue), Cafe Public (vegan cafe), UPSTAIRS (listening lounge/cocktail bar), and The Nursery (open-air event space). Focus on electronic music.
nodes.cv: Founded by Shane Davis, Francis Harris, and Erik VanderWal. Sound Room features a Devon Turnbull OJAS sound system. Capacity ~200. Partnered with NYCEDC for new music and arts venue at Bush Terminal.

nodes.name: Stylus
nodes.bio: New private members' club and acoustic salon on the Lower East Side dedicated to sound-focused experiences — active listening sessions, curated talks, performances, wellness therapies, and dining. Bespoke listening rooms designed by O'Neill Rose Architects. Culinary Director: Michelin-starred Anita Lo.
nodes.cv: Opening 2026, Lower East Side NYC. Private members' club. Listening rooms with world-class acoustic technology (O'Neill Rose Architects). Culinary Director: Anita Lo. Backed by Mara Partners.

nodes.name: The Living Room
nodes.bio: Multifunctional creative space in downtown Los Angeles hosting open mics, workshops, lectures, pop-up markets, and intimate performances. The space shifts configurations to serve as desk, gallery, networking lounge, and workshop floor.
nodes.cv: Located in downtown Los Angeles. Multipurpose creative venue. Programming: open mics, workshops, lectures, pop-ups, and performances.

nodes.name: Pioneer Works
nodes.bio: Artist and scientist-led nonprofit cultural center in Red Hook, Brooklyn founded by Dustin Yellin in 2012. Fosters innovative thinking across visual art, music, technology, and science. Features three floors of studio, performance, exhibition, and multipurpose spaces — the majority of programming is free.
nodes.cv: Founded 2012 by Dustin Yellin. Located at 159 Pioneer St, Red Hook, Brooklyn. Dedicated recording studio with month-long artist residencies. Key series: Second Sundays (free monthly open house since 2013). Music spans electronic, experimental, doom metal, avant-garde jazz.

notableWork.label: Second Sundays (free monthly open house)
Music residency program (custom recording studio)

nodes.name: Julia Stoschek Foundation
nodes.bio: Non-profit foundation dedicated to the public presentation, conservation, and scholarship of time-based art. Holds one of the world's foremost collections of video, film, moving image installation, multimedia, performance, sound, and VR — spanning 1,000+ works by 300+ artists. Founded by Julia Stoschek.
nodes.cv: Founded by Julia Stoschek. Düsseldorf space opened 2007; Berlin-Mitte space (2,500 sqm, former Czech Cultural Centre) opened 2016. Collection roots in 1960s–70s moving image experiments. Artists span global contemporary practices.

notableWork.label: Time-based art collection (1,000+ works, 300+ artists)

nodes.name: Tokyo Record Bar
nodes.bio: Underground izakaya-meets-listening-room at 127 MacDougal St, Greenwich Village. Two-floor 'homage to the jewel boxes of vinyl in Tokyo.' Guests select songs from an extensive record collection; the house DJ curates an all-vinyl playlist paired with a 7-course omakase tasting menu. Strong soul, jazz, reggae, funk, and leftfield selections.
nodes.cv: 127 MacDougal St, Greenwich Village. Vinyl jukebox omakase downstairs; cocktail bar upstairs (beneath Air's Champagne Parlor). Seating at 6:30pm and 8:30pm; a la carte at 10:30pm. Closed Sun–Mon.

nodes.name: All Blues
nodes.bio: Japanese-style audiophile listening bar in Tribeca founded by Yuji Fukushima. Custom JBL studio monitors and vintage McIntosh gear. Divided into a bar area and a dedicated curtained listening theater. Quiet voices encouraged. Deep collection spanning Stevie Wonder, Miles Davis, Diana Ross, Marvin Gaye.
nodes.cv: Founded by Yuji Fukushima. Tribeca, Manhattan. Custom JBL studio monitors, vintage McIntosh gear. Two-zone layout: bar + curtained listening theater. Focus on soul, jazz, R&B classics.

nodes.name: Bar Orai
nodes.bio: Speakeasy-style vinyl and Japanese whiskey listening bar on the 2nd floor at 212 E 52nd St, Midtown East. 2,000 vinyl records across hip-hop, soul, R&B, and jazz. Three pairs of top-of-the-line loudspeakers with bass traps. Mid-century design with armchairs and couches. Asian-inspired tapas and whiskey flights.
nodes.cv: 212 E 52nd St, 2nd fl, Midtown East. 2,000 vinyl records. 3 speaker pairs + bass traps. Japanese whiskey focus; also Irish/Scotch, cocktails, beer. Custom Orai candles (Soul, Funk, Jazz scents). Tue–Sat 6pm–12am.

nodes.name: Eavesdrop
nodes.bio: Jazz kissa-inspired listening bar at 674 Manhattan Ave, Greenpoint, Brooklyn. State-of-the-art sound system and international vinyl collection. Craft cocktails, natural wines, and small plates. Monthly DJ programming spans jazz, soul, experimental, electronic, and contemporary underground music.
nodes.cv: 674 Manhattan Ave, Greenpoint, Brooklyn. Inspired by Tokyo jazz kissas. State-of-the-art sound system. International vinyl collection. Monthly DJ schedule posted on Instagram. 45K Instagram followers.

nodes.name: Honeycomb Hi-Fi Lounge
nodes.bio: Tokyo jazz kissa-inspired hi-fi listening bar at 74 5th Ave, Park Slope. Albums played front-to-back on weeknights; local DJs take over weekends. Acoustic honeycomb ceiling panels designed for optimal sound. Dark, cozy atmosphere with cocktails, wine, and spirits.
nodes.cv: 74 5th Ave, Park Slope, Brooklyn. Opened 2023. Honeycomb acoustic ceiling paneling. Weeknights: full albums front-to-back. Weekends: local DJs. Open Tue–Thu from 5pm, Fri–Sun from 4pm.

nodes.name: Suono
nodes.bio: Intimate basement listening room beneath Bar Camillo at 333 Tompkins Ave, Bed-Stuy. Custom vintage speakers built by owner Danilo Braca's father — founder of Italy's first audio magazine, also named Suono. All-vinyl DJ sets every Friday and Saturday. Tiny, no-frills, serious sound.
nodes.cv: 333 Tompkins Ave (below Bar Camillo), Bed-Stuy, Brooklyn. Owner: Danilo Braca (son of founder of Italy's first audio magazine, Suono). Custom father-built vintage speakers. Fri–Sat only, 9pm–1am. All-vinyl sets.

nodes.name: Kissa Kissa
nodes.bio: America's first traditional Japanese jazz kissa at 667 Franklin Ave, Crown Heights. 5,000 vintage jazz LPs from the 1950s–70s in a dedicated listening environment. East Asian-inspired craft cocktails featuring shochu, sake, and Japanese whiskey alongside conservas and small plates.
nodes.cv: 667 Franklin Ave, Crown Heights, Brooklyn. Self-described first jazz kissa outside Japan. 5,000 vinyl jazz records (1950s–70s). East Asian spirits program: shochu, sake, Japanese whiskey. Phone: (347) 507-9090.

nodes.name: Moondog HiFi
nodes.bio: Audiophile vinyl listening bar at 119 Ingraham St, East Williamsburg. High-fidelity sound system paired with elevated Mediterranean-Mexican cuisine and craft cocktails. Intimate space with walnut millwork, lush greenery, and marble bar. Weekly local and global DJs playing reggae, soul, funk, jazz, and electronic.
nodes.cv: 119 Ingraham St, East Williamsburg, Brooklyn. High-fidelity sound system. Menu: elevated Mediterranean-Mexican + craft cocktails. Weekly DJs: reggae, soul, funk, jazz, electronic. Open from 5pm. Email: info@moondoghifi.com.

nodes.name: Gold Line
nodes.bio: Vinyl listening bar in Highland Park, LA conceived by Chris Manak (Peanut Butter Wolf), founder of Stones Throw Records. Built around expert curation and high-fidelity sound. Leans toward whiskey, mezcal, and well-made cocktails. One of LA's most respected listening bars, beloved by the city's music community.
nodes.cv: 5607 N. Figueroa St, Highland Park, Los Angeles. Founded by Peanut Butter Wolf (Chris Manak, Stones Throw Records). Whiskey, mezcal, highballs. Real-time curated vinyl programming.

nodes.name: Wax On Hi-Fi
nodes.bio: Downtown LA hi-fi vinyl café, restaurant, and bar focused on jazz, soul, R&B, and blues. Eclectic fusion menu of Japanese and American comfort food alongside tea, coffee, beer, wine, and sake. One of the earlier anchors of LA's listening-bar scene. Now permanently closed.
nodes.cv: 212 W 5th St, DTLA. Instagram: @waxonhifi. Hi-fi playback. Jazz/soul/R&B/blues focus. Japanese-American fusion food and drinks. [Permanently Closed]

nodes.name: Vinyl Room at Hollywood Palladium
nodes.bio: Japanese kissa-inspired hi-fi listening lounge inside the historic Hollywood Palladium, opened 2026. Acoustically tuned interiors, curated Asian-inspired menu, and dedicated vinyl DJ programming. Accessible on show nights or via membership, catering to music lovers, creatives, and industry tastemakers.
nodes.cv: Inside Hollywood Palladium. Opened 2026. Membership-based access plus show-night entry. Acoustically designed. Asian-inspired menu. Vinyl DJ programming.

nodes.name: Let's Go! Disco
nodes.bio: Dance-oriented cocktail bar occupying the former In Sheep's Clothing space in the Arts District. Inherited the original room's hi-fi audio DNA while leaning toward a more dance-floor energy. 1970s-inspired Italian disco aesthetic with circular booths, neon lighting, and disco balls. Part of De La Nonna.
nodes.cv: 710 E 4th Place, Arts District, LA. Former In Sheep's Clothing location. Retains strong sound system. 70s Italian disco aesthetic. Part of De La Nonna / The Simon Collective.

nodes.name: Common Wave Hi-Fi
nodes.bio: DTLA's first home audio hi-fi showroom, social listening space, and record shop, combined with a turntable workshop and repair center. A community hub for LA's audiophile culture. Carries 200+ hi-fi systems and acts as the LA dealer for Nagra Audio. Programming includes Hi-Fi Night School events.
nodes.cv: 1451 E 4th St, Suite 106, Arts District, DTLA. Hi-fi showroom + record shop + listening space + turntable repair. Nagra Audio dealer. Instagram: @commonwave_hifi.

notableWork.label: Hi-Fi Night School events

nodes.name: Slow Jamz
nodes.bio: LA gallery, event space, and hi-fi listening environment founded by Elwood Espiritu in 2017. Hosts album listening parties, live jazz, R&B events, and record-focused gatherings. Lifestyle brand ('a playlist you can wear') with a gallery and event space at 627 S Central Ave. Strong community following.
nodes.cv: 627 S Central Ave, DTLA. Founded 2017 by Elwood Espiritu, Long Beach. Gallery + event space + bar. Programming: jazz covers, album listening parties, R&B yoga, vinyl events. Instagram: @slowjamzgallery / @slowjamz.co.

nodes.name: Supervinyl
nodes.bio: Hollywood hi-fi record store and listening space founded by Barry Perlman (co-founder of Lucky Brand). Specializes in new and remastered vinyl alongside turntables, speakers, and amplifiers. Dark room with large speakers and vinyl-centric programming. Crowd that pays attention to what's playing.
nodes.cv: 900 N Sycamore Ave, Hollywood, CA. Founded by Barry Perlman (Lucky Brand co-founder). New + remastered vinyl. Turntables, speakers, amps. Instagram: @supervinylusa.

nodes.name: Darling
nodes.bio: West Hollywood restaurant and all-vinyl hi-fi listening lounge opened August 2025 by James Beard Award-winning chef Sean Brock. Adjacent dining room and dedicated listening lounge with custom seven-speaker system built with DJ/speaker artisan MAXV, vintage Tannoy equipment, and a Swiss-made mixer. Vinyl drawn from Brock's personal collection of 5,000+ records — Blue Note jazz to obscure cumbia.
nodes.cv: 631 N Robertson Blvd, West Hollywood. Opened August 2025. Chef: Sean Brock (James Beard Award). Custom 7-speaker system (w/ MAXV) + vintage Tannoy + Swiss mixer. 5,000+ record collection. Live-fire California cuisine.

nodes.name: Dusty Vinyl
nodes.bio: Speakeasy dive bar and vinyl-focused neighborhood spot in West LA hosting DJs and record-centered events. Features pool tables, red leather booths, a dance floor, cocktails, and food. Low-key and neighborhood-oriented.
nodes.cv: 11326 W Pico Blvd, West LA. Speakeasy dive aesthetic. Pool tables, red leather booths, dance floor. Vinyl DJs and record events. Tue–Sat 5pm–2am. Instagram: @dustyvinylbar.

nodes.name: The Hi-Lo
nodes.bio: One of America's earliest dedicated listening bars, built around vinyl culture rather than Instagram aesthetics. Natural wine and cocktail bar with a large outdoor patio in Humboldt Park. Offers both 'hi' (stronger) and 'lo' (lighter) cocktail options. Deep roots in Chicago's record community.
nodes.cv: 1108 N California Ave, Humboldt Park, Chicago. Natural wine + cocktails. No food served (outside food welcome). Tues–Thurs 5pm–2am, Fri–Mon 2pm–2am.

nodes.name: Dorian's Through The Record Shop
nodes.bio: Hidden speakeasy bar accessed through a record shop front in Wicker Park. Creative cocktails, globally inspired shared plates, progressive performances, and vinyl selections. Resident DJs and live programming spanning jazz, soul, disco, house, and experimental. A crowd that genuinely cares about records.
nodes.cv: 1939 W North Ave, Wicker Park, Chicago. Enter through the record shop. Tue–Sun 6pm–1/2am. Creative cocktails + globally inspired small plates. Regular DJ and live music programming.

nodes.name: Judson & Moore Distillery
nodes.bio: American whiskey distillery in Chicago whose Tasting Room at Rockwell on the River features a custom sound system and hosts world-class music and listening events. 120-capacity room built in a century-old former leather tannery. Focus on acoustic music, community programming, and dedicated audiophile listening sessions.
nodes.cv: 3057 N Rockwell St, Chicago. 120-cap tasting room in former leather tannery. Custom sound system. Community-focused music programming. Fri 4pm–10pm, Sat 2pm–8pm.

nodes.name: Bar Shiru
nodes.bio: The Bay Area's first hi-fi vinyl listening bar and one of the most respected in the country. Fully analog audiophile sound system; records played in their entirety. Focuses on jazz, soul, afrobeat, spiritual jazz, dub, ambient, and leftfield electronics. Craft cocktails, Japanese whisky, local beer and sake. Groups of 6 or fewer only.
nodes.cv: Founded 2019 by Daniel Gahr and Shirin Raza. 1611 Telegraph Ave, Uptown Oakland. Fully analog system. Jazz/soul/afrobeat focus. 21+. No food. First-come, first-served seating.

nodes.name: Paradiso Vinyl Club
nodes.bio: New Orleans vinyl listening bar and events series with sophisticated programming moving from jazz and soul into cosmic and experimental territory. Strong selectors and sound. Operates in a city whose deep record culture is underrepresented in its listening-bar scene.
nodes.cv: New Orleans, LA. Vinyl club / listening bar. Programming spans jazz, soul, cosmic, experimental. Instagram: @paradisovinylclub.

nodes.name: Dante's HiFi
nodes.bio: Miami's first vinyl listening bar and one of the most cited in America. Co-founded by Walshy Fire (Major Lazer). Music Director: Rich Medina, with a personal library of 8,000+ records. Cozy 50-seat venue with extraordinary analog sound system. Strong Japanese audiophile influence and resident selectors. Genres span jazz, soul, reggae, dub, and global music.
nodes.cv: 519 NW 26th St, Wynwood, Miami. Founded with Walshy Fire (Major Lazer). Music Director: Rich Medina (8,000+ records). 50-seat capacity. Analog system. Thu–Sun 7pm–1am. Listed in World's 50 Best Bars Discovery.

nodes.name: Press Club
nodes.bio: Record bar and cocktail lounge in Dupont Circle with 1970s cocktail lounge aesthetics and a 1990s record shop soul. Serious vinyl program with unusually thoughtful booking — genres rotate daily from vintage disco and jazz to Brazilian music. Co-founded by Will Patton, Devin Kennedy, and Hive Hospitality.
nodes.cv: 1506 19th St NW, Dupont Circle, DC. Co-founders: Will Patton, Devin Kennedy (Hive Hospitality). Daily genre rotation. Mon–Thu 5pm–12am, Fri 5pm–1am, Sat 2pm–1am.

nodes.name: Mickey's Tavern
nodes.bio: East Nashville institution and one of the better record-centric spaces in the South. Laid-back neighborhood bar with an excellent jukebox, pool tables, darts, foosball, and a back deck. Deep roots in Nashville's vinyl and music community.
nodes.cv: 2907 Gallatin Pike, East Nashville. Great jukebox. Pool, darts, foosball. 8 beers on tap + substantial whiskey selection. Mon–Fri 2pm–3am, Sat–Sun 12pm–3am.

nodes.name: Tiger Bar
nodes.bio: East Nashville cocktail bar with a hi-fi focus and eclectic vinyl selectors. Known among Nashville music and record people for strong programming and an attentive listening environment.
nodes.cv: East Nashville. Hi-fi focus. Eclectic selectors. Cocktail-forward.

nodes.name: Life on Mars
nodes.bio: Vinyl bar, vegan restaurant, and record shop on Capitol Hill, Seattle. Features a wall of 6,000 vinyl records guests can select from. Excellent jazz and soul programming with one of the most collector-oriented crowds in the Pacific Northwest. Covered outdoor patio. 21+ with ID.
nodes.cv: 722 E Pike St, Capitol Hill, Seattle. 6,000 vinyl record wall. Vegan food. Craft cocktails. Tue–Fri from 4pm, Sat–Sun brunch from 11am. 21+.

nodes.name: Equipment Room
nodes.bio: Perhaps the most beautifully designed listening room built in the US in recent years. Hidden in the former equipment room beneath Hotel Magdalena on Music Lane. Japanese jazz kissa culture meets Texas sensibility. Custom vintage hi-fi equipment and 1,200-LP collection. Co-founded by Amar Lalvani (Standard International/Bunkhouse), James Moody (Mohawk), and Josh LaRue & Gabe Vaughn (Breakaway Records).
nodes.cv: 1101 Music Ln, Hotel Magdalena, South Congress, Austin. Founded by Lalvani (Standard Hotels/Bunkhouse), Moody (Mohawk), LaRue & Vaughn (Breakaway Records). 1,200 LPs. Custom vintage hi-fi. Mon–Thu 5pm–12am, Fri 5pm–2am, Sat 2pm–2am.

nodes.name: Spiritland
nodes.bio: The flagship European listening bar. Founded by broadcaster and radio veteran Stuart Stubbs, Spiritland is built around a bespoke Living Voice hi-fi system designed by Kevin Scott — widely considered one of the finest in the world. Daily album playbacks at 6pm. Programming is rigorously eclectic: electronica, country, jazz, soul, and beyond, seven days a week. Also houses a professional radio production suite.
nodes.cv: 9–10 Stable Street, King's Cross, London. Free entry. Booking advised. Daily 6pm album playback. Radio production suite available to hire. Café by day, bar by night.

nodes.name: Jumbi
nodes.bio: Co-founded by DJ and selector Bradley Zero (Rhythm Section) and Nathanael Williams. Built around Zero's personal vinyl collection — guests are invited to play records from a giant 12" wall. Deliberately one-turntable-only, prioritising deep listening and selection over mixing. Afro-Caribbean sounds, reggae, dub, jazz, and global grooves. Rum cocktails. Community-driven.
nodes.cv: Rye Lane, Peckham, London. Co-founded by Bradley Zero (Rhythm Section) and Nathanael Williams. Single-turntable listening focus. Rum cocktails. Resident DJs + guest selectors. Live sets and listening sessions.

nodes.name: Brilliant Corners
nodes.bio: The most influential London venue in the listening-bar category and one of the most respected in the world. Founded 2013 by brothers Amit and Aneesh Patel. Combines a world-class sound system (Klipschorn speakers), Japanese small plates, live jazz, and carefully curated vinyl sessions. Pioneered London's entire hi-fi bar scene. Patel's wider universe includes Giant Steps (a travelling sound system), Idle Moments wine and record store, and Mu.
nodes.cv: 470 Kingsland Rd, Dalston, London E8 4AE. Founded 2013 by Amit & Aneesh Patel. Klipschorn sound system. Japanese small plates. Izakaya atmosphere. Sibling projects: Giant Steps, Idle Moments, Mu.

nodes.name: Behind This Wall
nodes.bio: One of London's earliest and most characterful listening bars. Hidden beneath the Narrow Way in Hackney Central in a former Turkish men's club. Opened 2015. Vintage Tannoy Gold sound system — originally scored from Joy Division producer Martin Hannett. No menu: cocktails are made to order. All-seated; booking essential. Lo-fi bar with hi-fi intentions.
nodes.cv: Beneath the Narrow Way, Hackney Central, London. Opened 2015. Vintage Tannoy Gold system (ex-Martin Hannett). No cocktail menu — bespoke to taste. All-seated, bookings essential. Tonkotsu food available to order in.

nodes.name: Bambi
nodes.bio: Café and listening bar in London Fields by day; record bar, natural wine, cocktails, and small plates by night. Founded by James Dye (Frank's Café, Camberwell Arms). Music programme curated by Charlie Dark. Features a bespoke stainless-steel-and-wood vinyl wall cabinet. Expanded and relaunched in 2026 with doubled space.
nodes.cv: Netil House, 1 Westgate Street, Hackney E8. Founded by James Dye. Music curator: Charlie Dark. Café mornings, listening bar evenings. Expanded 2026. Natural wine + cocktails + small plates.

nodes.name: Bambino
nodes.bio: Probably the most celebrated contemporary Paris record bar. Founded by Fabien Lombardi. Blends Japanese jazz kissa culture with a Parisian sensibility: Klipsch speakers, natural wines, charcoal-grilled cuisine, and vinyl-driven selector nights. Transforms into a dance bar on weekends. A favourite meeting point for Paris's fashion and music crowd.
nodes.cv: 25 Rue Saint-Sébastien, Haut Marais, Paris. Founded by Fabien Lombardi. Klipsch sound system. Natural wines. Charcoal-grilled cuisine. DJ nights Fri–Sat.

nodes.name: Fréquence
nodes.bio: Dedicated vinyl and cocktail bar founded by Matthieu Biron (ex-Andy Wahloo) and Guillaume Quenza (ex-Sherry Butt). Back bar stocked with records rather than bottles. Soul, funk, reggae, disco, and afro. Japanese-influenced cocktail menu (sake, umeshu). Transitions into a late-night dancing bar at midnight. Open until 2am weekdays, 4am weekends.
nodes.cv: Paris, France. Founded by Matthieu Biron & Guillaume Quenza. Records behind the bar. Soul/funk/reggae/disco/afro. Live DJ sets weekends. Late-night dancing from midnight. Open until 4am Fri–Sat.

nodes.name: Superflu
nodes.bio: Audio-focused restaurant and wine bar near Montmartre, opened by Jean-Baptiste Andrau (architect) and Louis Bruneteau (founder of wine bar Stéréo). Natural wines, vinyl DJs, and seasonal shared plates with a retro-futuristic interior. Weekend tables are cleared for a dance floor. One of the fastest-rising spaces in Paris for the wine-meets-vinyl crowd.
nodes.cv: 227 bis Rue Marcadet, 18th arrondissement, Paris. Founded by Jean-Baptiste Andrau & Louis Bruneteau. Natural wines. Seasonal shared plates. Vinyl DJs on weekends. Dance floor transforms the space at night.

nodes.name: La Bellevilloise
nodes.bio: Historic multidisciplinary cultural venue founded 1877 as a worker's cooperative — now a 2,500m² space for concerts, clubs, DJ nights, exhibitions, film, workshops, and Sunday jazz brunches. Not a listening bar per se, but central to Paris's selector culture and a key venue for roots, disco, afro, cumbia, and experimental music programming.
nodes.cv: 19 Rue Boyer, 20th arrondissement, Paris. Founded 1877. 2,500m² multidisciplinary space. Concerts, club nights, jazz brunches, exhibitions. House, soul, afro, cumbia, electro, jazz programming.

nodes.name: Bar Neiro
nodes.bio: Berlin's most critically admired listening bar, modelled on Japanese jazz kissaten. Sound system designed by recording engineer Erik Breuer: Garrard 301 turntables, Altec A5 speakers, Shindo amplifiers, Audio-Technica cartridges. Every LP played front to back. Japanese-inspired cocktails. Part of the Analogue Foundation Berlin (co-founded 2016 by producer Russell Elbad, Soundwalk Collective, and Audio-Technica).
nodes.cv: Ohmstraße 11, Neukölln, Berlin. Part of Analogue Foundation Berlin (Russell Elbad, Soundwalk Collective, Audio-Technica). Garrard 301 / Altec A5 / Shindo system. Psychedelic jazz to experimental. Japanese cocktails.

nodes.name: Unkompress
nodes.bio: Berlin hi-fi listening space and café founded by Kevin Rodriguez, designed for enjoying analogue audio in a refined social environment. Klipsch Cornwall IV speakers with custom Elekit tube amplification. Japanese-inspired cocktails and small plates. Sits at the intersection of Berlin bar culture and Japanese kissa aesthetics.
nodes.cv: Berlin, Germany. Founded by Kevin Rodriguez. Klipsch Cornwall IV + Elekit tube amp. Japanese-inspired cocktails and small plates. Intimate listening culture.

nodes.name: Oona Bar
nodes.bio: In-studio bar and community space inside Refuge Worldwide radio station, launched 2021. Natural wine, cocktails, and DJ sets broadcast live from the back room. Regularly hosts secondhand record sales (including collections from Berghain resident Nick Höppner, HKW music director Edna Martinez). A casual but genuinely music-literate space in Neukölln.
nodes.cv: Weserstrasse, Neukölln, Berlin. Bar inside Refuge Worldwide radio. Natural wine + cocktails. Live DJ sets + radio broadcasts. Record sales. Founded 2021.

nodes.name: Doka
nodes.bio: Subterranean sound bar in the basement of Volkshotel. Six-point Danley Sound Labs system plus ATC SCM45A studio monitors, custom DJ booth by Luuk Vlammings (1970s recording booth aesthetic). Long, uncompromising vinyl sets (house, disco, techno, experimental) curated with artists and creatives from Volkshotel's Broedplaats VKG studios. One of Europe's best-sounding underground club-bar spaces.
nodes.cv: Wibautstraat 150, Amsterdam (Volkshotel basement). Six-point Danley + ATC studio monitor system. Custom DJ booth by Luuk Vlammings. Fri–Sat open-to-close sets. House/disco/techno/experimental.

nodes.name: Murmur
nodes.bio: Listening bar and cultural space in Amsterdam Noord with a custom 4-point sound system, concrete floors, warm wood accents, and a collector-minded crowd. Extended 6-hour vinyl sets from resident and guest selectors. Natural wines, minimal cocktails, small plates. Open Friday and Saturday only. One of Amsterdam's most considered listening environments.
nodes.cv: Aambeeldstraat 60, 1021 KB Amsterdam Noord. Custom 4-point sound system. Natural wines + minimal cocktails. 6-hour vinyl sets Fri–Sat. Restaurant, bar, and live music space.

nodes.name: Canvas
nodes.bio: Rooftop bar and venue on the 7th floor of Volkshotel. Regular vinyl and listening events sit alongside club nights, concerts, and exhibitions. Panoramic city views. Central to Amsterdam's creative cultural scene and a key space for underground DJ programming.
nodes.cv: Wibautstraat 150, 7th floor, Amsterdam (Volkshotel). Rooftop bar + venue. Regular vinyl nights, club programming, concerts, exhibitions. Panoramic Amsterdam views.

nodes.name: Bird
nodes.bio: Perhaps Europe's most design-conscious listening bar. Named after Charlie Parker. Acoustically engineered space with custom-built speakers and wall panels delivering crystal-clear vinyl reproduction. Weekly visits from local collectors and international selectors across low-fi genres. Cocktail programme of the highest standard. Recognised in the World's 50 Best Bars Discovery guide.
nodes.cv: Frederiksberg, Copenhagen. Acoustic free-space design. Custom-built speakers. Local + international selectors weekly. Charlie Parker-referenced concept. World's 50 Best Discovery.

nodes.name: ALICE
nodes.bio: Copenhagen's home for jazz, experimental electronic, global, and folk music. Formed from the merger of Global and Jazzhouse in 2018. A collectively run booking team ensures democratic, diverse programming across improvised jazz, sound art, roots, metal, and avant-garde electronics. Intimate brick-building setting in an idyllic Nørrebro courtyard. Vinyl in the bar.
nodes.cv: Inner Nørrebro, Copenhagen. Merger of Global and Jazzhouse (2018). Collectively run. Jazz, experimental, folk, global, electronic, avant-garde. Brick courtyard setting.

nodes.name: Den Vandrette
nodes.bio: Natural wine bar in Copenhagen's Nyhavn harbour by merchant house Rosforth & Rosforth, suppliers to some of the city's best restaurants. All wines are biodynamic and natural. Charcuterie, cheese, bread, and a daily hot dish. Embedded in Copenhagen's selector and record culture as a gathering space — not a dedicated listening bar but adjacent to the scene.
nodes.cv: Havnegade 53, Nyhavn, Copenhagen. By Rosforth & Rosforth. 100% biodynamic/natural wine. Charcuterie + cheese + hot dish daily. Relaxed harbour setting.

nodes.name: Gesto
nodes.bio: Often cited as Italy's first and best-known listening bar. Inspired by Japanese jazz kissaten. Separate restaurant and listening-bar spaces. The 'Malinconia' listening room hosts live jam sessions and vinyl DJ sets late into the night. Secret karaoke room ('Almost Famous'). Annual art installations by international artists. 300+ record collection.
nodes.cv: Porta Venezia, Milan. Japanese listening bar concept. Restaurant + Malinconia listening room + Almost Famous karaoke. Live jam sessions + vinyl DJs. Annual art residencies. 300+ LPs.

nodes.name: Moebius
nodes.bio: Ranked #7 on the World's 50 Best Bars 2025. A former textile warehouse transformed by Lorenzo Querci into an extraordinary multidisciplinary space: cocktail bar (led by Giovanni Allario), vinyl shop, live jazz (Mon & Sun), vinyl DJs (Thu), tapas bistro, and a glass-enclosed fine-dining restaurant elevated on a first floor above a 700-year-old, 10-metre olive tree under 20-metre-high ceilings. One of the most ambitious listening and hospitality spaces in Europe.
nodes.cv: Milan. Founded by Lorenzo Querci. Cocktail bar (Giovanni Allario) + vinyl shop + live jazz Mon/Sun + vinyl DJs Thu + tapas bistro + fine-dining restaurant. 700-year-old olive tree. 20m ceilings. World's 50 Best Bars #7 (2025).

nodes.name: Dexter Soundbites
nodes.bio: Intimate 25-seat listening bar and restaurant in Milan's Isola district founded by English-Italian chef Giovanni DeNardi after a formative month in Japan. Vinyl plays through the meal. Japanese, Peruvian, and Mexican influences on the kitchen. Strong cocktail and wine list. Milan's most food-forward dedicated listening space.
nodes.cv: Via Carmagnola 15, Isola, Milan. Founded by Giovanni DeNardi. 25 seats. Japanese/Peruvian/Mexican kitchen. Vinyl throughout service. Craft cocktails + wine.

nodes.name: Livingston
nodes.bio: Marseille's leading wine-and-music restaurant and cultural venue. Founded by chef Valentin Raffali with The Small Group France 5 (Harry Cummins, Julia Litton, Laura Vidal). Marseille's first dedicated orange wine bar. Music-forward philosophy: 'great food and music, all night long'. Yakitori, sashimi, grilled mains. Won Best French Wine Bar at Le Fooding 2022.
nodes.cv: 5 Rue Crudère, 13006 Marseille. Founded by Valentin Raffali (chef) with The Small Group France 5. Orange wine bar + music venue. Weekly rotating seasonal menu. Wed–Sun 19:15–23:30. Le Fooding Best French Wine Bar 2022.

nodes.name: Musa de Marvila
nodes.bio: Artisanal brewery, concert hall, and cultural venue in Lisbon's Marvila warehouse district. Regular vinyl DJ sets across funk, world, and disco alongside live performances, karaoke, and community events. One of Lisbon's most important spaces for the intersection of music, craft beer, and neighbourhood culture.
nodes.cv: Rua do Vale Formoso 9, Marvila, Lisbon. Artisanal brewery + concert hall + cultural venue. Vinyl DJ nights (funk/world/disco). Live music. Tue–Thu until midnight, Fri–Sat until 3am.

nodes.name: Bonnefooi
nodes.bio: Music bar and late-night venue in the heart of Brussels with free entry and an adventurous booking policy. Live music and DJ sets Thu–Sat from 8pm to 8am. Name means 'by chance' — reflecting its open, unpredictable programming. Part of Brussels's independent music community.
nodes.cv: Brussels city centre. Free entry. Thu–Sat 8pm–8am. Live music + DJ sets. Independent, community-driven programming.

nodes.name: Curtis Audiophile Cafe
nodes.bio: Barcelona's leading audiophile café and record store. Three spaces in one: hi-fi listening room, record store, and café/cocktail bar. State-of-the-art sound system designed for crystal-clear audio. Live DJ sessions and performances showcasing local talent. Located in Eixample, part of Europe's growing Japanese jazz kissa tradition.
nodes.cv: Carrer de Mallorca 196, Eixample, Barcelona. Audiophile sound system. Record store. Café + cocktails. Live DJ sessions + performances.

nodes.name: Rhiz
nodes.bio: Legendary Vienna bar and experimental music venue established 1998, set beneath the U-Bahn arches (Gürtel). Central to Vienna's electronic music scene — the artistic home where Fennesz, Radian, and Pita Rehberg developed their work. Exceptional sound quality. Experimental electronics, avant-garde, and advanced club music several nights a week.
nodes.cv: U-Bahnbogen 37, Gürtel, Vienna. Opened 1998. Under U-Bahn arches. Vienna's electronic music home: Fennesz, Radian, Pita Rehberg. Experimental electronics + avant-garde + club music.

nodes.name: Pet Sounds Bar
nodes.bio: Extension of Pet Sounds Records, one of Scandinavia's most celebrated independent record shops (est. 1979). Bar and restaurant in Katarinahuset at Slussen with waterfront views, records on the walls, and music as the focus. Concerts, DJ gigs, and music quizzes. European wine focus + craft beer. Summer terrace overlooking the Slussen waters.
nodes.cv: Stadsgården 6, Katarinahuset, Slussen, Stockholm. Extension of Pet Sounds Records (est. 1979). Bar + restaurant. Concerts, DJs, music quizzes. European wine + craft beer. Summer waterfront terrace.

nodes.name: Fasching
nodes.bio: Scandinavia's largest and most historic jazz club, founded 1977. A concert venue, club, bar, and restaurant. Shaped how Scandinavia listens across jazz, soul, and experimental music. Soul Saturdays (Club Soul) has run continuously for 30+ years with Robert Baum, Magnus Räms, and Fredrik Ekander ('The Swedish Soul Mafia') spinning vinyl front to back. Also hosts global, electronic, and avant-garde programming.
nodes.cv: Kungsgatan 63, Stockholm. Founded 1977. Scandinavia's largest jazz organiser. Club Soul (Saturday vinyl nights) running 30+ years with The Swedish Soul Mafia (Baum, Räms, Ekander). Jazz, global, electronic, avant-garde.

nodes.name: Diana Kunst
nodes.bio: Filipino-Spanish photographer and director. One of the most sought-after music video directors working today. Trained in fine art photography at Escuela de Arte La Palma in Madrid before moving into film. Known for visually meticulous work that balances still-image precision with strong storytelling.
nodes.cv: Director at ANORAK Film. Music videos: Madonna + Maluma 'Medellín' (2019), Lenny Kravitz 'Honey' (2024), C. Tangana 'Caballo Ganador' (2017), A$AP Rocky, FKA Twigs, Rosalía, James Blake. Commercial: Gucci, Ralph Lauren, Mercedes.

notableWork.label: Madonna + Maluma – Medellín (2019)
Lenny Kravitz – Honey (2024)
C. Tangana – Caballo Ganador (2017)

nodes.name: Mah Ferraz
nodes.bio: Brazilian film editor and co-founder of Church Edit (LA/NY). One of the most decorated editors working in music video and commercial film. Over 85 awards including Cannes Lions, Emmys, D&AD, VMAs, Clio, and Webby. Forbes 30 Under 30 nominee (2022).
