Church Edit named Editing Company of the Year at the inaugural Eyecandy Awards (2024); Ferraz named Editor of the Year.
nodes.cv: Co-founder, Church Edit (LA/NY). Editing credits: Billie Eilish, Lady Gaga, Megan Thee Stallion. Brand clients: Apple, Nike, Google, Meta, Puma, Coca-Cola. 85+ awards: Cannes Lions, Emmy, D&AD, AICP, The One Show, Ciclope. Vimeo Staff Picks. Featured in NYT, Vogue, Forbes, Variety, Nowness.

nodes.name: Romain Gavras
nodes.bio: French director, son of filmmaker Costa-Gavras. One of the most influential and provocative music video directors of his generation. Known for cinematic scale, political intensity, and visceral imagery. Also works in feature film.
nodes.cv: Music videos: M.I.A. 'Born Free' (2010), M.I.A. 'Bad Girls', Kanye West + Jay-Z 'No Church in the Wild', Jamie xx 'Gosh' (Golden Lion, Cannes Lions 2016), Justice. Features: 'Our Day Will Come' (2010, Vincent Cassel), 'The World Is Yours' (2018, Cannes Directors' Fortnight).

notableWork.label: M.I.A. – Born Free (2010)
Jamie xx – Gosh (Golden Lion, Cannes Lions 2016)
Kanye West + Jay-Z – No Church in the Wild

nodes.name: Philip Sherburne
nodes.bio: One of the most authoritative voices on electronic and experimental music writing in English. Contributing editor at Pitchfork since 2014, with 500+ reviews. Coined the term 'Microhouse' in a 2001 article for The Wire. Freelance writer for NYT, The Wire, SPIN, Resident Advisor, and more. Co-runs Balmat, an ambient and ambient-adjacent record label. Co-hosts Lapsus Radio (weekly Spanish-language electronic music show). Publishes weekly Substack 'Futurism Restated' (new music picks, interviews, playlists).
nodes.cv: Pitchfork contributing editor (2014–present). Coined 'Microhouse' (The Wire, 2001). Publications: NYT, The Wire, SPIN, Resident Advisor. Label: Balmat (ambient/ambient-adjacent). Radio: Lapsus Radio (co-host). Substack: Futurism Restated.

notableWork.label: Coined 'Microhouse' – The Wire (2001)
Balmat (record label, ambient/ambient-adjacent)
Futurism Restated (Substack)

nodes.name: Nowadays
nodes.bio: One of NYC's most beloved independent indoor-outdoor music venues, on the Bushwick/Ridgewood border. 5,000 sq ft indoor warehouse space plus an expansive seasonal outdoor area. Home to Mister Sunday (the legendary day party by Justin Carter and Eamon Harkin), Ridgewood Market, and a year-round programme spanning techno, house, funk, soul, and experimental music.
nodes.cv: 56-06 Cooper Ave, Ridgewood, NY 11385. Founded 2015. Indoor warehouse + outdoor beer garden. Mister Sunday day parties (Justin Carter & Eamon Harkin). Nonstop weekend programming. Also hosts readings and community events.

notableWork.label: Mister Sunday (resident day party)

nodes.name: Sam Hine
nodes.bio: GQ's Global Fashion Correspondent. Joined GQ Style in 2016, became style editor in 2019, appointed Global Fashion Correspondent in 2022. Writes about fashion, beauty, and culture with a particular focus on menswear and new masculinity. Co-hosts GQ's style podcast Corporate Lunch. Launched GQ's Show Notes newsletter in June 2022. Has profiled A$AP Rocky, John Mayer, and Henry Golding.
nodes.cv: GQ Global Fashion Correspondent (2022–present). GQ Style Editor (2019–22). Co-host: Corporate Lunch podcast. Newsletter: Show Notes (GQ). Studied Chinese and history. Based in New York.

notableWork.label: Corporate Lunch (GQ podcast, co-host)
Show Notes newsletter (GQ)

nodes.name: Will Welch
nodes.bio: One of the most influential editors in American fashion and culture media. Started at The Fader in the early 2000s before joining GQ in 2007. Rose to become GQ's Creative Director and Editor-in-Chief of GQ Style, then Editor-in-Chief of American GQ (2019), then Global Brand Editorial Director (2020). Oversaw the folding of Pitchfork into GQ in 2024. Championed 'new masculinity' throughout his tenure. Subsequently partnered with Pharrell Williams after departing Condé Nast.
nodes.cv: Global Brand Editorial Director, GQ (2020–2024). Editor-in-Chief, GQ US (2019–20). Editor-in-Chief, GQ Style. Creative Director, GQ. The Fader (early career). Oversaw Pitchfork's absorption into GQ (2024). Now working with Pharrell Williams.

notableWork.label: GQ Style (Editor-in-Chief)
Pitchfork integration into GQ (2024)

nodes.name: Special Projects
nodes.bio: UI/UX and product design studio specialising in experiences for future hardware. Combines technical depth with human insight to create strategies for robotics, aerospace, AI, biotech, transportation, and defence. Designs the software, interfaces, and launch narratives for emerging technology companies and their investors.
nodes.cv: Co-founders: Dennis Dang (@2050beyond) and Case Miller (@CaseMiller___). Services: Product Strategy, Product Experience, Product Launch. Industries: Robotics, Aerospace, Transportation, Defence, AI, Biotech. South Pasadena, CA — by appointment.

nodes.name: Special Offer, Inc.
nodes.bio: Award-winning creative tech studio committed to the growth of subculture through digital experience. Founded 2014 by artist Brent David Freaney. Work spans creative direction, visual identity, UI/UX, website design and development, album artwork, campaign identity, exhibition design, and titles for film and TV. Made global headlines for the visual identity behind Charli XCX's 'Brat' campaign (2024). Deep roots in music, fashion, and underground culture.
nodes.cv: Founded 2014 by Brent David Freaney. Lower Manhattan. Services: creative direction, visual identity, UI/UX, web dev, campaign identity, album artwork, exhibition design, film/TV titles. Notable: Charli XCX 'Brat' campaign (2024). Full-time design + engineering team.

notableWork.label: Charli XCX – Brat campaign identity (2024)

nodes.name: Bailey Marklew
nodes.bio: London-based film and creative director. Founder of Studio Info. Specialist in concept-driven campaign content for fashion, streetwear, and outdoor brands. Long-running creative relationship with Cav Empt. Known for a rigorous, understated visual language that suits subculture-adjacent brands.
nodes.cv: Director and founder of Studio Info. Campaign credits: Cav Empt (ongoing, SS23–Nike DN8 2025), Palace Skateboards, Arc'teryx, Nike, Gore-Tex, Salomon, The Trilogy Tapes (TTT), Pop Trading Co, Rapha.

notableWork.label: Cav Empt x Nike DN8 (2025)
Palace x Arc'teryx (2020)
Palace x Salomon XT-6 (2021)

nodes.name: Studio Info
nodes.bio: London-based creative studio founded by Bailey Marklew. Produces concept-driven content for fashion and commerce, from conception through production and delivery. Strong ties to Palace Skateboards, Cav Empt, and the broader streetwear/outdoor crossover world.
nodes.cv: Founded by Bailey Marklew. Services: creative campaigns, film direction, content production. Key clients: Palace Skateboards, Cav Empt, Arc'teryx, Nike, Gore-Tex, Salomon, The Trilogy Tapes, Pop Trading Co, Rapha.

notableWork.label: Cav Empt (ongoing campaign partner)
Palace Skateboards (ongoing)

nodes.name: Berlin Atonal
nodes.bio: One of Europe's most important festivals for sonic and visual art. Originally ran 1982–1990 as a pivotal force in the progressive electronic and industrial underground, then relaunched in 2013 under new direction. Held annually at Kraftwerk Berlin — the vast former East Berlin power station repurposed by Tresor founder Dimitri Hegemann. Five-day programme of premieres, live A/V performances, installations, and experimental commissions spanning industrial, noise, ambient, techno, and post-club sound.
nodes.cv: Annual festival, Kraftwerk Berlin (Mitte/Kreuzberg). Originally 1982–1990; relaunched 2013. Five-day format. Focus: sonic and visual art premieres, live A/V, installations, commissions. Genres: industrial, experimental, ambient, techno, noise, post-club.

notableWork.label: 1982–1990 era: Einstürzende Neubauten, Malaria!, Laibach, Test Dept, Psychic TV, 808 State
2013–present: Kraftwerk Berlin home base; audio-visual commissions; avant-garde electronic and experimental programming
Unseen, Unheard — OPENLESS project (The Brvtalist); ongoing AV collaborations; DJ Mag 'fuelling the electronic avant-garde' feature

nodes.name: LOST Music Festival
nodes.bio: Immersive electronic and art music festival held inside the world's largest bamboo labyrinth — the Masone Labyrinth in Fontanellato, Parma. A 7-hectare park of 200,000+ bamboo plants conceived by publisher and graphic artist Franco Maria Ricci. Promoted by the Franco Maria Ricci Art Foundation. Celebrates contemporary electronic music through a carefully curated selection of Italian and international artists, emphasising the mystical, esoteric, and unknown. Covered by Crack Magazine.
nodes.cv: Held annually at Labirinto della Masone, Fontanellato, Parma. Promoted by the Franco Maria Ricci Art Foundation. Immersive format: sound, light, nature. Focus: leftfield and experimental electronic music. Annual editions since at least 2023.

nodes.name: C2C Festival (Club to Club)

location.raw: Turin, Italy / Milan / New York, NY

nodes.bio: One of Europe's most important avant-pop and experimental electronic music festivals. Founded 2002 in Turin by Sergio Ricciardone, originally as a multi-venue club night — a single ticket granting access across the city. Grew into a four-day festival welcoming 40,000+ attendees from around the world. Operates at the intersection of electronic music, contemporary art, design, and cultural research. Has moved programming into museums, opera houses, and institutional spaces without losing its experimental edge. Notable brand collaborations include Gucci and Stone Island. Expanded internationally with a New York debut and Milan programming.
nodes.cv: Founded 2002 by Sergio Ricciardone, Turin. Annual festival (Oct–Nov). 40,000+ attendees. Multi-venue format. Avant-pop / experimental / electronic. Collaborators: Gucci, Stone Island. New York and Milan expansions. 2026 marks 25th anniversary.

notableWork.label: Gucci x C2C (multi-year collaboration)
Stone Island x C2C (Milan event)
C2C NYC debut (Queens)

nodes.name: Another Man
nodes.bio: UK men's fashion and culture magazine published by Dazed Group. Founded by Jefferson Hack. Bi-annual print title known for its literary and artistic rigour alongside high fashion — a more cerebral counterpart to AnOther Magazine. Covers fashion, art, music, culture, and ideas with a strong emphasis on long-form editorial and photography.
nodes.cv: Published by Dazed Group. Founded by Jefferson Hack. Bi-annual print. Sister title to AnOther Magazine. Editorial focus: men's fashion, art, music, long-form culture.

nodes.name: Numéro
nodes.bio: French luxury fashion magazine with international editions across multiple markets. Known for exceptional fashion photography, high-end editorial vision, and an uncompromisingly aesthetic approach. A key platform for top photographers, stylists, and designers. Editions include Numéro Paris, Numéro Russia, Numéro Berlin, and more.
nodes.cv: French luxury fashion magazine. Founded 1999. International editions: Paris, Berlin, Russia, and others. Known for photography-forward editorial, luxury advertising partnerships, and strong creative direction.

nodes.name: Vice Magazine
nodes.bio: Originally founded 1994 in Montreal; after Vice Media's 2023 bankruptcy, the print magazine relaunched in 2024 as a lean, London-based independent operation. Global Editorial Director: Ben Ditto. Editor-in-Chief: Kevin Lee Kharas. Quarterly print, targeting 20,000 subscribers. Making some of the most provocative and culturally tuned-in journalism of its new era.
nodes.cv: Founded 1994, Montreal. After Vice Media bankruptcy (2023), print relaunched 2024. Global Editorial Director: Ben Ditto. EIC: Kevin Lee Kharas. London-based. Quarterly print. Goal: 20,000 subscribers.

nodes.name: Public Editions
nodes.bio: Collector-oriented arts and fashion magazine founded 2018 by Alex McWhirter, based in Paris. Each issue is a leading voice on experimental art and design, aimed at collectors and industry insiders rather than the newsstand. Limited edition, deliberately exclusive distribution model.
nodes.cv: Founded 2018 by Alex McWhirter. Paris. Limited-edition format. Collector/trade focus. Experimental art and design editorial. Not distributed on newsstands.

nodes.name: Arkiv Paper
nodes.bio: Bookazine by photographer and human rights activist Kenny Karpov. A publication by photographers, for photographers — combining moody full-bleed imagery with essays, interviews, and profiles that illuminate underground subcultures, human rights issues, and contemporary image-making. Distributed through independent global retailers.
nodes.cv: Founded by Kenny Karpov (photographer/human rights activist). Bookazine format. Themes: photography, subculture, human rights. Issue 2: Sex Workers. Global independent distribution.

nodes.name: HommeGirls
nodes.bio: New York bi-annual magazine built around the concept of women dressing as men — the 'HommeGirl'. Explores the dynamic intersection of fashion, culture, and gender identity through high-concept photography, cutting-edge styling, and in-depth interviews. A leading voice in conversations around gender fluidity in fashion. Has featured Emily Ratajkowski, Kylie Jenner, Kiko Mizuhara, Paloma Elsesser.
nodes.cv: New York. Bi-annual. Concept: women dressing as men / gender-fluid fashion. Studio and street photography. Profiles, interviews, editorials. Notable covers: Ratajkowski, Jenner, Mizuhara, Elsesser.

nodes.name: Cultured Magazine
nodes.bio: New York-based arts and culture magazine covering contemporary art, architecture, design, fashion, and film. Known for beautifully produced print issues, long-form profiles, and a community-oriented approach to covering the creative world. Bridging the museum world and the broader cultural moment.
nodes.cv: New York. Contemporary art, architecture, design, fashion, film. Print + digital. Community-focused. Profiles, studio visits, long-form features. Strong museum and gallery relationships.

nodes.name: 10 Magazine
nodes.bio: UK fashion and culture magazine focused on women's fashion and beauty. Known for strong photography, editorial fashion stories, and a roster of top photographers and stylists. Published quarterly. Part of London's influential independent fashion press landscape alongside titles like POP and Self Service.
nodes.cv: London. Quarterly women's fashion and beauty magazine. Strong editorial photography. Independent fashion press. Features fashion weeks, beauty, profile interviews.

nodes.name: Toilet Paper
nodes.bio: Italian art and photography magazine co-founded by artist Maurizio Cattelan and photographer Pierpaolo Ferrari. Published since 2010. Each issue is a visual experience — surreal, provocative, maximalist imagery that sits between contemporary art, advertising aesthetics, and absurdist humour. Highly collectible. Has expanded into objects, clothing, and brand collaborations.
nodes.cv: Founded 2010 by Maurizio Cattelan (artist) and Pierpaolo Ferrari (photographer). Milan. Bi-annual. Surreal/provocative visual language. Expanded into objects, apparel, collaborations.

nodes.name: L'Officiel
nodes.bio: One of France's oldest and most influential fashion magazines, founded 1921. International editions across 20+ countries. Covers fashion, beauty, art, culture, and lifestyle with a distinctly French sensibility. A key platform for luxury fashion houses and a long record of iconic fashion photography.
nodes.cv: Founded 1921, Paris. Jalou Media Group. 20+ international editions. Fashion, beauty, art, culture. Oldest surviving French fashion magazine.

nodes.name: Mousse Magazine
nodes.bio: Milan-based contemporary art magazine and publishing house founded in 2006. Trilingual (English/Italian/French). Strong focus on international contemporary art, criticism, and artist interviews. Respected within the gallery and museum world as a rigorous, independently-minded publication. Also produces artist books and monographs.
nodes.cv: Founded 2006, Milan. Trilingual: English/Italian/French. Contemporary art criticism, artist interviews, exhibition reviews. Also publishes artist books and monographs. Gallery/museum community focused.

nodes.name: DJ Mag
nodes.bio: The world's leading electronic music magazine, founded 1991 in the UK. Publisher of the annual DJ Mag Top 100 DJs chart — the most widely followed ranking in electronic music. Covers dance music, electronic culture, club nights, festivals, gear, and artist profiles. Print and digital. Global reach across the club music ecosystem.
nodes.cv: Founded 1991, London. World's leading electronic music publication. Publisher of the Top 100 DJs chart (annual). Print + digital. Covers DJ culture, dance music, festivals, clubs, gear.

nodes.name: Office Magazine
nodes.bio: New York-based bi-annual fashion and culture magazine founded in 2014. Designed to subvert convention with an unorthodox approach to creative culture. Covers fashion, fine arts, sports, music, and beauty — spotlighting subversive and unexpected artists alongside high-visibility cultural figures. Combines print publication, digital platform, events, and social programming.
nodes.cv: Founded 2014, NYC. Bi-annual print. Digital platform + events + social programming. Fashion, fine arts, music, sport, beauty. Subversive editorial approach.

nodes.name: Monocle
nodes.bio: Global affairs, business, culture, and design magazine founded 2007 by Tyler Brûlé. Ten print issues per year plus specials. Also operates Monocle Radio, Monocle Café, and retail locations globally. Known for its deeply considered quality-of-life journalism, urban culture coverage, and influential city quality-of-life rankings. A benchmark for intelligent lifestyle media.
nodes.cv: Founded 2007 by Tyler Brûlé. 10 print issues/year + specials. Monocle Radio + cafés + retail globally. Global affairs, design, culture, business. Annual Quality of Life city rankings.

nodes.name: Interview Magazine
nodes.bio: Founded by Andy Warhol and John Wilcock in 1969. A foundational American pop culture and fashion magazine known for its raw, celebrity-driven Q&A interview format and bold photography. Went through bankruptcy and relaunched; currently operating as a digital and print title. An enduring landmark of American cultural media.
nodes.cv: Founded 1969 by Andy Warhol and John Wilcock. Q&A interview format. Fashion, pop culture, celebrity. Bankruptcy + relaunch. Continues as print + digital. Foundational to American fashion media.

nodes.name: POP Magazine
nodes.bio: London-based bi-annual women's fashion magazine founded in 2000. Known for bold, often maximalist fashion imagery and an energetic, youth-culture sensibility. Strong roster of contributing photographers and stylists. Part of the same generation of British independents as 10 and Dazed that redefined UK fashion publishing.
nodes.cv: Founded 2000, London. Bi-annual. Women's fashion. Bold, maximalist photography. British independent fashion press. Contributing photographers + stylists from top tier.

nodes.name: Flaunt
nodes.bio: Los Angeles-based bi-annual fashion and culture magazine founded 1998. Known for a distinctive die-cut cover format and an adventurous, LA-rooted editorial sensibility. Covers fashion, music, art, and culture. A consistent platform for emerging and established creative talent on the West Coast and beyond.
nodes.cv: Founded 1998, Los Angeles. Bi-annual. Distinctive die-cut covers. Fashion, music, art, culture. LA-rooted editorial sensibility.

nodes.name: Self Service
nodes.bio: Parisian bi-annual fashion magazine founded 1995 by Ezra Petronio and Suzanne Koller. Consistently cited as one of the most elegant and influential fashion publications of its generation. Known for its minimalist design, exceptional photography, and intimate access to top designers and houses. A collector's magazine among industry insiders.
nodes.cv: Founded 1995 by Ezra Petronio and Suzanne Koller. Paris. Bi-annual. Minimalist design. Exceptional fashion photography. Strong designer access. Industry collector's magazine.

nodes.name: A Magazine Curated By
nodes.bio: Unique Belgian fashion magazine where each issue is curated entirely by a different fashion designer or house, exploring their aesthetic universe, people, passions, and cultural values. Founded ~2000; first issue curated by Maison Martin Margiela. 29+ issues to date with curators including Yohji Yamamoto, Grace Wales Bonner, Sacai, Peter Do, Glenn Martens, Cecilie Bahnsen. Also produces special editions (e.g. A Manual for Living Curated for Nike, 2026).
nodes.cv: Founded ~2000, Antwerp. Each issue curated by a different fashion designer/house. 29+ issues. Curators include: Margiela, Yamamoto, Wales Bonner, Sacai, Peter Do, Glenn Martens, Cecilie Bahnsen. Twitter/IG: @amagazinecuratedby.

notableWork.label: N°1: Maison Martin Margiela
N°22: Grace Wales Bonner
A Manual for Living (Nike, 2026)

nodes.name: Double Magazine
nodes.bio: Parisian bi-annual fashion and photography magazine. Currently on issue 51. Known for exceptional fashion photography and a refined, collector-oriented approach to the print format. Instagram: @double_magazine.
nodes.cv: Paris. Bi-annual. Issue 51 (current). Fashion photography. Collector-oriented print format.

nodes.name: Highsnobiety
nodes.bio: Berlin-founded media brand and website covering streetwear, fashion, sneakers, music, art, and culture. Founded 2005 by David Fischer. One of the most influential platforms in the streetwear and sneaker space globally. Has expanded into brand consultancy, creative direction, content production, and retail. A key tastemaker for the hype and luxury streetwear crossover audience.
nodes.cv: Founded 2005 by David Fischer, Berlin. Website + brand consultancy + creative agency + retail. Streetwear, sneakers, fashion, music, art. Key platform for luxury-streetwear crossover culture.

nodes.name: Hypebeast
nodes.bio: Hong Kong-founded global media platform covering streetwear, sneakers, fashion, art, music, and design. Founded 2005 by Kevin Ma. One of the world's highest-traffic fashion and culture websites. Also operates HBX (e-commerce), Hypebeans (café), and Hypemaker (creative agency). An essential platform in the global streetwear, sneaker, and hype culture ecosystem.
nodes.cv: Founded 2005 by Kevin Ma, Hong Kong. Website + HBX (e-commerce) + Hypebeans (café) + Hypemaker (creative agency). Streetwear, sneakers, fashion, art, music. Global reach.

nodes.name: Jungle Magazine
nodes.bio: London-based bi-annual fashion and culture publication and creative studio. Covers fashion, art, music, and culture. Strong editorial photography, international contributors, and a distinct independent voice. Also operates Jungle Works, a creative studio arm. Has featured Jemima Kirke, Tove Lo, MNEK, Blue Lab Beats, and Poppy Ajudha.
nodes.cv: London. Bi-annual fashion and culture publication. Creative studio arm: Jungle Works. Covers fashion, art, music, culture. Features: Jemima Kirke, Tove Lo, MNEK, Blue Lab Beats, Poppy Ajudha.

nodes.name: Priscillia Saada
nodes.bio: Paris-based photographer and director represented by Home Agency (New York). Known for images that capture ephemeral moments of human expression — intimate, unforced, and carefully art-directed despite their apparent spontaneity. Works across fashion editorial, advertising campaigns, and personal fine art projects. Clients include Vogue Italia and STAUD.
nodes.cv: Represented by Home Agency, NYC. Fashion editorial, advertising, personal projects. Clients: Vogue Italia, STAUD. Motion work alongside stills practice.

nodes.name: Eugene Whang
nodes.bio: Vancouver-born industrial designer and DJ who spent 20+ years at Apple on Jony Ive's core industrial design team — one of the most secretive and consequential product design groups in history. Credited across patents spanning the iPod Nano, iPhone, iPad, MacBook, Apple Watch, and AirPods eras. Post-Apple, joined LoveFrom (Jony Ive's independent studio) before moving into independent creative direction. By night (and increasingly by day), he operates as DJ Eug — founder of Public Release Recordings and the FACE party series in San Francisco.
nodes.cv: 20+ years at Apple on Jony Ive's industrial design team (iPod Nano → AirPods era). LoveFrom (Jony Ive's studio post-Apple). DJ/producer: Eug. Founder: Public Release Recordings + FACE party series (Bay Area). Product collab: Pharrell/Humanrace travel kit. Featured: SSENSE, Highsnobiety, Wallpaper*.

notableWork.label: Apple industrial design — iPod Nano, iPhone, iPad, Apple Watch, AirPods era (Jony Ive's team)
Public Release Recordings (founder) + FACE party series, San Francisco
Humanrace travel kit design (Pharrell Williams, post-LoveFrom)

nodes.name: Michos
nodes.bio: 21-year-old Polish VFX artist, 3D generalist, compositor, and editor. Recruited by Neuron Studios after building his practice through gaming video editing and the After Effects/Blender community. Served as VFX Supervisor on PinkPantheress' 'Girl Like Me' (2026), directed by Lauzza (Laurie Lotus) — a noughties-infused, Rhythm Heaven-inspired video blending real street photography with complex 3D animation. Featured on It's Nice That (June 2026) as an emerging voice bringing a Gen Z, gamer-native visual attitude to contemporary pop music videos.
nodes.cv: Neuron Studios (VFX Supervisor). Tools: After Effects, Blender. PinkPantheress 'Girl Like Me' (2026, VFX Supervisor; dir. Lauzza). Featured: It's Nice That (June 2026). Instagram: @m.ichos.

notableWork.label: PinkPantheress – Girl Like Me (VFX Supervisor, 2026)

nodes.name: Nice Groceries
nodes.bio: Nice Groceries LLC is a New York-based creative agency and production company working across film, branded content, and art direction. Known for thoughtful, visually distinct work bridging commercial and editorial sensibilities.
nodes.cv: Production company and creative agency. Projects include Patagonia 2025 Holiday Campaign (dir. Annie Woods), Wrapbook Vol.5 Art of Cinema feat. Chase Infiniti (2026), and 'echoes through stone'.

notableWork.label: Patagonia 2025 Holiday Best Gift Campaign
Wrapbook Vol.5 Art of Cinema feat. Chase Infiniti (2026)
echoes through stone

nodes.name: Daryn Ci
nodes.bio: Daryn Roongrawewan (aka Daryn Ci) is a Berlin-based graphic designer known for ornamental, vintage-influenced visual systems and editorial design. Her work draws from historical design languages and everyday observation, with a signature expressive typographic sensibility.
nodes.cv: Graphic designer specialising in visual brand identity, editorial design, type design, and zine-making. Studied at Weißensee Kunsthochschule Berlin. Featured on It's Nice That (2026).

notableWork.label: Ornamental icon / logo systems
Editorial and zine design

nodes.name: Stoemp Studio
nodes.bio: Stoëmp Studio is a Brussels and Copenhagen-based branding and creative agency founded in 2009. Tagline: 'Where brands find themselves.' Specialises in brand identity, digital, strategy, and verbal identity — with a leaner, more human approach than traditional agency models.
nodes.cv: Founded 2009. Services: branding, digital, strategy, verbal identity. Notable clients: Atomium (Brussels), Mosaert (Stromae's creative house), Le Rideau, A2M Architecture, Crystal, Resortecs, Blender.

notableWork.label: Atomium Brussels — brand identity
Mosaert (Stromae's creative house) — digital & strategy
Le Rideau 25.26 — full brand campaign

nodes.name: Instrument
nodes.bio: Instrument is a digital-first design and technology agency specialising in brand, product, and marketing. Campaign US Design Studio Agency of the Year 2026. Known for high-calibre work at the intersection of creativity and technology, with a client roster spanning culture, tech, and sport.
nodes.cv: Founded in Portland. Services: brand identity, digital product, marketing campaigns. Campaign US Design Studio of the Year 2026. Clients: Nike, Google, Netflix, Spotify, Oura, Pinterest, Patagonia, NBA, Sephora, Sonos, Stripe, Levi's, Instagram, EA, Nordstrom. Recognition: The Webbys, Awwwards, Fast Company, Shorty Awards.

notableWork.label: Nike — brand & digital campaigns
Oura — brand & product design
Google / Spotify / Netflix — select brand and marketing work

nodes.name: Gail Bichler
nodes.bio: Gail Bichler is the Creative Director of The New York Times Magazine, where she leads design, art direction, interactive content, and supplements. Widely regarded as one of the most influential editorial designers working today, she has shaped the visual language of one of the world's most-read magazines across both print and digital.
nodes.cv: Creative Director, New York Times Magazine. Named Design Team of the Year by the Art Directors Club (ADC) for seven consecutive years. Brand of the Year, Society of Publication Designers (SPD), multiple times. Appointed Design Director in 2014 during the magazine's major relaunch.

notableWork.label: New York Times Magazine — creative direction & design leadership
ADC Design Team of the Year (7 consecutive years)
SPD Brand of the Year (multiple)

nodes.name: Unit Editions
nodes.bio: Unit Editions is an independent publisher of books about graphic design and visual culture, founded in 2009 by Tony Brook, Patricia Finegan (both of studio Spin), and Adrian Shaughnessy. In 2023, became an imprint of Thames & Hudson. Produces high-quality, limited-run titles on subjects neglected by mainstream publishers — from Neville Brody and M/M (Paris) to Vaughan Oliver and Soviet design archives.
nodes.cv: Founded 2009 by Tony Brook, Patricia Finegan (Spin) & Adrian Shaughnessy. Thames & Hudson imprint since 2023. Titles include: Octavo Redux, Pentagram: Living by Design, Vaughan Oliver: Archive, VNIITE: Discovering Utopia, Letraset: The DIY Typography Revolution, Ed Fella: A Life in Images.

notableWork.label: Octavo Redux — facsimile reissue of landmark typography journal
Pentagram: Living by Design
Vaughan Oliver: Archive

nodes.name: Delali Ayivi
nodes.bio: Delali Ayivi is a Togolese-German photographer born in the US, raised in Malawi and London, working between London and Lomé. Her practice centres on the Black diaspora, Pan-African identity, and reclaiming Togolese visual history — inspired by her great-great-grandfather Alex Agbaglo Acolatse, one of Togo's first photographers. Named to the Dazed 100.
nodes.cv: Dazed 100. Vogue Germany cover (Aminata Touré, Germany's first Black minister). Atmos Vol. 9: Kinship — portrait series across Lomé, Kpalimé and Aného. Featured: CNN, It's Nice That (The Graduates 2020), Atmos. Represented by @galeriegomis.

notableWork.label: Vogue Germany — Aminata Touré cover
Atmos Vol. 9: Kinship — Togo portrait series
Dazed 100

nodes.name: Vinca Petersen
nodes.bio: Vinca Petersen is a British photographer and artist whose career spans over three decades documenting rave culture, protest, and subversive joy. Best known for her seminal archive of 1990s–2000s rave and riot photography, her work is held in major institutional collections and has been exhibited at Tate Modern, the V&A, Saatchi Gallery, Turner Contemporary, and the National Portrait Gallery. Also a filmmaker, humanitarian activist, and founder of the Future Youth Project.
nodes.cv: Published: No System (Steidl, 1999), Future Fantasy (Ditto, 2017), Deuce and a Quarter (Idea, 2018). Exhibitions: Tate Modern (2019), Saatchi Gallery Sweet Harmony (2019), V&A Dundee (2021–22), V&A New Photography Gallery (2023–25), Edel Assanti, Turner Contemporary, Martin Parr Foundation. Goethe Institute touring show: Techno Worlds / Timeline of a Raver (2022–2026). Arts Council Collection (2022). Collab: Blood Orange (2025).

notableWork.label: Raves & Riots 1990–2004 — definitive rave photography archive
A Life of Subversive Joy — 23m installation, Saatchi / V&A Dundee
No System (Steidl, 1999) — landmark rave photobook

nodes.name: Mati Diop
nodes.bio: Mati Diop is a French-Senegalese filmmaker and actress, born 1982 in Paris. The first Black woman director to compete in the Cannes Film Festival Official Competition. Her debut feature Atlantics (2019) won the Grand Prix at Cannes. Her documentary Dahomey (2024) — about the repatriation of 26 royal artefacts looted from Benin by France — won the Golden Bear at the 2024 Berlin Film Festival.
nodes.cv: Feature debut: Atlantics (2019) — Grand Prix, Cannes. Documentary: Dahomey (2024) — Golden Bear, Berlin Film Festival. First Black woman director in Cannes Competition history. Lives and works between Paris and Dakar.

notableWork.label: Atlantics (2019) — Grand Prix, Cannes Film Festival
Dahomey (2024) — Golden Bear, Berlin Film Festival

nodes.name: Ffern
nodes.bio: Ffern is an independent British organic perfume maker founded in 2019, based in Somerset and with a flagship store at 23 Beak Street, Soho. Releases four strictly limited-edition natural eaux de parfum per year at the turn of each season, sold exclusively via a members-only waiting list. Created by perfumer Elodie Durande, protégée of master perfumer François Robert. Also produces candles, tea, and limited-edition artefacts. Covered by Vogue, GQ, The Guardian, and more.
nodes.cv: Founded 2019. Membership/waiting list model — one seasonal fragrance per quarter. Flagship: 23 Beak Street, Soho, London. Perfumers: Elodie Durande & François Robert. Additional products: candles, tea, limited artefacts. Press: Vogue, GQ, The Guardian. 11,000+ verified 5-star reviews.

notableWork.label: Seasonal organic Eau de Parfum releases (Spring/Summer/Autumn/Winter)
Permanent Collection + Archive fragrances
Folk Foundation & Ffern Cinema — cultural programming

nodes.name: Flamingo Estate
nodes.bio: Flamingo Estate is an LA-based lifestyle and wellness brand founded in 2020 by Richard Christiansen — a former advertising creative director — from his 7-acre estate in the Hollywood Hills. Started during COVID by delivering produce boxes to LA's creative community to support local farmers, it has grown into a 150-product lifestyle brand spanning candles, soaps, bath & body, pantry goods, honey, and farm boxes. Tagline: 'Pleasure from the garden. We fight for elegance and imagination, with green thumbs and middle fingers.'
nodes.cv: Founded 2020 by Richard Christiansen (ex-ad agency creative director). Oprah's Favourite Things 2021 (Three Sisters Candles). 150+ product portfolio. Products: candles, soaps, olive oil, honey, bath & body, pantry, farm boxes. Also: estate membership, cookbook (A Guide to Becoming Alive). Covered by Architectural Digest, NYT Wirecutter, WWD, Vogue.

notableWork.label: Heirloom Tomato Candle — bestselling hero product
Oprah's Favourite Things 2021 — Three Sisters Candles
The Tomato Collection — signature product range

nodes.name: Heliot Emil
nodes.bio: Heliot Emil is a Copenhagen-based ready-to-wear label founded by brothers Julius Juul (creative director) and Victor Juul, named after their great-grandfather. Debuted in Milan SS17. Known for a subversive Scandinavian monochromatic aesthetic, custom-developed materials, hardware trimmings, and a blurring of form and function. Unisex collections produced in Italy, France, and Portugal.
nodes.cv: Founded by Julius & Victor Juul. Debuted Milan SS17. Custom fabrics, hardware, and trimmings developed in-house. Signature: monochrome, experimental, gorpcore-adjacent. Stocked at Farfetch, END., and leading concept stores globally.

notableWork.label: Custom liquid metal and experimental fabric collections
Hiking boots — signature crossover footwear
Astrape Capsule — SS26

nodes.name: ROA Hiking
nodes.bio: ROA is an Italian outdoor footwear and apparel brand founded in 2015 by footwear designer Maurizio Quaglia and the team behind Slam Jam, named after the Forcella della Roa trail. Sits at the intersection of fashion and performance outdoor gear — a defining force in the gorpcore movement. Known for minimalist silhouettes, traditional Italian manufacturing, and technical materials.
nodes.cv: Founded 2015, Milan. Founders: Maurizio Quaglia + Slam Jam. Products: hiking boots, performance footwear, apparel, outerwear. Stocked at END., Ssense, Dover Street Market, and top concept stores. Featured: Highsnobiety, Field Mag.

notableWork.label: Hiking boot range — gorpcore fashion staple
Cave Collection — fashion-outdoor crossover

nodes.name: Albert Elm
nodes.bio: Albert Elm is a Danish photographer born in 1990, known for his restless, documentary approach to everyday life. Studied at Fatamorgana, Copenhagen and the Glasgow School of Art (2015). Assisted Magnum photographer Jacob Aue Sobol. His work captures banality, curiosity, and the textures of daily human activity. Published in Vice, British Journal of Photography, Photoworks, and exhibited across Europe.
nodes.cv: Studied Fatamorgana + Glasgow School of Art (2015). Assisted Jacob Aue Sobol (Magnum). Books: Elsewhere (2014), What Sort of Life Is This (The Ice Plant). Published: Vice, British Journal of Photography, Photoworks. Featured: It's Nice That, Booooooom, Phases Magazine.

notableWork.label: What Sort of Life Is This — ongoing photo series & book (The Ice Plant)
Elsewhere (2014) — debut book

nodes.name: Gideon Tsang
nodes.bio: Gideon Yeeshun Tsang (b. 1974, Saskatoon, Canada) is a lens-based artist based in Austin, Texas. For 25 years he led spiritual communities and collaborative art spaces; photography became the continuation of that practice. He makes large-scale 'paintings with a camera' — images created entirely through in-camera techniques, developed through daily meditation and deep sustained attention to nature. Founder of Space 12 (2007) and Vesper (2019), both Austin-based collaborative art spaces.
nodes.cv: BA History & Political Theory, York University (1995). MA Theology, Trinity University, Chicago (1998). Founder: Space 12 (2007) & Vesper (2019), Austin. Exhibitions: Elder Gallery Houston (2024), Rapha ClubHouse Austin, Webb Gallery Trieste (2022), Because We Fall (Bowen Island + Paris, 2026). Featured: NYT (2013), It's Nice That (2026), Booooooom Array Issue 4 (2026). Published: The Radavist, Wild Sam Magazine, Garrett Leight Spectacle.

notableWork.label: Because We Fall — 2026 (Bowen Island + Paris)
Scenic Route — Rapha ClubHouse, Austin (2022)
Effloresce — Elder Gallery, Houston (2024)

nodes.name: Emily Lipson
nodes.bio: Emily Lipson is a New York and Paris-based photographer known for her tactile, 90s-inflected imagery that straddles editorial, fashion, and fine art. Her process involves printing, scanning, and layering images to create works with physical texture and depth — treating photographs as objects. Draws heavily from techno culture, nightlife, and rave aesthetics. Represented by CLM Agency (London/NY).
nodes.cv: Represented by CLM Agency. Clients: Tom Ford, Helmut Lang, New York Magazine, Revue, Altered States. Published: Vogue Italia, Dazed. Monograph: Dykes (2026, dist. Antenne Books). Featured: Dazed (2023). Goes to Nowadays, Sustain-Release.

notableWork.label: Dykes — monograph (2026, Antenne Books)
Tom Ford Holiday Campaign — fashion editorial
Helmut Lang, New York Magazine, Revue — editorial work

nodes.name: Joel Wilson
nodes.bio: Joel Wilson (@joelsp_) is a London-based designer working at the intersection of fashion, product design, and performance art. Known for bespoke object and bag design with an industrial, utilitarian sensibility — incorporating binding, hardware, climbing elements, and hand-finishing techniques. Collaborated with côte&ciel and artist Will Pegna on custom 'Sonic Bags' for the Parasound performance at Whitechapel Gallery (2025).
nodes.cv: Bag/product designer. Collab: côte&ciel x Joel Wilson 'Sonic Bags' for Will Pegna's Parasound, Whitechapel Gallery, London (2025). Works across fashion, performance art, and functional design.

notableWork.label: Sonic Bags — côte&ciel x Joel Wilson for Parasound (Whitechapel Gallery, 2025)
Parasound — Will Pegna / All Terrain Training performance series

nodes.name: Benji B
nodes.bio: Benji B (Benjamin Benstead, b. ~1978, London) is a British DJ, radio presenter, and music director. Presenter of a weekly show on BBC Radio 1 since 2010, championing hip-hop, R&B, and electronica. Appointed Music Director of Louis Vuitton by Virgil Abloh in 2018; previously consulted on music for Kanye West and Celine. Founder of London underground party series Deviation (since 2007).
nodes.cv: BBC Radio 1 presenter (2010–present); Music Director, Louis Vuitton under Virgil Abloh (2018); Music consultant, Kanye West & Celine; Founder, Deviation party series (2007–present). Active since 1998.

notableWork.label: BBC Radio 1 weekly show — tastemaking across hip-hop, R&B, and electronica since 2010
Music Director, Louis Vuitton (Virgil Abloh era, 2018)
Deviation — London underground DJ party series running since 2007

nodes.name: Acyde
nodes.bio: Acyde (Ade Odunlami) is a London-born, LA-based creative director and DJ who co-founded the roving collective No Vacancy Inn (NVI) with Tremaine Emory in 2015. NVI spans fashion, music, DJing, art direction, and ephemeral spaces worldwide. Former Nike marketing manager; has consulted for Converse, A$AP Mob, and collaborated with Kanye West, Tom Sachs, and Stüssy.
nodes.cv: Co-founder & Brand Director, No Vacancy Inn (2015–present); Marketing Manager, Nike; Consultant, Converse; Collaborator — A$AP Mob, Kanye West, Tom Sachs, Stüssy.

notableWork.label: No Vacancy Inn — roving fashion/music/art collective co-founded with Tremaine Emory (2015–present)
Collaborations with A$AP Mob, Kanye West, Tom Sachs, Stüssy
W Magazine profile; Hypebeast Magazine Issue 16; Dazed 100

nodes.name: Gilles Peterson
nodes.bio: Gilles Peterson (b. 1964, France) is a British DJ, radio broadcaster, label founder, and tastemaker whose four-decade career has spanned acid jazz, jazz, soul, hip-hop, electronica, and global sounds. Presenter of a flagship weekend show on BBC Radio 6 Music and founder of Brownswood Recordings (2006) and Worldwide FM (2016). A defining force in UK jazz's contemporary resurgence. Honorary MBE.
nodes.cv: BBC Radio 1 (1998–2012); BBC Radio 6 Music (2012–present); Co-founder, Acid Jazz Records (1990); Founder, Brownswood Recordings (2006); Founder, Worldwide FM (2016); Founder, Worldwide Festival (France); Founder, We Out Here Festival (UK). AIM Award: Indie Champion. Honorary MBE.

notableWork.label: Brownswood Recordings — label home to emerging jazz, soul, and global artists; released We Out Here (2018), Ghostpoet's Mercury-nominated debut
BBC Radio 6 Music weekend show — decades-long flagship platform for eclectic global music
Worldwide FM — online radio platform (2016–present); We Out Here Festival — UK festival championing the new jazz generation

nodes.name: Mary Anne Hobbs
nodes.bio: Mary Anne Hobbs (b. 1964, Preston) is a British DJ, broadcaster, journalist, and curator. Presented the Breezeblock on BBC Radio 1 for 14 years — one of electronic music's most influential radio shows — and is credited with breaking dubstep to a global audience. Currently hosts Weekend Breakfast on BBC Radio 6 Music. Former journalist at Sounds, NME, and co-founder of Loaded magazine.
nodes.cv: Journalist, Sounds & NME (1980s); Co-founder, Loaded magazine; BBC Radio 1 presenter (1996–2010) — Rock Show & Breezeblock; BBC Radio 6 Music Weekend Breakfast (2012–present); Grammy-nominated soundtrack contributor, Black Swan (2010); Best Radio Show — Dubstepforum & DJ Magazine Awards.

notableWork.label: The Breezeblock (BBC Radio 1) — landmark electronic music show credited with breaking dubstep globally
Dubstep showcase, Sónar Festival 2007 — curated with Skream, Oris Jay, Kode9 in front of 8,500 people
Grammy-nominated Black Swan soundtrack (2010); Women In Music Roll of Honour 2022 (Music Week)

nodes.name: Reid Kutrow
nodes.bio: Reid Kutrow is a publicist and music industry executive who joined Sacks & Co. as an intern in 2012, rising through Director and VP roles to become Co-President of SacksCo Creative — the management division for creative professionals launched by Sacks & Co. founder Carla Sacks in 2024. Based in Los Angeles, he has led PR campaigns for major acts including Florence + The Machine, The xx, and Kamasi Washington.
nodes.cv: Joined Sacks & Co. as intern (2012); Assistant to founder; Director (2013–); VP, Los Angeles; Co-President, SacksCo Creative (2024–present, with Carla Sacks). BA, Mass Communication / Journalism, UNC Chapel Hill (2013).

notableWork.label: PR campaigns for Florence + The Machine
PR campaigns for The xx
PR campaigns for Kamasi Washington

nodes.name: Eren Sarigul
nodes.bio: Eren Sarigul (b. London) is a self-taught photographer and filmmaker known for documenting his travels across Japan, Istanbul, and beyond. Raised on a working-class south London council estate, he picked up photography on an iPhone during a 2014 trip to Japan and built an international following from there. His work blends travel, lifestyle, and street photography with a strong sense of place and culture.
nodes.cv: Published, Trope (Chicago) — Tokyo & London books; Published, Mendo (Amsterdam) — Streets of London; Solo book, Across Japan (2022); Commercial clients include Microsoft, Samsung, Fujifilm, Adobe, Hilton, Oppo; Trope London won 2020 Edward Stanford Travel Book of the Year.

notableWork.label: Across Japan — solo photography book (2022)
Streets of London (Mendo) — street photography book
Tokyo (Trope) — contributing photographer

nodes.name: Andrew Tesdahl
nodes.bio: Andrew Tesdahl is a Michigan-based fine art photographer capturing the surreal beauty of overlooked places, blending cinematic influence with quiet Americana. His work explores memory, isolation, and resilience through honest, story-driven imagery of small-town and rural America. Also works commercially across weddings, portraits, and twilight real estate photography.
nodes.cv: Featured artist, Stills 2026 Design Trend Report (Americana photography); Published in Accidentally Wes Anderson: Adventures (2024); Featured in Cut+Paste Magazine and Inweekly (2022).

notableWork.label: Accidentally Wes Anderson: Adventures — contributing photographer (2024)
Stills 2026 Design Trend Report — featured Americana artist
Cut+Paste Magazine — feature

nodes.name: Gem Fletcher
nodes.bio: Gem Fletcher is a writer, photo director, consultant, and podcaster whose work explores photography, art, and contemporary culture and how they shape who we are. She hosts The Messy Truth, a podcast featuring candid conversations with leading and emerging photographers, curators, and critics, launched in 2018 with a mission to dismantle gatekeeping in the photography industry.
nodes.cv: Host & creator, The Messy Truth podcast (2018–present); Published writer for Aperture, Foam, Dazed, It's Nice That, Creative Review, 1000 Words, and The British Journal of Photography; Photo Director; Hosted 100th-episode salon with the International Center of Photography, New York.

notableWork.label: The Messy Truth — Conversations on Photography (podcast, 2018–present, 100+ episodes)
Writing for Aperture, Foam, and The British Journal of Photography
100th episode salon collaboration with the International Center of Photography (ICP), New York

nodes.name: Chose Commune
nodes.bio: Chose Commune is a French independent publishing house based in Marseille, founded in 2014 by French/Japanese editor Cécile Poimbœuf-Koizumi. Driven by a desire to discover and rediscover lesser-known voices on the international scene, it curates, designs, edits, and publishes artists' books across photography, works on paper, and ceramics — each title crafted in close collaboration with the artist.
nodes.cv: Founded by Cécile Poimbœuf-Koizumi (2014–present); Independent art-book publisher specializing in photography monographs and limited objects; Strong reputation within the international independent photobook community.

notableWork.label: Coco Capitán — If You've Seen It All Close Your Eyes
Raymond Meeks — Halfstory Halflife
Antoine Yogananthan — Dandaka

nodes.name: Shelley Horan
nodes.bio: Shelley Horan is a Melbourne-based commercial and fine art photographer specialising in still life. Her practice, sparked during 2020 lockdown, transforms everyday and thrifted objects — bandaids, candle wicks, peppers, toothpaste — into ethereal, unfamiliar compositions through meticulous styling, arrangement, and lighting. She works across both commercial campaigns and personal fine art projects.
nodes.cv: Featured, It's Nice That (Oct 2025) — 'Shelley Horan's otherworldly shots of everyday objects are a spectacle in styling'; Represented by BOTH Studio; Commercial still life work for brand and creative-team campaigns alongside ongoing personal practice since 2020.

notableWork.label: Still Life series — ongoing personal practice (2020–present), turning thrifted/found objects into surreal compositions
Feels Like — personal project
Compositions — personal project

nodes.name: Femi Adeyemi
nodes.bio: Femi Adeyemi is the London-based founder of NTS Radio, the globally influential independent online radio station he launched in 2011 on a £5,000 budget after being made redundant. Growing out of his Nuts To Soup music blog, NTS began broadcasting from a hut in Dalston's Gillett Square and has since become a 24/7 global institution with 500+ hosts and DJs, championing musical diversity against homogenous mainstream and pirate radio. He also had a hand in the early formation of Boiler Room.
nodes.cv: Founder & CCO, NTS Radio (2011–present); Co-formation involvement, Boiler Room; AIM Independent Music Awards — 'Indie Champion' (2018); Pioneer of the modern independent/online radio movement, inspired by pirate radio, MTV2, WFMU, and Plastic People's creative community.

notableWork.label: NTS Radio — global 24/7 independent station with 500+ hosts (2011–present)
AIM Independent Music Awards — Indie Champion (2018)
Featured: Mixmag oral history, SSENSE editorial, Something Curated profile (2025)

nodes.name: Francois Vaxelaire
nodes.bio: Francois Vaxelaire is the Brussels-born founder of The Lot Radio, the influential non-profit online radio station broadcasting from a shipping container in Greenpoint/Williamsburg, Brooklyn. After studying sociology in Brussels and photography/video in Mozambique, then media studies at The New School in NYC, he spent a year securing permits to convert a long-abandoned fenced lot into a radio station, launching it on 7 February 2016. The station is sustained by its on-site café and now operates alongside co-operator Pauline Le Mell as a major hub for underground electronic and global music.
nodes.cv: Founder, The Lot Radio (2016–present); Background in sociology (Brussels) and photography/video (Mozambique); Master's in Media Studies, The New School, NYC; Brooklyn 100 Influencer (BKMAG, 2017); Speaker, New York Media Festival (NYME).

notableWork.label: The Lot Radio — launch broadcast feat. Discwoman, Matt FX, Darker Than Wax (Feb 7, 2016)
The Lot Radio — 10 years of independent broadcasting from a Williamsburg shipping container
Featured: Hypebeast, MusicTech, 6sqft, BKMAG, Wikipedia

nodes.name: Selina Kehuan Wu
nodes.bio: Selina Kehuan Wu is a graphic designer, typographer, and image-maker from Nanjing, China, now based in New York City, where she works as a designer at the studio Collins. Her practice navigates personal and cultural history, most notably in her photobook Dancing In Utopia (2022), which documents public square dancing among elderly Chinese communities as a lens onto grassroots culture, politics, and memory.
nodes.cv: Designer, Collins (current); Dancing In Utopia (2022) — TDC Certificate of Typographic Excellence (2023); Young Ones ADC 2023 — Publication Design award & Capstone Project award; Featured, It's Nice That (Sept 2025); Creative Quarterly journal feature.

notableWork.label: Dancing In Utopia — photobook documenting Chinese public square dancing culture (2022)
Music Revisualization — personal design project
TDC Certificate of Typographic Excellence (2023); Young Ones ADC Award (2023)

nodes.name: Flo Dill
nodes.bio: Flo Dill is a London-born and based DJ and broadcaster who has worked in and around music throughout her career — running club nights, programming radio stations, interviewing musicians, music-supervising venues and soundtracks, and releasing cult compilations. She hosts the NTS Breakfast Show, the station's flagship and most-listened-to programme, alongside World In Flo Motion. Known for boundless musical knowledge and an open, unpretentious approach spanning post-punk, disco, and 80s new wave.
nodes.cv: Host, NTS Breakfast Show (since 2016) — NTS Radio's flagship programme; Host, World In Flo Motion (NTS); Host, Digging — music & culture podcast (NTS); Writes a regular culture newsletter; Represented for DJ bookings by The Pool Artists (London).

notableWork.label: NTS Breakfast Show — flagship NTS programme since 2016
Digging — music and culture podcast (NTS)
Perfect Motion — compilation release (Discogs)

nodes.name: Sophie Green
nodes.bio: Sophie Green (b. 1991) is a British documentary photographer who has spent over a decade exploring the margins of British culture and subculture — from Aladura Spiritualist churches to banger racing and Traveller communities. Trained in Fashion Photography at London College of Fashion, she merges documentary realism with bold colour and stylised composition, often building long-term relationships with the communities she photographs over multiple years.
nodes.cv: Three published books: Dented Pride (2016), Congregation (2019), Tangerine Dreams (2025); Exhibited at the National Portrait Gallery, the V&A, and Somerset House; Winner, Magnum Photos/Photo London Graduate Photographers Award; Winner, Creative Review Zeitgeist Award; Nominated, Taylor Wessing Photographic Portrait Prize & Deutsche Börse Photography Prize; Editorial work for The New York Times, Financial Times, The Guardian, and Vogue.

notableWork.label: Tangerine Dreams — photobook (2025)
Congregation — photobook documenting Aladura Spiritualist churches (2019)
Dented Pride — photobook on banger racing culture (2016)

nodes.name: Kayla Dantz
nodes.bio: Kayla Dantz is a photo-textile artist and designer from Massachusetts whose practice blends weaving, knitting, and embroidery with photography, drawing on a background in fashion design and her education at Parsons School of Design. She reassembles archival and personal photographs — often vintage family images from the 1960s and '70s — into handwoven textile works that explore how time distorts and preserves memory and identity.
nodes.cv: Featured, It's Nice That (Aug 2025) — 'Kayla Dantz's woven photographs hold up a mirror to the fragmented nature of memory'; Exhibited: Affordable Art Fair NY with Harsh Collective (March 2026); EMERGE IX, Bond Millen Gallery, Richmond VA (2025); Soft Archives, IA & A at Hillyer, Washington DC (2025); Parsons School of Design alum.

notableWork.label: Woven photo-textile collections — original handwoven photographic works
Soft Archives exhibition, IA & A at Hillyer, Washington D.C. (2025)
Featured in Elle Australia — 'Mail Clubs Are Bringing Joy Back To Checking The Letterbox'

nodes.name: Craic Magazine
nodes.bio: Craic Magazine is a DIY, image-led print and online zine exploring heritage, identity, and culture in Northern Ireland, founded in April 2022 by James Robinson. Borrowing its name from the Irish slang for 'what's the craic?', the publication takes an anarchistic, tongue-in-cheek approach to documenting the 'ceasefire generation' — envisioned by Robinson as an i-D or Dazed for post-Good Friday Agreement Northern Ireland, championing queer voices and the marginalised and outer-city kids.
nodes.cv: Founded by James Robinson (2022–present); Editor, image-maker, stylist, and art director; Collections held by the National Library of Ireland (Northern Ireland Photography collection); Acquired by the French National Museum of Modern Art (2024); Featured, It's Nice That (July 2025).

notableWork.label: Coming & Going — issue exploring leaving and returning to Northern Ireland (40-page issue)
What's the Craic — debut issue, Issue One
Acquisition by the French National Museum of Modern Art (2024)

nodes.name: Pelle Cass
nodes.bio: Pelle Cass (b. 1954) is an American fine art photographer best known for his 'Crowded Fields' series — meticulously composited sports and street photographs that compress hundreds of moments from a single fixed vantage point into one frenetic, all-at-once image. Raised by a painter/sculptor mother, he received his first camera around age 12 and studied across four undergraduate art schools.
nodes.cv: Twice named a Critical Mass Top 50 photographer; Two Yaddo fellowships, one Berkshire Taconic Foundation fellowship; Massachusetts Cultural Council finalist; Artist's Resource Trust grant; Work held in the Fogg Art Museum, Addison Gallery of American Art, Polaroid Collection, DeCordova Museum, Peabody Essex Museum, and MFA Houston; Exhibited at George Eastman House, Albright-Knox Gallery, and the Metamorf Biennial (Norway).

notableWork.label: Crowded Fields (2017–present) — composited sports/street photography series
Selected People: City / Parks / Uncrowded (2008–present)
Published in Wired, GQ, Bloomberg Businessweek, The Atlantic, The Washington Post, and the photobook Photoviz (Gestalten)

nodes.name: Free Practice
nodes.bio: Free Practice is a London-based, ideas-focused, interdisciplinary creative studio rooted in global contemporary culture, co-founded by Adrián (ex-Nike EMEA Brand Creative Art Director; previously Browns, dn&co, Pentagram, Wolff Olins) and Daniel Whiteneck (Co-Founder & ECD, 11 years across Nike Global/NA/EMEA). The studio works across strategy, creative direction, art direction, experience design, and branding — primarily for Nike, alongside clients like The North Face and municipal/cultural commissions.
nodes.cv: Co-founded by Adrián & Daniel Whiteneck; Core client: Nike (Global & EMEA Brand Creative); Additional clients include The North Face and Ayuntamiento de Casares; Output spans experiential activations, national team kit campaigns, and brand strategy.

notableWork.label: TOMA Casablanca — invite-only street football experience for Nike, on the eve of AFCON 2025 (spatial design, art direction, local artist curation)
Steal the Show — Nike SPSU26 National Team Kit campaigns (FA, KNVB, NFF)
Fear Nothing — Nike EC25 National Team Kit creative concept and graphic system

nodes.name: Pentagram

location.raw: London, UK (offices: NYC, San Francisco, Berlin, Austin)

nodes.bio: Pentagram is the world's largest independent design consultancy, founded in 1972 in Notting Hill, London by Alan Fletcher, Theo Crosby, Colin Forbes, Kenneth Grange, and Mervyn Kurlansky — its name taken from the five founding partners. Known for an unusual flat-partnership structure where each designer-owner runs their own team and clients while sharing centralized resources. Operates across graphic design, branding, product design, architecture, and digital across its five global studios.
nodes.cv: Founded 1972 (evolved from Fletcher/Forbes/Gill, 1962, and Crosby/Fletcher/Forbes, 1965); Notable partners have included Paula Scher, Michael Bierut, Woody Pirtle, Peter Harrison, and Emily Oberman; Celebrated 50th anniversary in 2022/2023; Offices in London, New York, San Francisco, Berlin, and Austin.

notableWork.label: Citibank rebrand and identity (Paula Scher)
Saks Fifth Avenue identity system (Michael Bierut)
Windows 8 brand identity (Paula Scher)

nodes.name: Helions
nodes.bio: Helions is a photography-focused independent publishing house founded by photographer Jack Davison, named after Helions Bumpstead, the village where he grew up. Self-described as 'an attempt to publish work directly,' it releases books, posters, newspapers, and special-edition prints — run on a small, intentionally personal scale, shipping twice a month. Brand identity designed by Matt Willey and team at Pentagram, featuring a refracted-light wordmark and a high-impact red and black system.
nodes.cv: Founded by photographer Jack Davison; Brand identity by Matt Willey / Pentagram (2025); Featured, It's Nice That (May 2025) — 'Matt Willey's Helions brand has a tender intensity in favour of tone rather than concept'; Collaborators include Ross Morrell (riso posters) and Bottega Veneta (Craft is our Language).

notableWork.label: A is for Ant — debut book release, companion to Davison's first short film
13-15 November. Portraits: London — photobook and print series
Bottega Veneta — Craft is our Language collaboration

nodes.name: Tais Sirote

location.raw: London, UK (b. Argentina, raised in Tenerife)

nodes.bio: Tais Sirote (aka Taisido) is a London-based photographer and director specialising in still life and product photography, known for a hyperreal aesthetic often mistaken for CGI. Born in Argentina and raised in Tenerife, she began photography at age 10, taught by her older brother. Her bold, highly controlled images blend elements of science and technology, working across cosmetics, jewellery, and fashion accessories, with client work for Apple, Prada, and Vogue. Currently expanding her practice toward portraiture.
nodes.cv: Featured, It's Nice That (April 2025) — 'Tais Sirote's surreal photos blur the line between real and rendered'; Commercial clients include Apple, Prada, and Vogue; Represented for still life by W. Schupfer Artists; Photographer & Art Director profile on Behance.

notableWork.label: Apple — commercial still life campaign
Prada — product photography campaign
Vogue — editorial still life work

nodes.name: Studio Dumbar
nodes.bio: Studio Dumbar is a Dutch design agency founded in 1977 in The Hague by Gert Dumbar, now operating as Studio Dumbar/DEPT and based in Rotterdam since 2003. Specialising in bespoke brand identity, visual design, motion design, sound, and creative coding, it has built one of the most respected reputations in European graphic design across institutional, cultural, sport, and tech clients.
nodes.cv: Founded 1977 by Gert Dumbar; Creative direction passed to Michel de Boer (2003) and Liza Enebeis (2011); Acquired by digital agency Dept (2016); Major identity work includes the Dutch National Police (1993) and Dutch national rail (NS); Long-running collaboration with OpenAI on brand identity and motion.

notableWork.label: OpenAI — ongoing brand identity and motion design collaboration
Dutch National Police — brand identity and livery system (1993)
Utah Jazz (NBA) — brand-in-motion identity across multiple seasons

nodes.name: Geoffrey Lillemon
nodes.bio: Geoffrey Lillemon is an Amsterdam-based artist and pioneer in CGI and media art, working across both commercial and fine art practice for over 20 years. A cult figure in Amsterdam's digital art scene, he co-founded The Department of New Realities with Wieden+Kennedy Amsterdam in 2017, later working in physical space design and interactive architecture with Random Studio, while teaching at the Royal Academy of Art in Den Haag.
nodes.cv: Co-founder, The Department of New Realities (with W+K Amsterdam, 2017); Collaborator, Random Studio; Educator, Royal Academy of Art, Den Haag; Work acquired by the Stedelijk Museum; Exhibited at Boijmans van Beuningen, Museo Tamayo (Mexico City), and Centre Pompidou (Paris); Named one of Adweek's Most Fascinating Creatives (2018, Cannes) and Creative Review's 50 Creative Leaders to Watch (2018).

notableWork.label: Collaborations with Beyoncé, Coldplay, Lady Gaga, Miley Cyrus, Nicki Minaj, Snoop Dogg, and Travis Scott
Brand work for adidas, Chanel, Converse, Diesel, Rick Owens, Gucci, and Versace
The Department of New Realities — creative technology unit co-founded with Wieden+Kennedy Amsterdam (2017)

nodes.name: Festival of the Sun
nodes.bio: Festival of the Sun (FOTSUN) is an annual boutique beachside music festival held each December at the Sundowner Breakwall Tourist Park in Port Macquarie, NSW. Billed as the only BYO festival in New South Wales, it combines camping, food trucks, and secret shows with a lineup historically spanning Australian indie, alt-pop, and hip-hop acts.
nodes.cv: Annual outdoor festival, held each December in Port Macquarie (Birpai Country), NSW; BYO and camping festival format; Past lineups have included Meg Mac, Ball Park Music, Holy Holy, Sampa the Great, and The Preatures.

notableWork.label: Annual December lineup — Australian indie, alt-pop, and hip-hop acts
Secret/surprise sets — a recurring festival feature

nodes.name: magCulture
nodes.bio: magCulture is a London institution dedicated to independent magazine culture, founded by Jeremy Leslie. What began as a 2003 book and 2006 blog grew into a physical shop in Clerkenwell, an online store, a design studio, a podcast, and the annual magCulture Live conference (London & New York) — together forming one of the most influential hubs for independent print publishing worldwide.
nodes.cv: Founded by Jeremy Leslie, 40-year veteran of magazine design (ex-Blitz, Time Out, John Brown Publishing); Author of The Modern Magazine and Independence; Winner, Mark Boxer Award, British Society of Magazine Editors (2018); Runs magCulture Studio (design consultancy) and magCulture Live conference; Featured in The New York Times, FT How to Spend It, and Eye magazine.

notableWork.label: magCulture Live — annual independent magazine conference (London & New York)
The Modern Magazine (2013) — book by Jeremy Leslie surveying contemporary magazine design
magCulture Shop — Clerkenwell, London, stocking 100+ international independent titles

nodes.name: MacGuffin Magazine
nodes.bio: MacGuffin is a biannual design and crafts magazine founded in 2015 by Kirsten Algera and Ernst van der Hoeven, exploring extraordinary stories about the life of ordinary things. Each edition is built around a single object — a bed, a ladder, a stitch — investigating its personal, cultural, and historical dimensions through essays, photography, and design. Named after Hitchcock's narrative device, the magazine treats objects as plot drivers rather than aesthetic subjects.
nodes.cv: Founded 2015 by design historian Kirsten Algera and architecture historian Ernst van der Hoeven; 15 issues published as of 2025; media partnerships with Hermès, Design Miami, and Maharam; published in English, distributed globally.

notableWork.label: No. 15 – The Stitch (2025) — 10th anniversary edition featuring contributions from Kader Attia, Rachel Dedman, Scheltens & Abbenes, and others
No. 12 – The Zipper; No. 9 – The Ladder; No. 1 – The Bed — single-object editorial format spanning architecture, craft, and anthropology
MacGuffin Podcast — companion audio series extending each object's story with interviews and essays

nodes.name: Nepenthes
nodes.bio: Nepenthes is a Japanese select shop and brand ecosystem founded in 1988 by Keizo Shimizu, operating as a retail platform housing several in-house labels including Engineered Garments, Needles, South2 West8, and Workaday. With locations in Tokyo, New York, Los Angeles, and London, Nepenthes is a cornerstone of Japanese-inflected workwear, military, and outdoor-inspired menswear. Its New York outpost in the Garment District opened in 2010 and has become a destination for serious menswear enthusiasts worldwide.
nodes.cv: Founded 1988 Tokyo by Keizo Shimizu; NY location opened 2010 at 307 W 38th St; LA location added 2021; home to Daiki Suzuki's Engineered Garments and Kiro Hirata's Needles; London outpost via nepentheslondon.com.

notableWork.label: Engineered Garments (Daiki Suzuki) — workwear-influenced American utility clothing designed in NYC
Needles (Kiro Hirata) — Japanese label known for rebuild/reconstruct track pants, butterfly embroidery, and craft-forward tailoring
South2 West8 — outdoor and fishing-inspired Japanese label stocked exclusively through Nepenthes

nodes.name: Departamento
nodes.bio: Departamento (DPTO) is a luxury multibrand concept store in Los Angeles's Arts District, founded by Andrew, Dryden, and Andres Quinones. Housed at 821 Traction Ave with entry through Concierge Coffee, the store is known for its speakeasy-style entrance and tightly curated selection of cutting-edge contemporary menswear. A second space, DPTO Annex, operates through Maru Coffee on S Santa Fe Ave.
nodes.cv: Founded by the Quinones family (Andrew, Dryden, Andres); originally operated as a hidden showroom at the back of Maru Coffee before relocating to a new Arts District space designed by 22RE design studio; known for introducing niche international labels to the LA market.

notableWork.label: DPTO Arts District — 821 Traction Ave, entry through Concierge Coffee, featuring global avant-garde and luxury menswear brands
DPTO Annex — 1019 S Santa Fe Ave, entry through Maru Coffee, secondary retail and showroom space

nodes.name: Buffalo Zine
nodes.bio: Buffalo Zine is a cult biannual fashion and culture magazine founded in 2010 by Adrián González-Cohen and David Uzquiza, based in London. Each issue takes a radically different form — a cookbook, a mail-order catalogue, a children's book, an interiors magazine — reinventing the medium to test what fashion can hold and how it can be seen. Known for rejecting celebrity and spectacle, the magazine focuses on people who live fashion rather than perform it: presence over profile.
nodes.cv: Founded 2010 by Adrián González-Cohen and David Uzquiza; 22+ issues published as of 2026; multiple D&AD Pencils per issue; Stack Awards Magazine of the Year 2017; Indie Fashion Magazine of the Decade 2019 (72.9% of votes); praised by NYT, i-D, Dazed, Monocle, Financial Times, and magCulture; published from Studio 315, 130–150 Hackney Road, London E2.

notableWork.label: D&AD Yellow Pencil (Issue 9, 2020) and Graphite Pencils for Issues 7, 8, 10, 12 — consistently among the most awarded magazine designs globally
Stack Awards Magazine of the Year 2017 and Indie Fashion Magazine of the Decade 2019 — beat 400+ independent titles
Issue 22 SS'26 — latest biannual issue; past formats include an unfinished issue, a fake celebrity cover issue, a children's book, and a mail-order catalogue

nodes.name: Liver Studio
nodes.bio: Liver is a Milan-based creative studio working across visual identity, photography, graphic design, and image manipulation. The studio offers rebranding and communication services, developing cohesive visual languages for fashion, beauty, and lifestyle clients. Their output spans still-life product photography, editorial imagery, and brand identity systems.
nodes.cv: Milan-based studio active in fashion and beauty brand communication; portfolio includes work for Clarins; known for a precise, image-led approach to visual identity and content creation.

notableWork.label: Clarins — product photography and visual content for the French beauty brand

nodes.name: Perrin Drumm
nodes.bio: Perrin Drumm is a Brooklyn-based writer, editor, and publisher currently serving as Head of Publishing at A24 Films, where she oversees the studio's book imprint — publishing screenplay collections, novelizations, anthologies, and titles tied to A24 releases. She previously founded and directed AIGA's Eye on Design in 2014, growing it from a blog into a full-scale design journalism platform with a print magazine, annual conference, and global community. Her writing has appeared in New York Magazine, The Paris Review Daily, GQ, Frieze, and Artforum.
nodes.cv: Founded Eye on Design (AIGA) 2014, directed for ~6 years; co-founded food and design platform MOLD; senior editorial roles at Condé Nast Details, Adobe, The Architect's Newspaper, and Sundance Channel; Head of Publishing at A24 Films; MFA fiction, Vermont College of Fine Arts; BA creative writing + fine art, College of Santa Fe.

notableWork.label: Eye on Design (AIGA, 2014–) — grew a design blog into a definitive platform with print magazine, annual conference, and global community of designers
A24 Books — overseeing screenplay collections and books tied to Everything Everywhere All at Once, Priscilla, The Whale, and other A24 titles
MOLD — co-founded food and design publication exploring the intersection of design, science, and culture

nodes.name: Mike Meiré
nodes.bio: Mike Meiré is a Cologne-based creative director, designer, and artist, co-founder of Meiré und Meiré — the influential agency he has run with his brother Marc since 1987. Working at the nexus of culture and design, he has shaped the visual identities of cutting-edge magazines and major brands alike, blending intellectual rigor with deliberate provocation. Known for introducing subversive typography, productive mistakes, and conceptual anarchism into high-profile brand communication, he is widely regarded as one of the defining figures of German graphic culture.
nodes.cv: Co-founded Meiré und Meiré with brother Marc 1987; long-term art director for 032c, Garage, brand eins, ARCH+, Kid's Wear, NZZ, Kunstforum; clients include BMW, MINI, Telekom, Rimowa, Courrèges; founded cultural production company NEO NOTO 2001; Visual Lead Award Germany 2006; honorary member German Designer Club (DDC) 2015; maintains separate art practice across Cologne's Factory and Atelier studios.

notableWork.label: 032c Magazine — defining art direction since early 2000s, including stretched fonts, inverted logos, and subversive typography that pioneered the "new ugly" aesthetic
Garage Magazine — developed randomized multi-font identity and co-created the first augmented reality app in print, collaborating with Jeff Koons, Cara Delevingne, Beats by Dre, and Marvel

BMW / Telekom / brand eins — brand identity and communication work bridging corporate design with cultural commentary; Bauhaus centenary sweatshirt design and Dilettant Monoblock chair among solo art objects

nodes.name: Tim Giesen
nodes.bio: Tim Giesen is a Cologne-based graphic and editorial designer, Creative Director at Meiré und Meiré and founder of Newflower. Working closely with Mike Meiré, he has been central to defining the visual language of 032c Magazine across multiple issues and has contributed editorial design to a wide range of German and international print publications. His practice sits at the intersection of high-concept editorial design and brand communication.
nodes.cv: Creative Director at Meiré und Meiré, Cologne; founder of Newflower; designer on 032c issues including #42 "Drain Gang" (2022) and #48 (Winter 2025/26); editorial work across ARCH+, brand eins, Interview Magazine Germany, Garage, Focus Style; BA Communication Design, University of Portsmouth.

notableWork.label: 032c Magazine — key designer on the publication working alongside art director Mike Meiré; credited on issues #42 "Drain Gang" (2022) and #48 (Winter 2025/26) among others
brand eins / ARCH+ / Interview Magazine Germany — editorial design spanning major German print publications
Newflower — independent studio project alongside his work at Meiré und Meiré

nodes.name: Martha
nodes.bio: Martha is a Montréal-based design studio formed as a collective of designers united by shared human and aesthetic values — described on its own site as not a person or a place, but the union of designers who came together to form a new entity. Working across brand identity, packaging, campaign design, and creative direction, the studio brings an authentic, distinctive perspective to projects spanning arts institutions, food and beverage, music, and fashion.
nodes.cv: Design, Strategy, Etc.; clients include Musée d'art contemporain de Montréal (Printemps du MAC), Festival transAmériques, Les Louanges, Jesse Mac Cormack, Sheertex, Alvéole, Bioitalia, Proxies, Ada, FOIL, Greenpeace; contact: info@frommartha.com.

notableWork.label: Printemps du MAC 2025 & 2026 — campaign identity for Montréal's Musée d'art contemporain seasonal programming
Les Louanges — art direction and album design for the acclaimed Québec indie artist
Festival transAmériques — campaign design for the major Montréal contemporary performing arts festival

nodes.name: Malvah
nodes.bio: Malvah is a Cape Town-based branding and digital design studio founded in 2018 by co-founders Ty Pearce and Tim Evans, working across brand identity, digital design, UI/UX, web development, and motion. Described as a studio in a continuous state of creative evolution, Malvah connects brands to people through culture and craft. With a global client base served from their Loop Street studio, they have accumulated 46+ awards including multiple Awwwards Sites of the Day and FWA recognitions.
nodes.cv: Founded 2018, 33 Loop Street, Cape Town; co-founders Ty Pearce and Tim Evans (Design Directors), Olive Hodge (Managing Director); 46+ awards including Awwwards SOTD, FWA of the Day, CSSDA; clients include M&C Saatchi Abel, Fold7, Rupert & Rothschild, Stewart & Partners, Pantheone Audio, Isoflow, Spot Money, Natroceutics, Sunya Collective.

notableWork.label: Fold7 — FWA of the Day + Awwwards Site of the Day + Developer Award 2023
Rupert & Rothschild — Awwwards Site of the Day + CSSDA Website of the Day + Mobile Excellence 2021; wine brand digital identity
M&C Saatchi Abel — Awwwards Site of the Day 2023; M&C Saatchi Group Mobile Excellence 2020

nodes.name: Rob Farmer
nodes.bio: Rob Farmer is a Melbourne-based graphic designer and illustrator working across brand, illustration, motion, and generative design. Best known for his Data Blooms series — kinetic typographic animations built in Cavalry software that recreate patterns from the natural world using type and simple graphic forms — his personal practice spans generative graphics, cross-stitch motion studies, and algorithmic compositions. Commercial commissions include campaigns for Google and Coinbase, and illustration for The Economist.
nodes.cv: Graphic designer based in Melbourne (previously New York); generative design practice in Cavalry software; commissions for Google, Coinbase (via Isle of Any), The Economist; featured in It's Nice That October 2025; currently at a brand design studio in Melbourne.

notableWork.label: Data Blooms (2025) — generative typographic animation series using Cavalry, depicting natural world patterns (blooms, jellyfish, ants, waves) through kinetic type and simple graphic forms
Coinbase 'System Update' campaign (2025, via Isle of Any) — motion contribution to the global brand campaign
The Economist — illustration commissions; also personal series: Cross Stitch, Glitch Garden, Digi-Waves, Ant Picnic

nodes.name: Vasilis Marmatakis
nodes.bio: Vasilis Marmatakis is an Athens-based graphic designer known for his striking poster work for Yorgos Lanthimos films and for a wide range of cultural institutions. His visual language — typographically precise, often unsettling — has become closely associated with Lanthimos's cinematic universe. Commercially represented by Big Active in London.
nodes.cv: Athens-based graphic designer; long-running collaboration with Yorgos Lanthimos on film posters and titles; commissions for DESTE Foundation, National Greek Theatre, Sternberg Press, Acropolis Museum, Oliver Sim, Kim Deal, Monheim Triennale; represented by Big Active (London); contact via info@marmatakis.net or hello@bigactive.com.

notableWork.label: Yorgos Lanthimos film posters — The Lobster, The Killing of a Sacred Deer, The Favourite, Poor Things, Kinds of Kindness, Bugonia; defining visual identity for the director's international releases
DESTE Foundation / National Greek Theatre / Acropolis Museum — cultural institution print and identity work across Greece's leading arts organisations
Oliver Sim vinyl artwork; Kim Deal (Big Ben Beat) poster; Sternberg Press publications; Monheim Triennale identity

nodes.name: Yoffdog
