nodes.bio: Yoffdog is a Staffordshire-based graphic designer whose work lives at the intersection of typography, texture, and youth culture. Using both digital and analogue techniques to "bitcrush" visual worlds together, his output spans cover art redesigns, campaign posters, and brand graphics driven by personal passions — music, film, clothing, and TV. His playfully jagged, maximalist style draws on the chaos of things he loves rather than formal design influences.
nodes.cv: Freelance graphic designer; campaign designs for Supreme, Nike, Palace, and Marc Jacobs' Heaven; cover art and poster work spanning Blur, Clairo, Oklou, Imogen Heap, Frou Frou, The Prodigy; featured in It's Nice That September 2025; open for commissions via dogyoff@gmail.com; active on Behance as yoffdog0.

notableWork.label: Campaign graphics for Supreme (Supreme x Aphex Twin, Supreme x Dr. Martens, Supreme x Transformers), Nike, Palace, and Marc Jacobs' Heaven — applied his textural maximalism to cult brand contexts
Cover art redesigns — The Prodigy Fat of the Land, Imogen Heap Speak for Yourself, Oklou Choke Enough, Blur, Clairo, Frou Frou — personal series reimagining records he loves
Tour poster concepts for Re6ce and Niko B; Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind poster; The Office Season One poster; Mighty Morphin Power Rangers graphic

nodes.name: Studio Nari
nodes.bio: Studio Nari is a London-based branding and design studio founded by Caterina Bianchini, designing brands as cultural systems people choose to belong to. The studio's name derives from 'Not Always Right Ideas' — celebrating a humanist, artistic approach to brand making. Relaunched from Caterina Bianchini Studio in 2020 to reflect a collaborative rather than singular practice, Nari works across identity, experience, and expression for culture-led brands.
nodes.cv: Founded by Caterina Bianchini (founder & creative director), working alongside Joe Osborne; 75K+ Instagram following; clients include Netflix, Nike, Apple Music, Puma, Lululemon, Mulberry, Tenzing, Field Day, Threads (Meta), Desmond & Dempsey, V&A, YouTube, Charli XCX, D&AD New Blood; featured in It's Nice That and Design Week.

notableWork.label: Netflix — brand identity, fandom reactive campaign, and Netflix House experiential project
Nike — Nike Kids, Nike By You, Nike x JD Sports SU24, plus Lululemon and Puma culture-led campaigns
Mulberry, Tenzing, Field Day, Apple Music, V&A, Threads (Meta), Charli XCX, Rafa Nadal — wide-ranging culture and lifestyle brand work

nodes.name: FOOD Arts & Technology
nodes.bio: FOOD is a transatlantic creative studio co-founded in 2022 by Iain Tait, Nick Farnhill, and Richard Turley — the same trio who previously co-founded digital agency Poke. Working at the intersection of arts, technology, and culture, FOOD explores the opportunities and challenges of the modern world through publications, brand work, and experimental creative projects. Contact: cook@food.xyz.
nodes.cv: Co-founded 2022 by Iain Tait, Nick Farnhill (previously Poke), and Richard Turley; London and New York offices; published NUTS fashion publication (Gold Lovie Award); Global AR Art Project with JR Studio, Niantic, and Nexus; Mulberry Softie NYC Launch campaign; Heavy Traffic Design project.

notableWork.label: NUTS — FOOD fashion publication exploring the meaning behind everyday clothes and personal style; Gold Lovie Award winner for editorial and visual excellence
Global AR Art Project — collaboration with JR Studio, Niantic, and Nexus Studios; large-scale augmented reality art experience
Mulberry Softie NYC Launch — integrated campaign for the British luxury brand's New York push

nodes.name: Richard Turley
nodes.bio: Richard Turley is a New York-based English creative director and graphic designer, one of the most influential editorial designers of the past two decades. Best known for his transformative work at Bloomberg Businessweek (2011–2014) and MTV (2014–2016), his signature text-on-image aesthetic became widely referenced across design culture. He subsequently co-founded Civilization magazine and FOOD Arts & Technology, and served as editorial director of Interview magazine.
nodes.cv: Senior designer at The Guardian under Mark Porter (2003–2005); Creative Director Bloomberg Businessweek (2011–2014); SVP Visual Storytelling MTV (2014–2016); Executive Creative Director Wieden+Kennedy (2016+); co-founded Civilization magazine with Mia Kerin and Lucas Mascatello (2018); Editorial Director Interview magazine (2018); co-founded FOOD Arts & Technology with Iain Tait and Nick Farnhill; 35 D&AD awards; worked with Rosalía, Calvin Klein, Formula 1, Guess.

notableWork.label: Bloomberg Businessweek — transformed the magazine into one of the most visually bold and culturally talked-about publications in American media during his 2011–2014 tenure
Civilization magazine — co-founded anarchic NYC broadsheet with Mia Kerin and Lucas Mascatello (2018–); Issue 7 published 2025; also served as Editorial Director of Interview magazine
Rosalía LUX album visual identity (2025); FOOD Arts & Technology co-founder; commercial work for Calvin Klein, Formula 1, and Guess

nodes.name: Frank DeRose
nodes.bio: Frank DeRose is a New York-based graphic designer and co-founder of Zut Alors!, a studio he launched in 2008 with partner Brendan Griffiths. Equally committed to independent studio practice and institutional client work, he has also worked as a designer at Wieden+Kennedy New York, where his projects appeared in the WKNY Dossier alongside collaborators including Richard Turley. He teaches Visual Communications, Typography, and Web Design at Pratt Institute and Interaction Design at Parsons The New School for Design.
nodes.cv: Co-founder, Zut Alors! (2008–present) with Brendan Griffiths; Designer, Wieden+Kennedy New York (WKNY Design Dept.); editorial illustration clients include Bloomberg View, New York Times, Time Magazine, New York Observer, Print Magazine; brand clients include Christie's, Condé Nast, Creative Time, Performa, Socrates Sculpture Park, The Suzanne Geiss Company, Alexander Gorlin Architects, VH1, Bon Appétit; former work at Karlssonwilker; teaches at Pratt Institute and Parsons.

notableWork.label: Zut Alors! — NYC graphic design studio (est. 2008) with clients including Christie's, Condé Nast, Performa, Creative Time, Socrates Sculpture Park; extensive editorial illustration for Bloomberg View, New York Times, Time Magazine, and Print Magazine

Wieden+Kennedy New York (WKNY Design Dept.) — designer on YES! (with Jordi Ng), WHAT'S TO BE DONE (with Richard Turley), and TWO SIDES TO EVERY STORY; work featured in the ongoing WKNY Dossier publication series

Teaching at Pratt Institute (Visual Communications, Typography, Web Design, Senior Project) and Parsons The New School for Design (Interaction Design) | Editorial illustration — symbol and sign-based imagery for Bloomberg, NYT, and Pitchfork

nodes.name: Zoe Beyer
nodes.bio: Zoe Beyer is the Creative Director of A24, widely credited with driving the studio's transformation into a full-fledged cultural brand. Since joining in 2013, she has built A24's merchandise shop, social media presence, podcast, auctions platform, and Public Access initiative — turning a film distributor into a cult object. Air Mail named her one of 50 young New Yorkers remaking Lower Manhattan, describing her work as building A24 into 'a drug-addled Disney.'
nodes.cv: BA Film Studies & History, Wesleyan University (2010); Production Coordinator at Brand New School (2010–2011); Pre-production Coordinator on The Better Angels (2011–2012); Creative Director at A24 since January 2013; produced A24 books Horror Caviar: A Cookbook and For Promotional Use Only (2021); A24 x Intramural Shop vintage apparel collab; A24 x Scarr's Pizza collab for Mike Mills' C'mon C'mon (2021); named to Air Mail's Downtown Set (2022) — 50 young New Yorkers remaking Lower Manhattan.

notableWork.label: A24 brand — merchandise shop, social media, podcast, A24 Auctions, and Public Access; the creative architecture behind A24's transformation from indie distributor into one of the most culturally dominant entertainment brands in the world
A24 books — Horror Caviar: A Cookbook and For Promotional Use Only (2021); A24 Auctions initiative; Public Access platform
A24 x Intramural Shop vintage movie-themed apparel and accessories collaboration; A24 x Scarr's Pizza campaign for C'mon C'mon (dir. Mike Mills, 2021)

nodes.name: Eckhaus Latta
nodes.bio: Eckhaus Latta is an American fashion brand co-founded in 2011 by Mike Eckhaus and Zoe Latta, who met studying sculpture and textiles respectively at Rhode Island School of Design. The label is known for unexpected materials, tactility, and gender-neutral design, and for incorporating writing, performance, and video into their practice. Their casting of unconventional models — artists, musicians, gallerists — across all genders, ages, and body types is as central to the brand as the clothes themselves.
nodes.cv: Founded 2011 by Mike Eckhaus (sculpture, RISD) and Zoe Latta (textiles, RISD); Eckhaus previously at Marc by Marc Jacobs, Latta ran textile firm Ruth Prince supplying Calvin Klein and Proenza Schouler; LVMH Prize finalist 2018; Possessed solo exhibition at Whitney Museum (2018, first fashion show there in 21 years); group shows at MoMA PS1 (2015) and Hammer Museum (2016); permanent stores in LA (2016) and NYC (2018); stocked in 55+ locations worldwide incl. Nordstrom, SSENSE, Opening Ceremony; collaborators include Dev Hynes, Alex Da Corte, Martine Syms, Rachel Chandler, Charlotte Wales, Susan Cianciolo.

notableWork.label: Possessed — solo exhibition at Whitney Museum of American Art (Aug–Oct 2018), first fashion-related show there in 21 years; a sculptural collage of fashion, fine art, and video exploring the relationship between clothes and identity

Spring 2017 campaign — first major ad campaign featuring adult models in unsimulated sex; one of the most widely discussed fashion campaigns of the decade; cast also included Kelela, Dev Hynes, Juliana Huxtable, Hari Nef, Ethel Cain, Sophie

Collaborations with Dev Hynes, Alex Da Corte, Martine Syms, Rachel Chandler, Charlotte Wales, Susan Cianciolo, Jessi Reaves; Camper footwear collab; group shows at Hammer Museum (2016) and MoMA PS1 (2015)

nodes.name: Haley Wollens
nodes.bio: Haley Wollens is a New York–based fashion stylist, creative director, editor, and photographer, raised in the East Village. She broke through in 2004 styling M.I.A.'s tour and has since built a practice that spans music, film, editorial, and brand work. She is best known as Chloë Sevigny's go-to stylist — Vogue has written that the pair 'simply never miss' — and in 2024 launched Myth Magazine, a digital fashion publication, as editor-in-chief.
nodes.cv: Studied motion media design at SCAD before transferring to School of Visual Arts; early assistant to Mel Ottenberg; broke out styling M.I.A. (2004, via The Fader); music clients include Blood Orange, Miley Cyrus, Drake, Arca, Inc.; commercial work for Alexander Wang x Adidas Originals, Hood By Air, Mugler x Wolford, Nike+ FuelBand, Opening Ceremony, Dsquared2, KENZO, Versace, Chanel; longtime collaborator with photographer Brianna Capozzi (Le Monde Magazine, Double Magazine); styled Chloë Sevigny's three wedding looks (2022); represented by Streeters; launched Myth Magazine (NYFW 2024) with contributors Mariacarla Boscono, Chloë Sevigny, Telfar Clemens, Addison Rae, Natasha Lyonne; Vanity Fair (Oct 2025).

notableWork.label: Myth Magazine — digital fashion publication launched at NYFW 2024; editor-in-chief; rejects rigid print structures; inaugural issue featured Chloë Sevigny, Mariacarla Boscono, Telfar Clemens; launch party covered by Vogue and WWD

Chloë Sevigny — ongoing styling partnership spanning covers, campaigns, events, and three wedding looks (2022); Vogue Emmys 2025 coverage; Vanity Fair closet sale feature (Oct 2025); collaborations with photographer Brianna Capozzi for Le Monde Magazine and Double Magazine

Music styling — Blood Orange, Miley Cyrus ('23' video, 2013), Drake, M.I.A. (tour, 2004), Arca; directed Blood Orange 'Champagne Coast' video (2012); editorial illustration for i-D, Vogue Italia, Self Service, Re-Edition

nodes.name: Gia Kuan
nodes.bio: Gia Kuan is a New Zealand-Taiwanese publicist and creative consultant, born in Taipei and raised between Santo Domingo and Auckland, known as the 'PR powerhouse behind New York's fashion renaissance' (Document Journal). She founded Gia Kuan Consulting in 2019 after stints at Comme des Garçons, Dover Street Market, and Nadine Johnson (Arts & Culture dept.), building one of NYC's most culturally attuned boutique PR practices. New York Magazine dubbed her 'the Melbourne club kid taking over the NYC fashion and art scene.'
nodes.cv: Born Taipei; raised Santo Domingo / Auckland; Law, Communications & Art History at University of Melbourne; Fashion Marketing at Parsons (2010); PR career began at Comme des Garçons, Dover Street Market, Nadine Johnson (Arts & Culture dept.); founded Gia Kuan Consulting 2019; co-founded Whaam! art gallery, NY (2019, with Anatoly Kirichenko); clients: Telfar, Luar, Puppets and Puppets, PRISCAVera, Kim Shui, Fear of God (Jerry Lorenzo), Barragan, Anna Sui, Jordan Brand, Rui, Kiko Kostadinov, Lu'u Dan, Stüssy, Starface, Sukeban, La Horde; PR Net Next Gen Art & Culture Agency of the Year 2024; The Observer most powerful PR firms 2024; Paper Predictions 100 (2019); Vogue September 2020 feature.

notableWork.label: Telfar, Luar, Puppets and Puppets, PRISCAVera, Kiko Kostadinov, Stüssy, La Horde — core fashion and culture PR client roster spanning New York, London, Shanghai, Tokyo, Paris
Whaam! — co-founded NYC art gallery (2019) with partner Anatoly Kirichenko; featured in Cultured Magazine; bridging art and fashion clientele

PR Net Next Gen Art & Culture Agency of the Year 2024; Document Journal 'PR powerhouse behind New York's fashion renaissance' (2019); Paper Predictions 100 (2019); Vogue September 2020 feature; New York Magazine 'Taking Over The NYC Fashion and Art Scene' (2021)

nodes.name: Sean Price Williams
nodes.bio: Sean Price Williams is a New York-based cinematographer and film director from Wilmington, Delaware, regarded as the defining visual voice of American independent cinema in the 2010s. Known for textured handheld camerawork and mastery of available light, he has shot dozens of acclaimed indie films — most frequently for the Safdie Brothers and Alex Ross Perry. The New Yorker's Richard Brody called him 'the cinematographer for many of the best and most significant independent films of the past decade.' He made his directorial debut with The Sweet East (2023), which premiered in Directors' Fortnight at Cannes.
nodes.cv: Former employee of Kim's Video and Music, NYC (alongside Alex Ross Perry, Kate Lyn Sheil, Robert Greene); key cinematography credits: Frownland (2007), The Color Wheel (2011), Listen Up Philip (2014), Heaven Knows What (2014), Good Time (Safdie Bros., 2017), Her Smell (2018), Funny Pages (2022), Between the Temples (2024), Harvest (2024); music videos for Oneohtrix Point Never, ASAP Rocky ('D.M.B.' 2022), Boygenius (2023), Sabrina Carpenter ('Please Please Please' 2024), Addison Rae ('Diet Pepsi,' 'Aquamarine,' 'Fame Is a Gun' 2024–25); directorial debut The Sweet East (2023, Cannes Directors' Fortnight), written by Nick Pinkerton.

notableWork.label: The Sweet East (2023) — directorial debut; written by film critic Nick Pinkerton; stars Talia Ryder, Jacob Elordi, Simon Rex, Ayo Edebiri, Jeremy O. Harris; premiered Directors' Fortnight, Cannes 2023; acquired by The Match Factory

Safdie Brothers / Alex Ross Perry cinematography — Good Time (2017), Heaven Knows What (2014), Listen Up Philip (2014), Queen of Earth (2015), Her Smell (2018); Richard Brody (New Yorker): 'cinematographer for many of the best and most significant independent films of the past decade'

Music video DP/director — Oneohtrix Point Never 'The Pure and the Damned' (2017), ASAP Rocky 'D.M.B.' (2022), Boygenius short film (2023), Sabrina Carpenter 'Please Please Please' (2024), Addison Rae 'Diet Pepsi' / 'Aquamarine' / 'Fame Is a Gun' (2024–25)

nodes.name: Hailey Benton Gates
nodes.bio: Hailey Benton Gates is a New York-based director, writer, actress, and journalist — granddaughter of Nashville screenwriter Joan Tewkesbury. A BFA in experimental theater from NYU Tisch led to early work as director of advertising at The Paris Review, then hosting Viceland's globe-trotting docuseries States of Undress (2016–17). She broke through as a filmmaker with Atropia (2025), produced by Luca Guadagnino, which won the Grand Jury Prize at Sundance 2025. As an actress she is embedded in the Guadagnino and Safdie orbit: Uncut Gems, Challengers, Marty Supreme, and The Moment.
nodes.cv: BFA Experimental Theater, NYU Tisch (2012); director of advertising at The Paris Review (2012–2015); co-wrote/produced A Space Program with Tom Sachs (2015); hosted and produced States of Undress (Viceland, 2016–17, 14 episodes); short Shako Mako (Venice 2019, Miu Miu Women's Tales, starring Alia Shawkat); feature Atropia (Sundance Grand Jury Prize 2025, produced by Luca Guadagnino, starring Alia Shawkat & Callum Turner); actress in Uncut Gems (Safdie Bros., 2019), Twin Peaks: The Return (Lynch, 2017), Challengers (Guadagnino, 2024), Marty Supreme (Josh Safdie, 2025), The Moment (Aidan Zamiri/A24, 2026); modeled for Miu Miu (Steve Meisel), Costume National; commercial direction for Vogue, Gohar World, New York Magazine.

notableWork.label: Atropia (2025) — feature debut written and directed; Grand Jury Prize, Sundance 2025; produced by Luca Guadagnino; stars Alia Shawkat and Callum Turner; a nuanced satire of a U.S. military base simulating an Iraqi war zone; Tokyo International Film Festival competition

Shako Mako (short, 2019) — directed for Miu Miu Women's Tales; starring Alia Shawkat; premiered Venice Film Festival 2019; the seed for Atropia; States of Undress (Viceland, 2016–17) — host and producer, 14 episodes across global conflict zones exploring the intersection of fashion and politics

Actress in Guadagnino/Safdie orbit — Uncut Gems (2019), Challengers (2024), Marty Supreme (2025), The Moment (A24, 2026, as Charli XCX's creative director Celeste); Twin Peaks: The Return (Lynch, 2017); A Space Program co-written with artist Tom Sachs (2015)

nodes.name: Tracy Ma
nodes.bio: Tracy Ma is a Hong Kong-born, Toronto-raised graphic designer and art director based in New York City. She rose to prominence as Deputy Creative Director at Bloomberg Businessweek (2011–2016), where she helped define one of the most visually distinctive eras in magazine design alongside Richard Turley. She subsequently became Visual Editor at The New York Times Styles desk (2018–2021), and is currently graphic design lead at Homer, Frank Ocean's brand.
nodes.cv: Grew up in Toronto; immigrated from Hong Kong; Deputy Creative Director at Bloomberg Businessweek (2011–2016, under Richard Turley); taught three semesters at Parsons (2017–2018); Visual Editor, New York Times Styles desk (2018–2021); graphic design lead at Homer, Frank Ocean's brand (2021–present); self-published Total Power Move magazine at Matter Studios (2016, with Kurt Woerpel); clients include Christine and the Queens, Google Creative Labs, Columbia GSAPP; spoke at An Interesting Day (Amsterdam), RISD, Cooper Union Typographics, Society of News Design; featured in Computer Arts Magazine, It's Nice That, SSENSE, AIGA Eye on Design, Walker Art Magazine.

notableWork.label: Homer 'A–OK' Catalog (2021) — 160-page catalog, layout and type design; creative direction by Frank Ocean; Blonded.co animations (2019) — bubble letter animation commissioned by the Blonded team, featured on blonded.co and printed as a double-sided poster

Bloomberg Businessweek covers (2013–2016) — key designer on one of the most talked-about runs in contemporary magazine design; worked with Richard Turley and Rob Vargas; SSENSE 'Dream Chairs' (2021) — sketched, designed, and 3D modelled designers' imagined chairs for SSENSE editorial

NYT Styles interactive work (2018–2021) — Election Distractor, What Is Celebrity?, Y2K@20, Gen X Is A Mess, Royal Wedding FAQ, Attack on Voting cover (NYT Magazine, CD Gail Bichler); Total Power Move self-published magazine (2016); title design for Hannahs (dir. India Donaldson, 2019)

nodes.name: Liam Hamill
nodes.bio: Liam Hamill is an Ireland-born, NYC-based graphic designer and Associate Creative Director at A24, behind the studio's most distinctive posters, motion design, and campaign materials. Before A24 he was a designer at Pentagram (2020–2022) and design lead at Wieden+Kennedy. Alongside his A24 work, he produces a monthly series of venue posters for Baby's All Right in Williamsburg — playful, brainrot-inflected designs that have earned him a feature on It's Nice That (June 2026). Inspired by Richard Turley, Frank DeRose, and Zoe Beyer.
nodes.cv: Ireland-born, NYC-based; designer at Pentagram (2020–2022); design lead at Wieden+Kennedy; Associate Creative Director at A24 (current); A24 campaign work includes The Smashing Machine (low-polygon imagery, arcade cabinet visuals), Palace Skateboards x McDonald's collaboration; monthly poster series for Baby's All Right, Williamsburg (ongoing); featured on It's Nice That, June 2026; inspired by Richard Turley, Frank DeRose (W+K), Zoe Beyer (A24).

notableWork.label: A24 campaign design — The Smashing Machine promotional materials (low-polygon imagery and arcade cabinet visuals); Palace Skateboards x McDonald's A24 collaboration; the wider visual identity behind A24's most talked-about film campaigns

Baby's All Right monthly poster series (2025–ongoing) — ongoing personal project for Williamsburg bar and music venue; each poster reframes the brief 'Baby's is a feeling' through internet satire, brainrot aesthetics, Comic Sans, Wikipedia design, meme imagery; featured on It's Nice That (June 2026)
Pentagram (2020–2022); Wieden+Kennedy design lead; A24 Associate Creative Director — placing him at the intersection of three of the most design-forward institutions in contemporary creative culture

nodes.name: Daryn Roongrawewan
nodes.bio: Daryn Roongrawewan is a Berlin-based graphic designer with an extensive background in editorial design, known for detailed and ornate visual systems that draw from vintage design languages. She crafts what she calls 'ornamental icons' rather than conventional logos — expressive, type-led identities rooted in off-screen research, historical print references (Slavic alphabets, Victorian carpet bedding, 1920s children's books), and observations from the natural world. Featured on It's Nice That, June 2026.
nodes.cv: Extensive editorial design background; working independently in Berlin; 39K Instagram followers (@darynci); featured on It's Nice That (June 2026, 'Daryn Roongrawewan doesn't design logos, she crafts ornamental icons'); approach described as drawing from vintage design languages and day-to-day observation; open to graphic design commissions and collaborations.

notableWork.label: Atelier Lin — brand identity for Berlin flower shop; custom letterforms drawn from Slavic alphabets with ornamental curves and purposely rigid, carved-metal edges; bespoke L that rotates into multiple flower shapes ('a single letter into an ornamental icon'); care card illustrations inspired by 1920s children's books and Victorian carpet bedding plans and monograms

Padma — visual identity for Los Angeles art and design studio rooted in ecology; P letterform derived from watching the purple sugar magnolia snap pea in nature rather than constructed at screen; custom wordmark mimics nodes of a plant stem; clay and raw texture elements developed for Padma's website; entire project began off-screen in the natural world

'Every mark should mean something' — design philosophy grounded in the belief that visual forms drawn from observed, recognisable things 'carry a warmth people can feel even if they can't explain it'; applies same logic to her own tattoos

nodes.name: Rajshree Saraf
nodes.bio: Rajshree Saraf is a Brooklyn-based Indian art director, designer, and creative technologist whose practice sits at the intersection of type design, augmented reality, and speculative communication design. She was an art director at Wieden+Kennedy New Delhi (2018–2020) and New York (2023–present), and holds an MPS in Interactive Telecommunications from NYU Tisch. Named one of the top 25 under-25 change-makers in India in art and design.
nodes.cv: National Institute of Fashion Technology, Kolkata (Design); MPS Interactive Telecommunications, NYU Tisch; Art Director at Wieden+Kennedy New Delhi (2018–2020) and New York (2023–present); created Typespace (2022–23) — the first AR-native variable typeface and app; TDC 69 Certificate of Typographic Excellence (2023); AIGA 365 Typographic Design winner (2023); Creative Review Awards Honorable Mention Typography (2023); top 25 under-25 Indian change-makers in art & design; featured in Communication Arts, It's Nice That, Homegrown.in.

notableWork.label: Typespace (2022–23) — the first variable typeface and app made explicitly for AR, enabling designers to create spatial type posters without 3D modelling or coding pipelines; TDC 69 Certificate of Typographic Excellence; AIGA 365 winner; Creative Review Honorable Mention

Hallucinating Type (2022) — daily AR animated typographic poster project plastered across NYC streets; angsty phrases (Fuck My Life, Full of Shit, Art School Doesn't Count) treated as 3D type sculptures; featured on It's Nice That (July 2022); new poster designed, coded, and shot in the wild every single day

Wieden+Kennedy — art direction across New Delhi (2018–20) and New York (2023–present); speculative design work confronting traditional design practice through cultural criticism and humor; IndiGo airline campaigns (New Delhi); sarafrajshree.com portfolio

nodes.name: Nada Rodanant
nodes.bio: Nada Rodanant (goes by Neat) is a Thai graphic designer and sculptor studying BFA Graphic Design and Sculpture at Rhode Island School of Design. Her practice centers on publication design and bookmaking — treating books as 3D tangible objects rather than vessels for text, with a focus on the rhythm of type and image across pages. Featured on It's Nice That (April 2026). Member of the Thai Creative Student Collective.
nodes.cv: BFA Graphic Design and Sculpture, Rhode Island School of Design (RISD); publication, typography, editorial design, visual identity; featured in It's Nice That (April 2026); member of Thai Creative Student Collective; inspired by Irma Boom, Ben Denzer; @risd_gd featured in 'Behind the Desk' series.

notableWork.label: Gum Girl — publication exploring girlhood and the grotesque; garishly pink, satirical; designed to evoke specific sensory experiences; draws on feminist literature and film quotes (something you want to touch, but also something that disgusts you)
El Lissitzky On Type + Books — printed entirely on acetate and tracing paper; laid out like architectural plans to demonstrate El Lissitzky's constructivist typographic theories
Psycho (film-to-book translation) — typography screams alongside Hitchcock's nail-biting scenes; image-centred pages that unfold rapidly through the film's most intense moments

nodes.name: Alexis Mark
nodes.bio: Alexis Mark is the shared pseudonym of Marie Grønkær, Kristoffer Li & Martin Bek — a Copenhagen-based office for graphic design, creative direction, and communication strategies. With an emphasis on identity, they help artists, institutions, and companies become culturally relevant by developing and visualising core narratives. Work ranges from branding and publication design to exhibition-making, curating, and typeface design. Educated at Yale School of Art (USA) and The Gerrit Rietveld Academie (NL). Founder and organiser of exhibition space Annual Reportt.
nodes.cv: Yale School of Art (MFA); Gerrit Rietveld Academie (NL); Bradbury Thompson Memorial Prize, Yale University (2016); Fontanel Dutch Design Talents (2014); AIGA Eye on Design (2018); Danish Arts Foundation talent program Den Unge Kunstneriske Elite (2018); Best Danish Bookdesign 2018 (two titles); Best Danish Bookdesign 2021 winner; Best Book Design from all over the World 2023 shortlisted; It's Nice That Ones to Watch (2020); The Brand Identity (2019); It's Nice That features (2017, 2018, 2022).

notableWork.label: Clients: Harvard University, Harvard Graduate School of Design, MIT Press, Yale University, The Venice Biennale, SMK — The National Gallery of Denmark, Kyoto Art Center, Sony Music, CHART Art Fair, Mads Nørgaard Copenhagen, The Ny Carlsberg Glyptotek, Dorte Mandrup Architects, Danish Architectural Press, The Royal Danish Academy of Fine Arts, When Saints Go Machine

Best Danish Bookdesign 2018 winner for Perspecta 50: Urban Divides (Harvard GSD) and Immanuel Kant: Oplysning, Historie, Fremskridt; Best Danish Bookdesign 2021 winner; shortlisted Best Book Design from all over the World 2023
Annual Reportt — founder and organiser of Copenhagen-based exhibition and project space; self-organised autonomous design projects alongside client commissions

nodes.name: Allie Hughes
nodes.bio: Allie Hughes is a Boston-based design director working at TBWA\Chiat\Day, with a background spanning Goodby Silverstein & Partners (San Francisco), Arnold Worldwide, and Mullen. Her practice covers branding, identity, and advertising campaigns — known for work that balances playfulness with rigour. Featured on It's Nice That (March 2026) for her rebrand of Jordan's Furniture.
nodes.cv: Fine arts education; Design Director at TBWA\Chiat\Day; formerly Senior Designer at Goodby Silverstein & Partners (SF) and Arnold Worldwide; Mullen (Boston); featured It's Nice That (March 2026). Clients include Jordan's Furniture, ADC Awards, Branch Museum, Mount Gay, Sperry, Schick, KFC, Naked x Coco, Stanley Forever.

notableWork.label: Jordan's Rebrand (2026, TBWA\Chiat\Day) — first rebrand in 30+ years for New England furniture chain; reimagined wordmark dropping 'Furniture', custom J with a 'wink' dot, ergonomic icon system (reclining chairs, beds, lamps); featured It's Nice That March 2026

Jack Daniels Tennessee Honey — art direction for launch campaign; honey-with-attitude visual language; illustration by Nathan Fox; animation by Pete Candeland; anchored in JD's strength and patriotism codes
Branch Museum (Richmond, VA) — identity and design; American Eagle campaigns; ADC Awards identity

nodes.name: Kimera Corp
nodes.bio: Kimera Corp is a Munich-based independent type foundry and design studio founded by Michael Clasen (typeface design with Marcel Saidov on select projects). Every project begins with historical research — investigating how historical ideas can be translated into contemporary typefaces meeting today's technological requirements. Operates as type foundry, art direction studio, and product store simultaneously; philosophy: 'Unity in Multiplicity.' Featured on It's Nice That (March 2026).
nodes.cv: Founded by Michael Clasen; collaborators include Marcel Saidov; Munich-based (Parkstrasse 22); retail typeface library plus bespoke commissions; design services covering identity, custom type, editorial art direction; featured It's Nice That (March 2026) for Waldenburg's use in Oscar-winning film Sentimental Value (dir. Joachim Trier); clients include AFFXWRKS, Souvenir Official, Wastecare, Transmoderna, Visitors Collective, Athene run club (London); The Brand Identity feature on Cursor identity system.

notableWork.label: Waldenburg — sans serif 'superfamily' of 24 cuts; named after founder Michael Clasen's small hometown in southwest Germany; balances humanist core with mathematical precision and long, elegant x-height; combines early rationalism of Akzidenz Grotesk with the calligraphic warmth of Univers; used in Oscar-winning Sentimental Value (dir. Joachim Trier) film campaign (2025); featured It's Nice That March 2026

Apparat (KMR Apparat) — early-70s-infused, ink-trap-heavy sans serif; designed by Michael Clasen & Marcel Saidov. Melange Grotesk — warm, modernist sans serif. Both part of Kimera's retail type library.

Bespoke client work: AFFXWRKS wordmark (custom type); Athene run club logo (London); Cursor identity system built around custom typeface (The Brand Identity feature); Souvenir Official rebrand; PMCO21 branding; Thermal Dynamics editorial; Visitors Collective identity

nodes.name: KNWLS
nodes.bio: KNWLS is a south London-based fashion brand designed by Charlotte Knowles (UK) and Alexandre Arsenault (Canada), who met at Central Saint Martins. Founded in 2017 as Charlotte Knowles, rebranded to KNWLS in 2021 to reflect the studio-wide, collective nature of their design process. The brand sits at the intersection of underwear, sportswear, and historical dress — a disruptive vision of femininity that is dangerous yet sensual. LVMH Prize 2022 finalist.
nodes.cv: Founded 2017; graduated Central Saint Martins; rebranded to KNWLS 2021; LVMH Prize 2022 finalist (alongside ERL, S.S. Daley, RYUNOSUKEOKAZAKI); London Fashion Week; Milan Fashion Week debut (recent); stockists: SSENSE, H.Lorenzo, LN-CC, Nordstrom. Informed by Charlotte's upbringing around strong English women and Alexandre's formative years in Montreal's metal scene.

notableWork.label: Nike x KNWLS collaboration — eight-piece womenswear collection including corset, twinset, tops, trousers, bag, and exclusive Air Max Muse colourway; launched globally via SNKRS app; presented at Milan Fashion Week; described as 'high-femme armor celebrating grace, power and fearless sensuality'
Miss Sixty x KNWLS collaboration — capsule collection merging both labels' attitude and irreverence

Collections: SS26, Ballistic (Victorian-inspired — poufed shoulders, corsetry, gathered busts; shown at the Painting Rooms), Halcyon. Core aesthetic: corsetry, structural silhouettes, mesh, second-skin fabrications reworked for an empowered digital wardrobe.

nodes.name: Louis Garella
nodes.bio: Louis Garella is a Paris-based independent graphic designer and art director (originally from Aix-en-Provence) running Studio Garella. His hybrid practice sits at the intersection of visual arts, graphic design, and spatial design — defined by a 'constant back-and-forth between digital and print.' Work intercepts scans, collages, and analogue impressions to create visual languages that feel closely connected to their subject matter. Featured on It's Nice That (January 2026). Has designed for Hermès.
nodes.cv: Studied at National Superior Diploma of Plastic Expression (art and design school, Saint-Étienne); independent studio: Studio Garella, Paris; expertise: visual identities, logos, editorial design, photography, film, motion, 3D; clients include Hermès, Puma, Sonata Electronica, Abri Ramen, Opaline; featured It's Nice That January 2026.

notableWork.label: Hermès 'une nuit d'été' identity (2025) — hazy, smudged visual language achieved through collage, digital manipulation, and printmaking on various surfaces; xerox and printmaking imperfections make designs feel ephemeral when digitised; featured It's Nice That
Sonata Electronica identity (2025) — logo scans where xerox ink running low creates a shapeshifting, dancing effect; scanning graphic elements as creative raw material
Abri Ramen identity (2025); Opaline (2024–25); Procession (2023); Ngorongoro Road (2025); Window Shopper (2024); Puma x 'Le cœur engagé' Paola Locatelli (2024)

nodes.name: G.I.P. Laboratory
nodes.bio: G.I.P. Laboratory is a Japanese audio manufacturer founded by Shinichi Suzuki that builds horn loudspeaker systems and vacuum tube amplifiers based on Western Electric theater technology from the 1920s–30s. Suzuki's mission is to recreate the field-coil drivers many consider the greatest ever made — used originally in cinema sound systems — with near-identical engineering fidelity, including cobalt/iron alloy construction. The 594A replica alone took over 10 years to engineer. Described by Stereophile as 'craftspeople who treat stereo gear as a calling, not a commodity.'
nodes.cv: Founded by Shinichi Suzuki (musician and audio engineer); manufactures complete horn loudspeaker systems, field-coil compression drivers (594A, 597A, 555 replicas), field-coil woofers (4181A), horn tweeters, vacuum tube amplifiers and preamplifiers; US distributor: True Source Audio Distribution; featured Stereophile; shown at Capital AudioFest 2024 and Sierra Sound.

notableWork.label: 9700A flagship loudspeaker system — two 18" field-coil woofers per side, large midrange horn of Japanese Cherry Blossom wood; modern interpretation of WE 1930s cinema theater sound; adjustable mid and treble attenuation

594A field-coil compression driver replica — took founder Shinichi Suzuki 10+ years to engineer; cobalt/iron alloy construction faithful to original; used in large theater applications; considered 'one of the many sonic masterpieces Western Electric created during audio's Golden Age'

597A field-coil tweeter replica — considered the first production tweeter ever made; solid brass horn construction (9501 variant); 555 full-range compression driver replica; 4181A 18" field-coil woofer (originally used in Mirrophonic systems for movie palaces)

nodes.name: Teenage Engineering
nodes.bio: Teenage Engineering is a Stockholm-based consumer electronics and design company founded in 2005 by Jesper Kouthoofd, David Eriksson, Jens Rudberg, and David Möllerstedt. Known for treating music hardware as design objects — playful, minimal, and obsessively considered. Their OP-1 synthesizer is in the permanent collection of SFMOMA. Collaborators span IKEA, Nothing, Panic (Playdate), and Rabbit Inc, demonstrating a design practice that extends well beyond audio into product and industrial design.
nodes.cv: Founded 2005 (Stockholm); ~55 employees; OP-1 introduced 2010/released 2011; Design S Award Sweden (2012); Good Design Award Japan (2017, Pocket Operators); OP-1 in SFMOMA permanent collection; Georgia Tech Margaret Guthman Musical Instrument Competition 2nd prize (2014); co-founders previously led Netbabyworld games; David Möllerstedt ex-audio department EA DICE. Collaborations: IKEA (Frekvens, 2018), Cheap Monday (Pocket Operators), Nothing (ear 1 audio, 2021), Panic (Playdate, 2019), Rabbit Inc (r1, 2024), Yuri Suzuki (PO-80 vinyl recorder), Capcom, Adult Swim.

notableWork.label: OP-1 / OP-1 Field — synthesizer, sampler, sequencer; AMOLED display; unconventional form factor; in SFMOMA permanent collection; used by Bon Iver, Beck, Depeche Mode, Jean Michel Jarre; Design S Award 2012; current retail ~$1,399

Pocket Operators (2015–ongoing) — pocket calculator-sized synthesizers and sequencers; studio-quality sound; $59–99; co-developed with Cheap Monday; Good Design Award Japan 2017; ~40,000 units sold at launch; multiple series (PO-10 through PO-137). EP-133 K.O. II sampler (2023); OP-Z (2018); OP-XY sequencer/synthesizer (2024); TX-6 field mixer (2022); TP-7 field recorder (2023)

Design collaborations beyond audio: IKEA Frekvens audio/lighting system (2018, Bauhaus-inspired); Playdate handheld game console for Panic Inc. (2019, mechanical crank credited to TE); Nothing ear (1) audio design (2021); Rabbit r1 AI device design (2024); B-1 generative film deck for Brian Eno documentary (2024); Impossible I-1 instant camera for The Impossible Project (2016)

nodes.name: Yamamoto Sound Craft
nodes.bio: Yamamoto Sound Craft is a Japanese audio manufacturer based in Ono City, Hyogo, founded by Shigeki Yamamoto — a musician, machinist, and master of single-ended triode (SET) vacuum tube amplifier design. All amplifiers are built by point-to-point hand-wiring with no printed circuits. Yamamoto machines many components himself, including PTFE (Teflon) tube sockets that are used in some of the world's most expensive amplifiers, and ebony capacitor cases, knobs, and wooden headshells for turntables. Multiple Japanese audio magazine prize winner; 6moons Blue Moon Award.
nodes.cv: Founded by Shigeki Yamamoto; Ono City, Hyogo, Japan (726 Sakaki-tyo, Ono-city 675-1323); manufactures SET amplifiers, preamplifiers, phono stages, DA converters, horn speakers, wooden and titanium headshells, PTFE vacuum tube sockets, magnet floating bases, audio racks, and phono accessories; multiple MJ Technology of the Year awards; Audio Excellence Award; AV Grand Prix; 6moons Blue Moon Award (A-08, YDA-01B); Stereophile published; products sold via Mockingbird Distribution (US).

notableWork.label: A-08S / A-08SS — 45 and 2A3 single-ended triode power amplifiers; point-to-point hand-wiring; no printed circuits; multiple Japanese audio magazine prizes (MJ Technology of the Year, Audio Excellence, Visual Grand Prix); 6moons Blue Moon Award; Stereophile published; A-011SS also awarded two Japanese magazine prizes

PTFE (Teflon) vacuum tube sockets — precision-machined by Yamamoto himself; covers vast range of tube types (300B, 2A3, 45, WE VT-52, GM70, 304TH, EL152, etc.); used in some of the world's most expensive amplifiers; considered among the finest available; also YDA-01B vacuum tube output DAC (6moons Blue Moon Award)

Wooden & titanium headshells — African blackwood (HS-1A), boxwood (HS-3), carbon (HS-4), titanium (HS-5, HS-6/HS-6S Audio Meikisyo prize), camellia wood (HS-7); HSA-01 headshell with built-in head amplifier. Also: RS-1G gold record stabilizer; SUT-02 step-up transformer; 6N copper annealed wire; magnet floating bases (MGB series); speaker stands (DK/DKS); OS/OSW audio racks.

nodes.name: SPEC Corporation
nodes.bio: SPEC Corporation is a Tokyo-based high-end audio manufacturer founded in January 2010 by Shuzou Ishimi and led by chief engineer Shirokazu Yazaki, formerly chief engineer at TEAC and Pioneer. On the belief that 'the joy of music is only found in Real-Sound,' SPEC exclusively designs Class D amplifiers engineered to reproduce the warmth and musicality of tube amplifiers through a proprietary approach. Products are distinguished by the integration of natural wood into the chassis aesthetic.
nodes.cv: Founded January 6, 2010; co-founder Shuzou Ishimi; chief engineer Shirokazu Yazaki (ex-TEAC and Pioneer chief engineer); Tokyo, Japan; product categories: integrated amplifiers (RSA series), power amplifiers, DACs, network players, turntable, phono preamp, line selectors, pure direct controllers; international distribution across Europe and Asia.

notableWork.label: RSA-EX1000 / RSA-M99 / RSA-777EX integrated amplifiers — flagship Class D designs with tube-like sonic character; wood-integrated chassis; reviewed in Positive Feedback (RSA-EX1000), Alpha Audio (RSA-M99); proprietary 'Real-Sound' Class D topology
RMP-DAC3EX — artisanal Japanese DAC with vacuum tube output stage; reviewed Witchdoctor (2023) as 'not like the others'
Network player, turntable, and phono preamplifier; pure direct controllers; monaural and stereo power amplifiers; full analog and digital source ecosystem

nodes.name: Ascendo Immersive Audio
nodes.bio: Ascendo Immersive Audio GmbH (AIA) is a German audio manufacturer established in 1999 in Ansbach, dedicated to state-of-the-art home theater and immersive audio systems. The company leads the industry in infrasonic bass reproduction — achieving bass as low as 3Hz — and employs point-source coaxial speaker design for time-coherent immersive audio. Predominantly European business for its first two decades before expanding to the US market. (Note: listed by user as 'Ascendo Japan' — no Japan-specific entity found; this is the German manufacturer.)
nodes.cv: Established 1999; Ansbach, Germany; specializes in immersive audio home theater, high-performance speakers, infrasonic subwoofers; coaxial point-source driver technology; HTA certified; US distributor added in recent years; products engineered for the distinct acoustic challenges of residential cinema (reflections, standing waves, time-domain interactions).

notableWork.label: Infrasonic subwoofer systems — bass reproduction to 3Hz; unique infrasonic bass technology unmatched in residential audio
Coaxial point-source speaker systems — tweeter centered within midrange driver for perfect concentric alignment and time-coherent immersive audio reproduction
Full residential home theater systems engineered for immersive audio formats (Dolby Atmos, DTS:X); complete high-performance audio and whole-house music solutions

nodes.name: Avantgarde Acoustic
nodes.bio: Avantgarde Acoustic is a German high-end horn loudspeaker manufacturer founded in 1991 by Holger Fromme and Matthias Ruff in the Odenwald region near Darmstadt. Exclusively dedicated to horn technology for over 30 years, the company handcrafts its speakers and amplifiers at its Lautertal-Reichenbach factory, shipping to over 60 countries. Each speaker undergoes 32 tests and quality checks before leaving. (Note: listed by user as France — this is a German manufacturer.)
nodes.cv: Founded 1991; Holger Fromme & Matthias Ruff; Nibelungenstrasse 349, 64686 Lautertal-Reichenbach, Germany; 30+ years exclusively horn technology; ships to 60+ countries; builds both loudspeakers and proprietary iTRON current amplifiers; worldwide dealer network; @avantgardeacoustic.

notableWork.label: TRIO G3 — flagship three-way spherical horn system; reviewed The Absolute Sound; ultra-high efficiency; separate horn units for bass, midrange, and treble
DUO GT / DUO SD / UNO SD — spherical horn midrange + horn tweeter + active bass; long-established reference models; wide range of sensitivity options; MEZZO compact horn system
OPUS 1 flagship; ZERO iTRON; SpaceHorn; iTRON electronics — proprietary current-mode amplification technology developed in-house; Colibri sub-brand for entry models

nodes.name: AER Loudspeakers
nodes.bio: AER Loudspeakers is a German full-range driver manufacturer based in the Stuttgart area, producing high-performance wide-range chassis since 1985. Founded by Filip Keller, AER has reached a level of quality and uniformity such that drivers no longer need to be matched in pairs for high-quality applications — an extraordinarily rare achievement. AER drivers have been used in legendary systems including the Audiovector, the Greek Arcadian Audio Pnoe Horn, and the RL Acoustique Lamhorn.
nodes.cv: Since 1985; Stuttgart area, Germany; founded by Filip Keller; 40+ years of research into full-range driver and horn enclosure design; manufacturers full-range/wide-range drivers (BD series, formerly MD series); OEM supplier to major high-end speaker builders worldwide; presented at Hifideluxe Munich; international press coverage.

notableWork.label: BD series (formerly MD series) — flagship wide-range/full-range drivers; over four decades of R&D; extraordinary driver-to-driver uniformity — pair matching no longer required; the first choice for high-end full-range technology
Pnoe horn compatibility — AER drivers specified in the legendary Arcadian Audio Pnoe horn loudspeaker (Greece); also used in Audiovector and RL Acoustique Lamhorn

Complete Pnoe horn system (in collaboration with Arcadian Audio); custom and OEM driver applications for high-end speaker manufacturers worldwide; BD4 and broader BD series for horn and open-baffle enclosures

nodes.name: Charney Audio
nodes.bio: Charney Audio is an American loudspeaker manufacturer based in Somerset, New Jersey, operated by Brian Charney. The company specializes in hand-built Tractrix horn enclosures using single full-range drivers — primarily Lowther and AER units. Each enclosure is CNC-machined by Brian Charney himself, with designs prioritizing extreme efficiency, speed, dynamics, and transparency. A regular presence at Capital AudioFest and the New York Audio Show.
nodes.cv: Founded and run by Brian Charney; Somerset, NJ, USA; single-driver Tractrix horn specialist; uses Lowther DX4, AER drivers; all enclosures CNC-machined by hand; shown at Capital AudioFest, New York Audio Show; reviewed in Stereophile.

notableWork.label: Concerto — flagship Tractrix horn loudspeaker; single Lowther DX4 full-range driver; CNC-machined enclosure by Brian Charney; extraordinary dynamics, speed, and transparency
Companion — smaller Tractrix horn design sharing the speed, dynamics, and transparency of the Concerto; companion to the flagship
Various models using AER and Lowther drivers in Tractrix horn enclosures; all hand-built and CNC-machined in Somerset, NJ

nodes.name: DeVore Fidelity
nodes.bio: DeVore Fidelity is a Brooklyn-based loudspeaker company founded in 2000 by John DeVore, a RISD graduate, lifelong musician, and 20-year speaker designer. Every speaker is hand-built in the Brooklyn Navy Yard, combining acoustic engineering with aesthetic artistry. The company produces two series — Gibbon and Orangutan — whose names playfully honor John's uncle, primatologist Irven DeVore. Universally acclaimed by the audiophile press since their debut; Stereophile noted in 2005 that their products 'pull down critical kudos and consumer raves that more established companies would kill for.'
nodes.cv: Founded 2000; John DeVore (RISD, 30 years musician, 20 years speaker design, 18 years Hi-Fi industry); Brooklyn Navy Yard, 63 Flushing Ave., Unit 259, Bld 280, Ste 510, Brooklyn NY 11205; cabinetmaker Anthony Abbate; two series: Gibbon and Orangutan; awards for every product; international distribution; speaker names inspired by primatology (uncle Irven DeVore, Harvard primatologist/anthropologist).

notableWork.label: Orangutan Series — O/96, O/93, O/Reference, O/Bronze, O/baby, micr/O; high-efficiency designs (96dB+) optimized for low-powered single-ended tube amplifiers; critically acclaimed; O/Reference is the flagship
Gibbon Series — gibbon X, gibbon Super Nine; dynamic designs covering a wider range of amplification; gibbon X praised in Stereophile and major audio publications
O/Reference flagship — hand-built Brooklyn Navy Yard; top-of-range Orangutan; AER and proprietary drivers; cabinetry by Anthony Abbate; 'nothing brings you closer to the music'

nodes.name: Fern & Roby
nodes.bio: High-end audio manufacturer and curated dealer based in Richmond, Virginia. Designs and builds heirloom-quality turntables (Montrose and Montrose Heirloom, machined from Richlite and brass with active platter feedback), single-driver speakers (Raven II bookshelf, Raven III floor-standing with custom SEAS drivers), and Amp No. 2. Also represents a select roster of brands including DeVore Fidelity, Weiss Engineering, Linear Tube Audio, and Grimm Audio, with a factory-adjacent listening room open for appointments in Richmond.
nodes.cv: Reviewed in Stereophile by Herb Reichert ("sound quality would put the sound of many amp farms to shame"), Dwell Magazine ("furniture combines modern finishes with a reverence for the history of American industry"), and Part-Time Audiophile. Regular exhibitor at Capital AudioFest.

notableWork.label: Raven III — floor-standing single-driver speakers with custom SEAS drivers (walnut or ash)
Montrose Heirloom Turntable — belt-driven, active platter feedback, Richlite and brass construction
Amp No. 2 — integrated amplifier reviewed in Stereophile

nodes.name: Zu Audio
nodes.bio: American high-sensitivity loudspeaker and cable manufacturer based in Ogden, Utah, all products made in the USA since the early 2000s. Known for full-range driver speaker designs emphasizing high efficiency, wide dynamic range, and musical immediacy. Product line spans loudspeakers (Method, Union, Druid, Definition series), subwoofers, speaker and interconnect cables, phono cartridges, turntable footers, and accessories — all backed by a 60-day satisfaction and price guarantee.
nodes.cv: Established early 2000s; 'A Revolution in American Hi-Fi' brand ethos; regular exhibitor at audio shows; broad community following through ZuB3 listening club and active blog.

notableWork.label: Method Bookshelf — compact high-efficiency full-range bookshelf speaker
Union — entry-level full-range loudspeaker
Mission Loudspeaker Cable Mk. III

nodes.name: Volti Audio
nodes.bio: Small, family-run high-sensitivity horn loudspeaker company founded in 2010 by Greg Roberts and his wife Laurie in Baxter, Tennessee. Roberts, a former custom home builder and lifelong Klipsch devotee, launched Volti after years of restoring and upgrading vintage Klipsch speakers. Every speaker is hand-built to order with a 2–4 month lead time. The line runs from Razz (entry) through Lucera (15″ woofer) and Rival to the flagship Vittora — a fully horn-loaded three-way with 105dB sensitivity and bass extension to 20Hz.
nodes.cv: Vittora, Rival, and Lucera all reviewed in Stereophile; Lucera also featured in Dagogo interview with Greg Roberts. Regular exhibitor at Capital AudioFest and major US audio shows.

notableWork.label: Vittora — flagship, 105dB sensitivity, fully horn-loaded three-way, bass to 20Hz
Rival — large three-way horn speaker, 'huge and enveloping soundstage'
Lucera — 15″ woofer horn design, reviewed in Stereophile

nodes.name: 47 Labs
nodes.bio: 47 Labs (四十七研究所) is a Japanese audiophile electronics manufacturer founded in 1992 by designer Junji Kimura, whose guiding philosophy is 'only the simplest can accommodate the most complex.' The landmark 4706 Gaincard integrated amplifier used just nine parts per channel and a 32mm signal path, pioneering the global gainclone/chip-amp movement. Current lines include the Reference Series, Shigaraki Series (ceramic chassis), Midnight Blue Series, and Arare Series (traditional Japanese wood-joinery chassis, separate power supply). Also produces turntables, CD transports, phono equalizers, and cables.
nodes.cv: Founded 1992 by Junji Kimura; 4706 Gaincard reviewed in Stereophile, 6moons, TNT-Audio, and Dagogo — widely cited as one of the most influential amplifiers of the late 1990s. Collaborated with Mark Audio on Fidelitatem Sound active speaker system.

notableWork.label: 4706 Gaincard — legendary minimalist chip amplifier (9 parts/channel, 32mm signal path)
Shigaraki Series — components in Shigaraki ceramic chassis
Arare Series — 4749 amp + 4752 phono EQ in traditional wood-joinery chassis with separate PSU

nodes.name: Buchardt Audio
nodes.bio: Danish loudspeaker manufacturer founded by Mads Buchardt, operating a direct-to-consumer model that bypasses traditional dealer and distributor networks to deliver high-end sound at maximum value. Free worldwide shipping on all speakers with all import duties and taxes included — the listed price is the final delivered price. Known for 'big sound in compact designs,' producing both passive and active speakers with strong critical reception across Europe and the US.
nodes.cv: Hi-fi+ on Anniversary 10: 'value for money is pretty much off the scale'; Steve Huff: 'A NEW Golden Age of Audio'; P300 called 'near perfect' by Cheap Audioman. Global community via Buchardt Audio Worldwide Owners Group on Facebook.

notableWork.label: S400 MK3 — evolution of their most celebrated passive bookshelf speaker (a modern classic since 2016)
Anniversary 10 — 10-year retrospective flagship bookshelf design
A10 — active integrated speaker system

nodes.name: Lyravox
nodes.bio: Hamburg-based high-end audio manufacturer founded in 2013 by Dr. Götz von Laffert and Jens Wietschorke, calling for 'a re-formation of audio' — a holistic approach integrating acoustics, craft, and modern technology into complete, all-in-one systems designed as natural centerpieces of a home. Wietschorke brings 15+ years of hand-building custom speakers; von Laffert brings aesthetic and business vision. Systems such as the Stereomaster and Karlmann Monolith fuse amplification, DSP, and loudspeaker into single architectural objects.
nodes.cv: Founded 2013; 10-year anniversary feature in Lowbeats; reviewed in Fairaudio (Karlmann Monolith); featured in Fidelity Online and Der Hamburger. Exhibited at Munich High End and major European audio shows.

notableWork.label: Stereomaster — integrated all-in-one audio wall system (inaugural product)
Karlmann Monolith — active loudspeaker, reviewed by Fairaudio
Minitower — compact holistic active speaker system

nodes.name: Rega
nodes.bio: British hi-fi manufacturer founded in 1973 by Roy Gandy, engineering everything by hand across a 70,000 sq ft facility in Essex. Obsessed with specialist materials, tolerances, and acoustic accuracy, Rega designs turntables (Planar 1 through Naia), tonearms (RB series), phono cartridges (Nd, Ania, Apheta, Aphelion), amplifiers (Io through Osiris), phono stages, loudspeakers (KYTE, AYA), and CD players. Over 150 employees; exported to 50+ countries.
nodes.cv: Founded 1973 by Roy Gandy. Planar 3 one of the best-selling turntables of all time; Naia reference turntable among the finest ever made. Regular at Munich High End and global audio shows. Philosophy: affordable excellence and lifelong durability.

notableWork.label: Planar 3 — definitive accessible audiophile belt-drive turntable (best-selling ever)
Naia — reference turntable, ultra-lightweight / high-rigidity plinth taken to the extreme
Aphelion 2 — reference moving-coil phono cartridge

nodes.name: Pro-Ject Audio Systems

location.raw: Mistelbach, Austria (+ factories in Czech Republic)

nodes.bio: Austrian turntable and hi-fi manufacturer founded in 1991 by Heinz Lichtenegger, who discovered surplus Tesla turntable components after the fall of the Iron Curtain and saw an opportunity to build affordable, high-quality analog audio. Pro-Ject now employs ~500 people across factories in Austria and the Czech Republic, producing turntables, phono stages, amplifiers, and accessories spanning the Debut, Xtension, X1, X2, RPM, and 6 PerspeX lines.
nodes.cv: Founded 1991 by Heinz Lichtenegger; Debut turntable (1999) widely credited with democratizing vinyl and fueling the global vinyl revival; distributed in 80+ countries; Lichtenegger regularly interviewed by Stereophile, What Hi-Fi, and StereoLife Magazine.

notableWork.label: Debut — the turntable credited with igniting the global vinyl revival (1999)
Xtension 12 — flagship high-performance turntable
Phono Box series — widely-used phono preamps from entry to mid tier

nodes.name: Technics
nodes.bio: Legendary Japanese audio brand launched by Panasonic in 1965, retired in 2010, and spectacularly revived in 2014 with new Reference and Grand Class product lines. Technics is synonymous with the SL-1200 direct-drive turntable (introduced 1972), which became the de facto standard for DJs worldwide and one of the most culturally significant audio devices ever made. Today's lineup includes the SL-1200GR2, SL-1000R reference turntable, SU-R1000 integrated amp, EAH-AZ100 hi-fi wireless earbuds, and all-in-one systems.
nodes.cv: SL-1200 (1972) a cornerstone of DJ culture and design history; brand revived 2014; SU-R1000 amp received Absolute Sound Golden Ear Award; EAH-AZ100 and AZ80 win multiple global awards (TechRadar, What Hi-Fi, Engadget, Rolling Stone, WIRED); SL-100C named a 'grand bargain' by Tracking Angle.

notableWork.label: SL-1200GR2 — contemporary evolution of the iconic direct-drive DJ/audiophile turntable (est. 1972)
SU-R1000 — Reference Class integrated amplifier (Absolute Sound Golden Ear Award)
EAH-AZ100 — Reference Class hi-fi true wireless earbuds with Dolby Atmos

nodes.name: Mobius Acoustics
nodes.bio: Mobius Acoustics is an independent audio brand positioning itself at the intersection of community and hi-fidelity, describing its mission as 'collaborative sound systems linking community and Hi-Fidelity.' A small, emerging operation with limited public documentation; active on Instagram where it cultivates an audiophile-community focused presence.
nodes.cv: Active on Instagram (@mobius_acoustics); limited published press or documented history as of 2026.

nodes.name: Waiting For Ideas
nodes.bio: Paris-based multidisciplinary design studio founded in 2021 by Jean-Baptiste Anotin, operating at the interface of objects, installations, and spatial experience. Clients include Saint Laurent, Grand Palais, Kith, Lacoste, and Mobilier National. Their debut audio product, the PP-1 turntable (2025, €5,800) — milled from a solid aluminium block, toneless and armless, playing vinyl upside-down — was named one of TIME Magazine's 300 Best Inventions of 2025 and won the Monocle Design Award for Best in Audio 2026.
nodes.cv: Founded 2021 by Jean-Baptiste Anotin. PP-1 covered by Dezeen, The Verge, Hypebeast, Wallpaper*, Design Boom, and Axis Magazine (Japan). Exhibited at DESIGNTIDE Tokyo (2025), Alcova Milan, Collectible Brussels, and Paris Design Week. Jury member: Paris Design Awards 2025/2026. Curated: Factory 'Regeneration' (Paris Design Week 2025).

notableWork.label: PP-1 — solid aluminium monolithic turntable; TIME Best Inventions 2025; Monocle Design Award 2026 Best in Audio
Featured: Dezeen 'monolithic PP-1 record player from solid aluminium block' (2025); Wallpaper* 'a minimal aluminium altar for vinyl worship'
Exhibited at DESIGNTIDE Tokyo (2025), Alcova Milan, Collectible Brussels

nodes.name: Phonon
nodes.bio: Japanese professional audio headphone brand founded in 2010 by mastering engineer Isao Kumano alongside DJ Alex Prat (Alex from Tokyo, of the Tokyo Black Star music production team) and audio technician Yusuke Uchiyama. Phonon's headphones are precision-tuned by Kumano for professional studio monitoring, prized for exceptional low-volume playback accuracy and spatial definition. The SMB-02 ('Subtonic Studio Monitor Basic') is crafted in Japan and occupies a niche at the intersection of professional audio, DJ culture, and audiophile streetwear.
nodes.cv: Founded 2010; SMB-02 featured on Hypebeast (2010); distributed via Phonon EU online shop and international dealers; documented on Resident Advisor (crowdfunding for 4400 headphone revival); 5,000+ Instagram followers.

notableWork.label: SMB-02 — flagship studio monitor headphones, precision-tuned by Isao Kumano, crafted in Japan
SMB-02G Special Edition — updated variant with gold driver
Phonon 4400 — open-back headphones (crowdfunded revival, covered by Resident Advisor)

nodes.name: Grado Labs
nodes.bio: Brooklyn-based headphone and phono cartridge manufacturer founded in 1953 by master watchmaker Joseph Grado, who invented the first stereo moving coil phono cartridge. Currently run by President and CEO John Grado (Joseph's nephew, at the company since 1965, owner since 1990), who created Grado's first headphone line in the early 1990s alongside his wife Loretta. Nearly all products are still hand-built in Brooklyn. Son Jonathan Grado joined in 2013 to modernize the brand. Cult following includes Neil Young, members of Aerosmith, and director Spike Jonze.
nodes.cv: Founded 1953; Joseph Grado invented first stereo MC cartridge; John Grado built first headphones in early 1990s; first wooden headphone 1994; cult documented in Fast Company, Engadget, PS Audio Copper; featured in Stereophile. Fanbase: Neil Young, Aerosmith, Spike Jonze.

notableWork.label: SR Series (SR60, SR80, SR325) — classic open-back dynamic headphones; cult audiophile objects
Statement Series — flagship reference headphones
Phono cartridge line — wood-body MC and MM cartridges, hand-built in Brooklyn

nodes.name: AIAIAI
nodes.bio: Danish audio company founded in 2006 dedicated to empowering music creators, with a Scandinavian design heritage and a modular approach to longevity. Headquartered in Copenhagen with a second community space in LA's Chinatown. The TMA-2 modular headphone system won the Cannes Lions product design gold trophy, a Clio Silver Award, the Good Design Award from the Chicago Athenaeum, and is part of the permanent collection at SF MoMA. UNIT-4 wireless studio monitor won Red Dot 2024. Artist roster: Benji B, Kaytranada, Charlotte de Witte, Bonobo, Richie Hawtin, Marcel Dettmann, Busy P, Young Guru.
nodes.cv: Founded 2006; TMA-2 in SF MoMA permanent collection; Cannes Lions Gold (product design); Good Design Award; Red Dot 2024 (UNIT-4); Brain Dead, Blood Orange (Dev Hynes), and HIDDEN NY collaborations. Student rebate and global sponsorship programs.

notableWork.label: TMA-2 — modular headphone system (SF MoMA permanent collection; Cannes Lions Gold; Good Design Award)
UNIT-4 Wireless — portable studio monitor (Red Dot Design Award 2024)
Blood Orange Edition TMA-2 — collaboration with Dev Hynes

nodes.name: Master & Dynamic
nodes.bio: New York City-based premium audio brand founded in 2014 by Jonathan Levine, inspired by WWII aviator headphones seen in a Washington DC museum. Built around 'beautifully crafted, richly appointed, technically sophisticated sound tools' using fine leathers, stainless steel, and Kevlar — engineered to last decades. Positioned at the intersection of luxury design and audiophile performance. NBA star Kevin Durant holds equity. Products: MH40 (wired + wireless), MW75 (ANC), MW09 true wireless, MW50+, ME05.
nodes.cv: Founded 2014; Red Dot Product Design 2019 (MW07), 2023 (MW75); Neurable MW75 Neuro BCI partnership. Collaborations: Rolling Stones, Leica (x2), Louis Vuitton (x2), BAPE (x2), Aston Martin, Lamborghini (x3), Bugatti, Mercedes-Benz/AMG, Chanel, Paris Saint-Germain, TUMI, Nike KD, Applied Art Forms, Oliver Peoples, Away, JetBlue Mint.

notableWork.label: MH40 Wireless — flagship over-ear; collab platform for Louis Vuitton, Leica, BAPE, Chanel
MW75 — ANC wireless over-ear (Red Dot 2023); MW75 Neuro BCI (with Neurable)
MW09 — ANC true wireless earphones

nodes.name: Koss
nodes.bio: American headphone company founded in 1958 by John C. Koss in Milwaukee — the inventor of the world's first stereo headphone. The original SP/3 Stereophone, initially developed as a demonstration accessory for a portable phonograph, became an accidental sensation and launched the entire personal listening industry. The SP/3 is enshrined at the Smithsonian; John Koss was inducted into the Audio Hall of Fame in 1979. In 1984, the Porta Pro launched — an iconic, foldable on-ear headphone that has been in continuous production ever since, over 40 years.
nodes.cv: Founded 1958; SP/3 (1958) world's first stereo headphone — at the Smithsonian Institution; John Koss, Audio Hall of Fame 1979 and Wisconsin History Maker Award; Porta Pro (1984) in continuous production 40+ years. Cult following among audiophiles and streetwear/sneaker communities.

notableWork.label: SP/3 Stereophone (1958) — world's first stereo headphone, displayed at the Smithsonian
Porta Pro (1984) — iconic foldable on-ear headphone, in continuous production for 40+ years
KPH30i — modern open-back on-ear headphone beloved in budget audiophile circles

nodes.name: Nothing
nodes.bio: London-based consumer technology company founded in 2020 by Carl Pei (co-founder of OnePlus) on a mission to remove barriers between people and technology through intuitive, transparent design. Raised $7M seed (investors include Tony Fadell, Casey Neistat, Kevin Lin) and $15M Series A from GV (Google Ventures). Founding design partner: Teenage Engineering (Tom and Jesper). Known for a transparent/glyph-lit aesthetic combining retro industrial references with bold futurism. Products: ear (1), ear (2), ear (open) wireless earbuds; Phone (1) and Phone (2) smartphones.
nodes.cv: Founded 2020 by Carl Pei; first community funding round raised $1.5M in 54 seconds (fastest European crowdfunding project to $1M); founding partner Teenage Engineering; GV investment 2021; ear (1) StockX blind drop sold at $1,000+. Phone (1) and Phone (2) widely reviewed across tech press.

notableWork.label: ear (1) — transparent true wireless earbuds, first product (StockX blind drop 2021)
Phone (2) — glyph-interface smartphone with Teenage Engineering-influenced design
ear (open) — open-ear true wireless earbuds

nodes.name: Schiit Audio

location.raw: Corpus Christi / San Antonio, Texas, USA

nodes.bio: American audio equipment manufacturer founded in 2010 by Jason Stoddard and Mike Moffat (ex-Theta Digital), committed to no-compromise hi-fi at affordable prices — all designed and built in the USA. Products from $49 (SYS passive preamp) to $2,299 (Yggdrasil DAC) are built around proprietary technologies: Unison USB, True Multibit DAC, Nexus and Continuity discrete analog topologies. Line includes headphone amps (Magni, Vali, Valhalla, Lyr, Mjolnir), DACs (Modi, Bifrost, Gungnir, Yggdrasil), preamps (Saga, Freya), speaker amps (Vidar, Aegir, Tyr, Wotan), and phono stages. All Norse mythology naming.
nodes.cv: Founded 2010; full story documented in 200,000-word Head-Fi thread 'Schiit Happened' (also published as a book by Jason Stoddard); Yggdrasil a benchmark multi-bit DAC; Magni + Modi 'stack' became the de facto starter audiophile setup. Up to 5-year warranty. PCB manufacturing in Corpus Christi; assembly in San Antonio.

notableWork.label: Yggdrasil — flagship multi-bit DAC; benchmark in high-end digital audio
Magni + Modi 'stack' — iconic entry-level headphone amp + DAC combo
Bifrost — mid-level upgradeable DAC with user-swappable DAC cards (Forkbeard modular system)

nodes.name: Klipsch

location.raw: Hope, Arkansas (Heritage mfg.) / Indianapolis, Indiana (HQ), USA

nodes.bio: Legendary American loudspeaker company founded in 1946 in Hope, Arkansas by engineer Paul W. Klipsch in a tin shack behind a dry cleaner. Klipsch pioneered the corner-horn loudspeaker — using room boundaries as a folded horn to achieve exceptional efficiency with minimal amplifier power. Heritage series speakers (Klipschorn, La Scala, Belle Klipsch, Cornwall, Heresy) are still hand-assembled in Hope, Arkansas. Paul Klipsch sold the company to a second cousin in 1989 and died in Hope in 2002 at age 98. Now owned by Voxx International. Klipsch Museum of Audio History opened in Hope in 2021.
nodes.cv: Founded 1946 by Paul W. Klipsch; Heritage series hand-assembled in Hope, AR continuously since 1946; Klipschorn one of the longest in-production speakers in audio history; PWK inducted into Engineering and Science Hall of Fame (alongside Edison, Carver, Wright Brothers); Klipsch Museum of Audio History opened 2021.

notableWork.label: Klipschorn — corner horn loudspeaker, in continuous production since 1946
La Scala — large-format heritage horn speaker, hand-assembled in Hope, AR
Heresy — compact heritage horn speaker (most accessible in Heritage line)

nodes.name: Tish Weinstock
nodes.bio: British beauty editor, writer, and author. Oxford-educated (Art History), she worked at i-D, then as beauty features editor at Dazed, then contributing beauty editor at British Vogue from 2020. Known for a gothic-inflected sensibility and wide-ranging cultural coverage across fashion, beauty, and subculture.
nodes.cv: Contributing Editor, British Vogue; formerly Beauty Features Editor, Dazed; Editorial Assistant, i-D. Bylines: i-D, HuffPost, MoonProject. Interned at Tank, Garage, Vanity Fair, i-D.

notableWork.label: How to Be a Goth: Notes on Undead Style (book, 2024)
British Vogue beauty coverage

nodes.name: Rachel Tashjian
nodes.bio: One of the most influential fashion critics working today. Formerly GQ's first fashion critic and fashion news director at Harper's Bazaar; most recently fashion critic at The Washington Post's Style section before moving to CNN. Creator of Opulent Tips, an invite-only newsletter with a cult following for its chatty tone and pearls of fashion knowledge.
nodes.cv: Fashion Critic, CNN (2025–); Fashion Critic, Washington Post; Fashion News Director, Harper's Bazaar; GQ's first fashion critic. BoF 500. 2024 ASME nominee; 1st place Society for Features Journalism arts & culture criticism award (2024).

notableWork.label: Opulent Tips (invite-only newsletter)
Washington Post fashion criticism
Harper's Bazaar — profiles of Miuccia Prada, Maria Grazia Chiuri

nodes.name: Emily Sundberg
nodes.bio: Writer and newsletter author behind Feed Me, a daily Substack covering the spirit of enterprise — business culture, media, and the creative industries. Known for an insider's perspective, impeccable timing, and a distinctive, non-judgmental voice that still carries a clear point of view.
nodes.cv: Author of Feed Me (Substack). Bylines and editorial work across culture and media.

notableWork.label: Feed Me newsletter (readfeedme.com — daily business/culture Substack)

nodes.name: Laura Reilly
nodes.bio: Writer and editor behind Magasin, a fashion shopping newsletter on Substack with over 46,000 subscribers. Part store, part magazine, Magasin is known for its sharp editorial taste, fresh writing, and highly curated recommendations — a lovable blend of cool-friend energy and serious fashion knowledge.
nodes.cv: Author of Magasin (Substack, 46k+ subscribers).

notableWork.label: Magasin newsletter (magasin.ltd — fashion shopping newsletter)

nodes.name: Sean Monahan
nodes.bio: Cultural theorist, trend forecaster, and writer. Co-founder of K-HOLE, the influential trend forecasting collective that coined the term 'normcore' (2013). Writes 8Ball, a Substack newsletter on culture, trends, and tech with over 23,000 subscribers.
nodes.cv: Co-founder, K-HOLE trend forecasting collective (coined 'normcore', 2013). Author of 8Ball newsletter (Substack, 23k+ subscribers).

notableWork.label: 8Ball newsletter (8ball.report)
K-HOLE trend reports (2011–2016) — coined 'normcore'

nodes.name: Casey Lewis
nodes.bio: Writer and researcher behind After School, a Substack newsletter examining youth culture with over 87,000 subscribers. Described as 'the go-to newsletter for youth culture and consumerism' — essential reading for understanding Gen Z tastes, aesthetics, and consumer behaviour.
nodes.cv: Author of After School (Substack, 87k+ subscribers). Youth culture reporter and researcher.

notableWork.label: After School newsletter (afterschool.substack.com — youth culture)

nodes.name: Biz Sherbert
nodes.bio: Writer behind American Style, a Substack newsletter about 'what people are really wearing and why,' with over 11,000 subscribers. Focuses on the sociology and psychology of dress — grounded, observational, and culturally sharp.
nodes.cv: Author of American Style (Substack, 11k+ subscribers).

notableWork.label: American Style newsletter (bizsherbert.substack.com)

nodes.name: Derek Blasberg
nodes.bio: American writer, socialite, and fashion media personality. Over two decades chronicling fashion, art, and pop culture; the youngest ever Editor-at-Large at Harper's Bazaar. Inaugural host of CNN Style (from 2016). Author of the coffee table book Harper's BAZAAR: Models (2015). Writes the Substack 'AND ANOTHER THING' — a dispatch on where he's been, who he's seen, and what they were wearing.
nodes.cv: Editor-at-Large, Harper's Bazaar; 'Our Man on the Street', Vanity Fair; Editor, V and VMan; Host, CNN Style (2016–). Bylines in every international edition of Vanity Fair, Vogue, AD, Harper's Bazaar.

notableWork.label: AND ANOTHER THING (Substack)
CNN Style (host, 2016–)
Harper's BAZAAR: Models (book, 2015)

nodes.name: Austin Tedesco
nodes.bio: Writer and editorial executive. Formerly senior editor at ESPN and Head of Business Development at Substack; now Head of Growth at Every, the AI-native media company. Contributor to Passion of the Weiss and other culture outlets. Runs Bangers & Jams, a bi-coastal food recommendation blog.
nodes.cv: Head of Growth, Every.to; formerly Head of Business Development, Substack; Senior Editor, ESPN. Contributor: Passion of the Weiss, After School (Casey Lewis). Bangers & Jams (food blog).

notableWork.label: Every.to writing
Bangers & Jams (food blog, bangersandjams.com)

nodes.name: Thom Bettridge
nodes.bio: Editor-in-chief of i-D Magazine and writer on culture and marketing. Writes CONTENT, a Substack newsletter sharing ideas, rants, secrets, and interviews from the frontiers of culture and marketing — describing culture and commerce as 'all one thing called content.' Over 7,000 subscribers.
nodes.cv: Editor-in-Chief, i-D Magazine. Author of CONTENT newsletter (Substack, 7k+ subscribers). Previously editor and writer across culture and fashion media.

notableWork.label: CONTENT newsletter (content.substack.com)
i-D Magazine (Editor-in-Chief)

nodes.name: Emilie Friedlander
nodes.bio: Culture journalist, writer, and podcaster. Co-hosts The Culture Journalist podcast with Andrea Domanick — a Substack publication about the future of culture with over 6,000 subscribers. Formerly an editor at The FADER, covering music and culture.
nodes.cv: Co-host, The Culture Journalist podcast/Substack (with Andrea Domanick). Formerly Editor, The FADER.

notableWork.label: The Culture Journalist (podcast/Substack — theculturejournalist.substack.com)
The FADER (editor)

nodes.name: Delia Cai
nodes.bio: Media and culture journalist. Writes Deez Links, billed as 'the original media & culture links report' — a daily Substack newsletter with over 39,000 subscribers tracking the conversations and stories shaping media and internet culture.
nodes.cv: Author of Deez Links (Substack, 39k+ subscribers). Media and culture reporter.

notableWork.label: Deez Links newsletter (deezlinks.com — media & culture)

nodes.name: Joshua Citarella
nodes.bio: Artist, writer, and researcher whose practice centres on online communities, internet subcultures, and political aesthetics. Founded the platform Do Not Research; hosts the Doomscroll podcast. Graduate professor at RISD. His research into niche political Instagram communities (Politigram) was formative in documenting how political identity forms online.
nodes.cv: Graduate Professor, RISD. Founder, Do Not Research. Host, Doomscroll podcast. Author: Politigram & the Post-Left (2018), 20 Interviews (2020). Exhibited at The Armory Show (2017).

notableWork.label: Politigram & the Post-Left (book/research, 2018)
Do Not Research (platform)
Doomscroll podcast

nodes.name: Dwarkesh Patel
nodes.bio: Podcaster and writer known for deeply researched, long-form interviews with scientists, economists, historians, and technologists. The Dwarkesh Podcast has become one of the most respected intellectual interview shows, with over 84,000 Substack subscribers. Guests have included leading figures in AI, history, and economics.
nodes.cv: Host and author, Dwarkesh Podcast (Substack, 84k+ subscribers). Known for marathon-length, rigorously researched interviews.

notableWork.label: Dwarkesh Podcast (dwarkesh.com)

nodes.name: Taylor Lorenz
nodes.bio: Tech and internet culture journalist. Author of User Mag, a Substack publication with over 100,000 subscribers covering how the internet reshapes politics, business, media, and culture. Previously staff reporter at The Washington Post and The New York Times. Author of Extremely Online: The Untold Story of Fame, Influence, and Power on the Internet (2023).
nodes.cv: Author, User Mag (Substack, 100k+ subscribers). Formerly: Washington Post, New York Times. Author of Extremely Online (Simon & Schuster, 2023).

notableWork.label: User Mag (usermag.co — tech & internet culture)
Extremely Online (book, Simon & Schuster, 2023)
Washington Post / NYT reporting on influencer culture and internet trends

nodes.name: Matthew Schnipper
nodes.bio: Music writer and editor. Writes Deep Voices, a Substack of curated Spotify and Apple Music playlists paired with writing about music, with over 6,000 subscribers. Formerly editor-in-chief at The FADER and an editor at Dazed, with a long career covering music culture.
nodes.cv: Author, Deep Voices (Substack, 6k+ subscribers). Formerly Editor-in-Chief, The FADER; Editor, Dazed.

notableWork.label: Deep Voices newsletter (deepvoices.substack.com — music playlists + writing)
The FADER (Editor-in-Chief)

nodes.name: Shawn Reynaldo
nodes.bio: Electronic music journalist and industry commentator.
