Writes First Floor, a Substack newsletter about electronic music and the culture and industry surrounding it, with over 23,000 subscribers. Based in Berlin, with a career covering dance music, club culture, and the wider electronic ecosystem.
nodes.cv: Author, First Floor (Substack, 23k+ subscribers). Electronic music journalist and critic, Berlin.

notableWork.label: First Floor newsletter (firstfloor.substack.com — electronic music & industry)

nodes.name: Sam Valenti IV
nodes.bio: Music executive, writer, and curator. Founder of Ghostly International, the influential Ann Arbor-based electronic and experimental record label (est. 1999). Writes Herb Sundays, a Substack featuring weekly music playlists curated by a rotating cast of contributors — praised by Philip Sherburne as 'beautiful in both simplicity and execution.'
nodes.cv: Founder, Ghostly International (record label, est. 1999). Author, Herb Sundays (Substack, 12k+ subscribers).

notableWork.label: Herb Sundays newsletter (herbsundays.substack.com — curated music playlists)
Ghostly International (founder — electronic/experimental label)

nodes.name: Blackbird Spyplane
nodes.bio: Style, culture, and travel newsletter described as 'your No. 1 source for unbeatable recon.' Written by Jonah Weiner (contributing writer, New York Times Magazine; has written for The New Yorker, Rolling Stone, WIRED, WSJ.) and Erin Wylie (former design scout, Apple Industrial Design; former editor, Martha Stewart and Condé Nast). Launched May 2020; also publishes Concorde, a companion women's-leaning offshoot.
nodes.cv: Jonah Weiner: Contributing writer, NYT Magazine; bylines at The New Yorker, Rolling Stone, WIRED, WSJ. Erin Wylie: Former design scout, Apple Industrial Design; former magazine editor, Condé Nast / Martha Stewart.

notableWork.label: Blackbird Spyplane newsletter (blackbirdspyplane.com — style, culture, travel)
Concorde (companion newsletter)

nodes.name: Alex Heath
nodes.bio: Senior tech journalist and editor at The Verge, where he has served as Deputy Editor and Senior Reporter covering artificial intelligence. His award-winning reporting on AI and Big Tech has been cited in congressional hearings and covered in the NYT and on Saturday Night Live. Author of Sources, an independent Substack newsletter chronicling how AI is upending Silicon Valley. Co-host of ACCESS, a weekly podcast on the tech industry's inside conversation.
nodes.cv: Deputy Editor / Senior Reporter (AI), The Verge. Author, Sources newsletter (Substack — #63 in Technology). Co-host, ACCESS podcast (Vox Media). Has interviewed Sam Altman and other leading tech figures.

notableWork.label: Sources newsletter (substack.com/@alexeheath — AI & tech industry)
ACCESS podcast (co-host, Vox Media)
The Verge — AI and Big Tech reporting

nodes.name: Talkhouse
nodes.bio: Webby Award-winning digital media outlet, podcast network, and production company founded in 2013 by Ian Wheeler and Tim Putnam. Talkhouse publishes first-person essays, criticism, and conversations by musicians, actors, and filmmakers — peers talking to peers. Known for landmark pieces including Lou Reed reviewing Kanye West's Yeezus and Ezra Koenig reviewing Drake. In 2025, expanded into film production, executive producing Björk's Cornucopia film for Apple TV+.
nodes.cv: Founded 2013 by Ian Wheeler and Tim Putnam. Webby Award winner. Talkhouse Podcast (Apple Podcasts / Spotify). Talkhouse Live events. Podcast network (expanded early 2020s). Film production (2025–): exec. produced Björk's Cornucopia (Apple TV+).

notableWork.label: Talkhouse Podcast (artists in conversation with peers)
Björk: Cornucopia — Apple Music Live film (exec. produced, 2025)
Lou Reed reviews Kanye West's Yeezus; Ezra Koenig reviews Drake's Nothing Was the Same

nodes.name: Natasha Advani
nodes.bio: Founder of Not/Applicable Vintage and brand/buying consultant. Formerly Head of Buying at Selfridges & Co (UK), where she helped usher in the merging of luxury and streetwear on the sales floor. Relocated to LA in 2014 and launched N/A with a curated vintage pop-up at Selfridges London. Specialises in rare vintage music tour T-shirts and sweatshirts — pop culture commemoratives from the 80s, 90s, and early 2000s, sourced across the USA. Clients include Travis Scott, Cardi B, and Lil Uzi Vert.
nodes.cv: Founder, Not/Applicable Vintage (LA). Former Head of Buying, Selfridges & Co; started at Harvey Nichols 2006. Brand and buying consultant for emerging luxury labels. Sources vintage wardrobe and merch for music artists.

notableWork.label: Not/Applicable Vintage (@itsnotapplicable) — rare vintage music tour tees and pop culture merch
Selfridges London vintage pop-up (launched N/A)
Vintage sourcing for artists incl. Travis Scott, Cardi B, Lil Uzi Vert

nodes.name: Colleen Allen
nodes.bio: New York-based fashion designer. Studied menswear at Central Saint Martins before joining The Row as a menswear designer for three years. Founded her eponymous label in 2023; debut collection (February 2024) was immediately picked up by SSENSE and earned coverage in Vogue and the New York Times Magazine. Known for womenswear that plays with asymmetry, deconstruction, and comfort-led functionality. Worn by Charli xcx, Paloma Elsesser, and Ayo Edebiri.
nodes.cv: Founder, Colleen Allen (est. 2023/24). Formerly menswear designer, The Row (3 years). Education: Central Saint Martins. Stockists: SSENSE, Moda Operandi. Profiled in W Magazine, Vogue, NYT Magazine.

notableWork.label: Debut FW24 collection — purchased by SSENSE; covered in Vogue + NYT Magazine
Worn by Charli xcx, Paloma Elsesser, Ayo Edebiri
FW26 collection (most recent)

nodes.name: August Barron
nodes.bio: Paris-based Norwegian-American design duo — married partners and co-founders of August Barron (formerly All-In). Vestbø is Norwegian; Barron is a New Yorker and Bard College graduate who founded the magazine All-In in New York in 2015; they met at its launch party. The label debuted its first collection in 2019, with Maryam Nassir Zadeh as the first stockist. Known for collections that recontextualize existing garments through fantastical, heightened constructions of femininity — beauty queens, pop stars, debutantes, bondage housewives. LVMH Prize 2025 finalists. Dazed Winter 2025 cover story. Collaborate with Norwegian music duo Smerz on show soundtracks.
nodes.cv: LVMH Prize 2025 finalists. Dazed cover (Winter 2025). Debuted at Maryam Nassir Zadeh. Stockists: Dover Street Market Paris, Voo Store, Jentestore, SSENSE. Fashion magazine founded 2015 (as All-In). First collection 2019.

notableWork.label: SS26 'Real Housewife' collection — Dazed cover, Winter 2025
LVMH Prize 2025 finalists
Smerz collaboration (show soundtracks); 'Allina' fictional pop star project (with Smerz EP)

nodes.name: Women's History Museum
nodes.bio: New York fashion label and art collective co-founded by Amanda McGowan and Mattie Barringer in 2015. The two met at NYU — McGowan studying journalism, Barringer cinema studies — while sharing a Chinatown apartment. Rather than traditional runway shows, they stage biennial exhibitions and institutional collaborations, presenting work at Company Gallery, the Ukrainian National Home, and Amant (Brooklyn). Joined the official NYFW calendar in 2024 after a decade of off-schedule work. Physical shop at 244 Canal Street, 3rd floor, New York.
nodes.cv: Co-founded 2015 by Amanda McGowan and Mattie Barringer. Biennial launched 2019. Official NYFW debut 2024. Exhibited: Company Gallery, Ukrainian National Home, Amant (Brooklyn, 2025). Shop: 244 Canal Street, 3rd floor, NYC. Covered in The Face, i-D, Office Magazine, Interview Magazine, Cultured.

notableWork.label: Official NYFW debut, SS25 (2024)
'Grisette à l'enfer' — Amant exhibition, Brooklyn (2025)
Women's History Museum Biennial (launched 2019, Company Gallery)

nodes.name: James Boulter
nodes.bio: Agent at United Talent Agency (UTA) specialising in brand partnerships, endorsements, and commercial deal-making. Has delivered over $200M in deals for clients, spanning brand equity agreements, licensing, and venture launches. Widely regarded in Hollywood and the luxury sector as a key architect of high-value talent-brand partnerships. Brother of British tennis player Katie Boulter.
nodes.cv: Agent, United Talent Agency (UTA) — Endorsements & Brand Partnerships. Over $200M in client deals secured. Featured in the Financial Times on luxury and endorsement deal-making.

notableWork.label: United Talent Agency — brand endorsements & partnerships

nodes.name: Oscar Ouyang
nodes.bio: Beijing-born, London-based knitwear designer, co-founded with business partner Yibo Chen (based in Shanghai). Studied BA and MA at Central Saint Martins (graduated 2023), where he specialised in knitwear. Known for story-driven, maximalist knitwear blending British tailoring heritage — safari jackets, Fair Isle, Donegal tweeds, military details — with a campy, preppy sensibility and nerdy craft (including an antique 1987 Dubied knitting machine). BFC Newgen recipient. Stockists include Dover Street Market globally, Selfridges, H-Town (London), and Shibuya (Tokyo). Formerly interned at Proenza Schouler and Asai.
nodes.cv: BFC Newgen. MA Central Saint Martins 2023. Co-founded with Yibo Chen. Interned: Proenza Schouler, Asai, Harper's Bazaar China. Stockists: Dover Street Market (London, Tokyo, NY, Paris, Singapore), Selfridges, H-Town. Included in Fashion Asia Hong Kong (FAHK). LFW participant.

notableWork.label: BFC Newgen recognition
AW26 'chic afters' collection — LFW (2026)
Dover Street Market global stockist; Selfridges London

nodes.name: Matière Noire
nodes.bio: Paris-based spatial design, light design, and scenography studio co-founded in 2019 by Pierre Dagba and Felix Ward. Merges architecture, light, and technology to create immersive environments spanning fashion shows, music events, and art installations. Clients include Chanel, Courrèges, Y-3, Hermès, MM6, Fondation Cartier, and Moncler.
nodes.cv: Founded 2019. BoF 500. First beneficiary of L'Incubateur by Alexandre de Betak / The Independents (2025). Address: 20 rue Saint Sauveur, 75002 Paris. Email: contact@matiere-noire.paris.

notableWork.label: Chanel Haute Horlogerie 2026 scenography
Ongoing runway set and light design for Courrèges (SS24–SS26) and Y-3 (AW24–AW26); MM6 (ongoing)
Fondation Cartier 40-year anniversary installation; Keinemusik Tempelhof; Bad Gyal Más Cara tour

nodes.name: Cora Delaney
nodes.bio: Founder and CEO of EYC Ltd, a London-based 360° agency specialising in influencer and talent management, brand partnerships, creative consultancy, and event planning. Built EYC organically from early career work managing talent and styling (Lily Allen), growing it into a culturally influential firm bridging global luxury brands with next-generation creators. Named to Vogue Business 2025 Top 100 Innovators and the Evening Standard Progress 1000.
nodes.cv: Founded EYC Ltd. Atlantic Records joint-venture EYC Records imprint. Vogue Business 2025 Top 100 Innovators. Evening Standard Progress 1000. Co-owner of Badwater RTD tequila brand. Clients include Louis Vuitton, Gucci, Prada, Bentley, Samsung, Depop.

notableWork.label: Atlantic Records joint-venture EYC Records imprint — incubator for emerging talent
Louis Vuitton, Gucci, Prada brand campaigns — talent casting and creative strategy
Vogue Business 2025 Top 100 Innovators

nodes.name: Torishéju Dumi
nodes.bio: British-Nigerian-Brazilian fashion designer and founder of eponymous label Torishéju. BA in menswear from London College of Fashion; MA from Central Saint Martins (2021). Designs blend Nigerian mysticism, traditional lappa garments, and British tailoring through sleek suiting, dresses, and wrap silhouettes. Mentored by Vogue editor Gabriella Karefa-Johnson; interned under Phoebe Philo at Celine.
nodes.cv: MA Central Saint Martins 2021. Dover Street Market Paris Brand Development Program. January 2024 British Vogue cover (with Priya Ahluwalia and Tolu Coker). Met Museum acquired debut collection pieces for Sleeping Beauties exhibition (2024). Met Gala 2025 (Kendall Jenner). UAL Honorary.

notableWork.label: "Mami Wata" debut collection (2023) — pieces acquired by the Met Museum for Sleeping Beauties exhibition (2024)
Paris Fashion Week debut FW23 — "Fire on the Mountain", opened by Naomi Campbell, closed by Paloma Elsesser; only Black woman to debut at PFW that season
Zendaya wore Torishéju to Dune: Part Two Mexico premiere (styled by Law Roach, 2024); Kendall Jenner at Met Gala 2025

nodes.name: Steve O Smith
nodes.bio: London-based fashion designer whose practice begins with drawing. Using fabric appliqué and creative construction developed during his MA at Central Saint Martins (2022), he transforms hand-drawn gestures and mark-making into wearable objects — treating pattern cutting, scissors, and stitch as drawing tools. Born in Amsterdam, raised in the UK; BA from Rhode Island School of Design.
nodes.cv: BA Rhode Island School of Design. MA Central Saint Martins 2022. 2025 LVMH Prize Karl Lagerfeld Award recipient. London Fashion Week. Dressed Harry Styles (stylist Harry Lambert), Cate Blanchett, and Eddie Redmayne. Featured in W Magazine, AnOther, Cultured, 10 Magazine.

notableWork.label: Harry Styles "Daylight" music video — black-and-white figure suit (stylist Harry Lambert, 2022)
2025 LVMH Prize Karl Lagerfeld Award
Dressed Cate Blanchett and Eddie Redmayne; Celeste-approved (Perfect Magazine)

nodes.name: Bubu Ogisi
nodes.bio: Nigerian fibre artist, multidisciplinary designer, and founder/creative director of IAMISIGO — a slow design house preserving Africa's intangible cultural heritage through handmade wearable art. Grew up across London, Lagos, Accra, and Paris; holds a BS in Computer Science (Regent University, Accra) and MA in Fashion Business from ESMOD Paris. Integrates ancestral textile techniques, upcycled materials, and circular design principles to interrogate post-colonialism, identity, gender, and ecological futures.
nodes.cv: BS Computer Science, Regent University Accra. MA Fashion Business, ESMOD Paris. LVMH Prize 2026 winner. Zalando Visionary Award 2025. Copenhagen Fashion Week SS26. V&A Afrika Fashion exhibition (2022). Barbican Dirty Looks (2025). Ars Electronica 2024. Member of art collective hFACTOR. BoF, Vogue US/UK, NYT, CNN coverage.

notableWork.label: Afrika Fashion, Victoria & Albert Museum, London (2022); work held in V&A, Barbican, and Fondation d'Entreprise Martell collections
LVMH Prize 2026 winner; Zalando Visionary Award 2025; Copenhagen Fashion Week SS26 debut
Victoria's Secret VS20 House of Lagos campaign; Dirty Looks: Desire and Decay in Fashion, Barbican (2025); Ars Electronica 2024

nodes.name: Kyle Smith
nodes.bio: The NFL's first-ever fashion editor, appointed in 2024. Bridges professional football and the global fashion industry by connecting players with luxury brands, helping plan custom social content, and shaping player personal brands through style. Previously a wardrobe assistant at NFL Media (2019) and an intern with celebrity stylist Karla Welch and brand Amiri.
nodes.cv: NFL's first Fashion Editor (2024). BoF 500. Wardrobe assistant NFL Media (2019). Interned with Karla Welch and Amiri. Featured in Washington Post, ESPN, Interview Magazine, Sharp Magazine.

notableWork.label: NFL Fashion Editor role — first of its kind in major US sports (2024)
Joe Burrow Paris Fashion Week preparation and styling
BoF 500 recognition; Washington Post and ESPN profiles (2025–26)

nodes.name: Streeters
nodes.bio: Leading global artist management agency representing talent in fashion and beauty, founded in London in 1988. Represents stylists, photographers, hair and makeup artists, set designers, casting directors, choreographers, and creative directors. Part of Great Bowery — a collective of luxury image-maker agencies with offices in London and New York.
nodes.cv: Founded London, 1988. Part of Great Bowery (est. 2015), alongside CLM, Trunk Archive, and others. Represents key talent across styling, hair, makeup, set design, casting, and multi-media. Works with Gucci, Dior, Balenciaga and other leading luxury brands.

notableWork.label: Styling division — represents top fashion and editorial stylists globally
Set design and casting roster — spanning luxury fashion campaigns and editorial
Part of Great Bowery collective alongside CLM, Trunk Archive, Bowie, and Gallery Stock

nodes.name: Iñigo Awewave
nodes.bio: Photographer working across fashion, beauty, and still life, born in San Sebastián and based in Paris. Guided by a graphic sensibility and instinctive sense of balance, his images merge intuition and precision, emotion and structure — revealing fashion as a state of feeling rather than mere appearance. Represented by Artlist Paris.
nodes.cv: Represented by Artlist Paris. Clients include Byredo, Loewe, MM6 Maison Margiela, Dries Van Noten, Rabanne, Missoni, Paloma Wool, Rhode. Editorial in AnOther Magazine, Harper's Bazaar France, T Magazine, D la Repubblica. Frequent collaborator with stylists Harry Lambert and Robbie Spencer.

nodes.name: Karl Felix
nodes.bio: London-based photographer and creative consultant working across fashion, beauty, and music. Develops holistic visual strategies encompassing creative direction, image consulting, and branding. His work moves fluidly between artistic vision and strategic thinking, shaping not just single images but entire visual worlds for brands and musicians.
nodes.cv: Clients include Off-White, Slam Jam, GOAT, Highsnobiety, Zalando, Reebok LTD, Camper, Just Cavalli, Samsøe Samsøe, Tinder. Publications include AnOther Man, Numéro, Numéro Art, Gruppe Magazine, Rouge Fashion Book, Replica Man, Dry Clean Only.

nodes.name: Léonard A. Bougault
nodes.bio: Paris-based set designer with a background in architecture. Born in Brittany (1992), graduate of Bauhaus University Weimar and Paris-Est. Practice spans fashion set design, scenography for theatre, and spatial exploration — his work examines the dynamic relationship between space and the body, often through illusion and trompe-l'œil, drawing inspiration from everyday materials reinterpreted in abstract ways.
nodes.cv: Architecture background. Graduate of Bauhaus University Weimar and Paris-Est. Represented by White Dot agency. Nominated for The Fashion Awards New Wave: Creatives. Collaborated with photographers Paolo Roversi, Willy Vanderperre, Charlotte Stouvenot, Julien Martinez Leclerc, Kito Muñoz, and Alessio Bolzoni.

notableWork.label: Set design for Arena Homme + Issue 65 (2026) and POP Magazine Issue 54

location.raw: São Paulo, Brazil / Los Angeles, USA

Contributor to Vogue Brasil, Vogue Italia, Elle UK, Elle Brasil, CR Fashion Book, and SSAW. Has photographed Jodie Comer, Greta Gerwig, Fernanda Torres, Hari Nef, Kat Graham, and Caroline Trentini.

nodes.bio: UK-based Nigerian fashion journalist and content creator known as Fashion Roadman. BA Fashion Journalism (First Class), Central Saint Martins (2023). Published in Vogue, GQ, Business of Fashion, Mr Porter, and System Magazine. Runs a YouTube channel (141K+ subscribers) covering fashion history, runway analysis, and industry discourse. Named in Vogue Business 100 Innovators (2022).

nodes.name: Pedro Napolinário
nodes.bio: Self-taught photographer born in Rio de Janeiro, based in São Paulo. Celebrated for his sensitive, celebratory lens on Brazil's Black youth and suburban culture — drawing from Rio's carnival, urban codes, and Afro-diasporic visual language. Dubbed one of Latin America's new wave fashion photographers by the Business of Fashion. Established a collaborative studio in 2020 with a makeup artist and stylist.
nodes.cv: Self-taught. Dazed100 2024. Latin American Fashion Awards nominee. BoF recognised as one of Latin America's new wave fashion photographers. Published in Vogue Brasil, Elle, Harper's Bazaar, Dazed, Nataal. Brand clients include Farm, Lacoste, Nike, Adidas, Netflix, Havaianas, Johnnie Walker.

nodes.name: Roxane Dia
nodes.bio: French-born casting director of Afro Caribbean descent, based between Berlin and New York. Rooted in skate, graffiti, and hip hop culture from her adolescent years in Germany — where she began casting friends from her community and connecting with unique characters on the street. Her practice focuses on models, celebrity talent, and street casting, finding beauty that transcends race, age, religion, culture, and socio-economic status. Represented by Webber.
nodes.cv: Represented by Webber (Berlin/New York). Clients include AnOther, Dazed, Diesel, Luncheon, Nike, POP, Rimowa, SSENSE, T Magazine, The Frankie Shop, SSAW, Aesther Ekme, Re-Edition, Arena Homme+. Collaborators include Mel Bles, Gabriel Moses, Chris Rhodes, Davit Giorgadze, Pegah Farahmand.

Crafts casting visions that fuse cultural nuance and editorial edge, with an eye for faces that feel cinematic and story-driven. Scouts through agencies, mother agents, and street casting. Editorial credits include L'Officiel Homme, Odda Magazine, Heroine, AnOther Man, HERO, and Dazed. Brand work includes Calvin Klein.
nodes.cv: Casting director. Credits include L'Officiel Homme, Odda Magazine, Heroine, AnOther Man, HERO, Dazed, Wonderland, Centrefold, Rollacoaster, Numero Homme. Brand work includes Calvin Klein. nodes.bio: New York-based casting director and co-founder (with Walter Pearce) of Midland Casting, established 2016. Represented by Art+Partner. nodes.name: Tuba Avalon
nodes.bio: Paris-based fashion commentator, YouTuber, and writer — self-described "fashion psychic." Previously studied economics and business administration, founded handbag label The Lezul, and worked as a product manager at a French luxury marketing company. SHOWstudio contributor.

nodes.name: America Korban
nodes.bio: Fashion editor and stylist based in New York. Fashion Editor at The Face (UK), known for gothic and cinematic editorial aesthetics. Also credits at Novembre Magazine, HommeGirls, Carcy Magazine, and Interview Magazine. Works across fashion editorial, covers, and advertising.
nodes.cv: Fashion Editor, The Face. Cover credits: Novembre Magazine #17 (2025), HommeGirls Volume 13 (2025), Carcy Magazine SS2025 & SS2026. Editorial: Interview Magazine, The Face. Show styling: Gabe Gordon FW26.

notableWork.label: The Face — 'I contain multitudes' editorial (Mila Rowyszyn / Olivia Drozdz, Winter 2025)
Novembre Magazine #17 2025 Cover (with photographer Valentin Herfray)
Interview Magazine — 'A Feral Existence' (Teresa Ciocia, May 2026)

nodes.name: Becky Akinyode
nodes.bio: Brooklyn-based Nigerian American stylist and creative consultant. Full name Oluwabukola Becky Akinyode. Georgetown University graduate. Career began interning with Patti Wilson; built an editorial and celebrity practice spanning Vogue, i-D, Purple, Dazed, Interview, Allure, Harper's Bazaar, T Magazine, Essence, and Citizen Magazine. Founder of OBA Fashion Ile.
nodes.cv: Founder, OBA Fashion Ile. Key credits: Vogue Summer 2026 (Deb & Somah Haaland); i-D FW25 cover (Teyana Taylor); Purple Magazine #41 cover; Sha'Carri Richardson for Essence; Ayo Edebiri for Citizen Magazine cover; Amandla Stenberg for Interview; Erykah Badu x SSENSE; Alek Wek for Citizen; JT for Interview; Dazed x Chanel; T Magazine; Cultured Mag (Chloe Sevigny x Roe Ethridge); Harper's Bazaar; Allure; CR Men's; GQ Middle East; Garage (Kelsey Lu); Crack Magazine (KeiyaA).

notableWork.label: i-D FW25 cover — Teyana Taylor
Vogue Summer 2026 — Deb & Somah Haaland
Ayo Edebiri for Citizen Magazine cover

nodes.name: Dani Bastidas
nodes.bio: Colombian photographer based in Paris. Works across editorial, advertising, and portraiture with a spare, intimate visual sensibility. Clients include Arena Homme+, Beat Magazine, Ray-Ban, Levi's, Port Tanger, and Justine Clenquet.
nodes.cv: Arena Homme+ #62 cover (Winter 2024/Spring 2025). Beat Magazine #36 SS24 (Sega Bodega). Ray-Ban lookbook (2026). Port Tanger Filotimo campaign (2024). Levi's Back in Blue FW23. Models.com editorial portraits.

notableWork.label: Arena Homme+ #62 Winter 2024/Spring 2025 — cover shoot
Sega Bodega for Beat Magazine #36 SS24
Ray-Ban 'Essentials' lookbook (2026); Port Tanger Filotimo campaign (2024)

nodes.name: Diego Bendezu
nodes.bio: Peruvian-born, New York–based photographer whose work documents the dignity, culture, and resilience of Latin American and immigrant communities. BFA in Photography from Fashion Institute of Technology (2015). Assisted Juergen Teller and Alexi Lubomirski. 2026 Vilcek Prize for Creative Promise in Fashion & Culture. US representation by Silver Tooth.
nodes.cv: 2026 Vilcek Prize for Creative Promise in Fashion & Culture. Vogue Mexico & Latin America 25th anniversary cover (Young Miko, 2024). Covers: Allure, L'Officiel Homme, Man About Town (2025). Ongoing collaboration with Willy Chavarria (campaigns, lookbooks, Kendrick Lamar Super Bowl LIX collection). Commercial: Don Julio, Adidas, Puma, Merrell. Editorial: i-D, GQ, Harper's Bazaar, The Face. Breakthrough project: Dear Lima (2023 photo essay, Venezuelan immigrants in Peru). First book (2026): documentation of traditional Peruvian/South American dances.

notableWork.label: Willy Chavarria — ongoing campaign and lookbook collaboration (incl. Kendrick Lamar Super Bowl LIX collection)
Vogue Mexico & Latin America 25th anniversary cover — Young Miko (2024)
Dear Lima (2023) — photo essay documenting Venezuelan immigrants at a Lima carwash

nodes.name: Noah Johnson
nodes.bio: New York-based writer and editor. Editor-in-Chief of Highsnobiety (from January 2025). MFA in Creative Writing from Columbia University. Career spans fashion, culture, and style journalism across print and digital.
nodes.cv: Editor-in-Chief, Highsnobiety (2025–present). Previously: Global Style Director, GQ. Editor positions at Details, Style.com, Complex. Writing published in The New York Times, New York Magazine, The Wall Street Journal.

notableWork.label: Highsnobiety — Editor-in-Chief (2025–present)
GQ — Global Style Director
Details; Style.com; Complex

nodes.name: Laila Gohar
nodes.bio: Egyptian-born, New York-based artist and creative director who works with food as her primary medium. Designs unique eating experiences, edible installations, and pop-ups in conjunction with art, design, and fashion events globally. Co-founder of Gohar World (with sister Nadia Gohar, 2022), a tableware and lifestyle brand. Contributing writer at FT HTSI ('How To Host It' column). Represented by Nevermind Agency.
nodes.cv: Co-founder, Gohar World (2022–present). Contributing writer, FT HTSI. Collaborations: Prada, Hermès, Comme des Garçons, Simone Rocha, Sotheby's. Work shown in museums and galleries worldwide. Represented by Nevermind Agency.

notableWork.label: Gohar World — tableware and lifestyle brand co-founded with Nadia Gohar (2022)
Prada, Hermès, Comme des Garçons, Simone Rocha — event and experiential collaborations
FT HTSI — 'How To Host It' column

nodes.name: Bianca Saunders
nodes.bio: British-Jamaican fashion designer and founder of her eponymous menswear label (est. 2017). MA from the Royal College of Art. Reframes menswear through a diasporic lens — culturally layered, emotionally resonant tailoring that challenges traditional codes of masculinity. First Black woman to win the ANDAM Grand Prix (2021).
nodes.cv: Founded Bianca Saunders label (2017). BFC 'One to Watch' (2018). BFC NEWGEN (2019). Dazed 100 (2020). Forbes 30 Under 30: Arts & Culture (2020). ANDAM Grand Prix winner (2021, first Black woman; €300K prize + Balenciaga/Cédric Charbit mentorship). LVMH Prize shortlist (2021). Debut solo runway, Palais de Tokyo, Paris (AW22). BoF 500 (2023). BFC New Establishment Menswear Award, British Fashion Awards (2023). BFC/GQ Designer Fashion Fund (2024).

notableWork.label: ANDAM Grand Prix 2021 — first Black woman to win
AW22 'A Stretch' — debut solo runway, Palais de Tokyo, Paris
BFC New Establishment Menswear Award, British Fashion Awards 2023

nodes.name: Lucien Pagès & AIPR
nodes.bio: London-based global PR and communications agency specialising in luxury fashion, beauty, fragrance, lifestyle, events, VIP, and digital. Founded by Lucien Pagès. One of the leading fashion PR agencies in Europe with a roster spanning heritage houses, independent designers, and emerging labels.
nodes.cv: Clients include: Acne Studios, ALAÏA, A.P.C., Carhartt WIP, Chopova Lowena, JW Anderson, Kiko Kostadinov, LOEWE, Moncler, Simone Rocha, S.S.DALEY, Stefan Cooke, Supriya Lele, Thom Browne, Rabanne, Le Labo, Margaret Howell, Goldwin, Wooyoungmi, Av Vattev, 16Arlington.

nodes.name: Purple PR
nodes.bio: Global communications agency for luxury brands and innovation, covering fashion, beauty, lifestyle, events, VIP, and digital. Offices in London, New York, Los Angeles, Miami, Hong Kong, Singapore, and Dubai. Part of Together Group. One of the most established names in luxury fashion PR.

nodes.name: Café Forgot
nodes.bio: New York independent fashion boutique and retail concept founded in 2017 by Lucy Weisner and Vita Haas (childhood friends, Reed College art history graduates). Started as a roving pop-up, now with a permanent location at 29 Ludlow Street, Chinatown. Approx. 90% of stock is one-of-a-kind or handmade. Champions emerging and indie designers using design as art practice — natural dye, upcycling, hand-knit, hand-painted. Gateway platform for labels like Marland Backus, Sherris, and Blobb.
nodes.cv: Founded 2017 by Lucy Weisner and Vita Haas. Permanent location: 29 Ludlow Street, Chinatown, NYC. Key designers carried: Marland Backus, Sherris, Blobb, Rebekah Bide, Claire McKinney, Sophie Andes Gascon. Featured in WWD, Interview Magazine, Document Journal, Fashionista.

notableWork.label: Interview Magazine — 'Cafe Forgot Is Healing New York's Retail Landscape'
WWD — 'Opening Ceremony Is Closing, Here Comes Café Forgot'
Document Journal — founding profile (2019)

nodes.name: The Independents
nodes.bio: Global marketing, design, and communications group for luxury and lifestyle brands. An umbrella collective of specialist partner agencies sharing common values around innovation, excellence, and creative collaboration. Brands described as 'brand stewards for the next generation of leaders.'
nodes.cv: Collective includes: Karla Otto (PR), Bureau Betak (events/shows), Lucien Pagès Communication, Kennedy (branding), INCA Productions, Prodject, Kitten Production, Sunshine, 2x4, Bureau Béatrice, Bureau Future, CTZAR, k2, Lefty (influencer), Atelier Athem, Atelier Lum, Kitty Events, The Qode, Terminal 9 Studios, Cadence Films, Cadence Image, WE ARE ONA, MA+Group.

nodes.name: Atelier Athem
nodes.bio: Paris-based scenography and design studio specialising in façade video mapping, experiential pop-ups, retail activations, and immersive brand installations. Part of The Independents group. Works at the intersection of architecture, light, and luxury brand storytelling — animating landmark buildings and retail environments across Paris and globally.
nodes.cv: Part of The Independents group. Clients: Louis Vuitton, Givenchy, ALAÏA, Cartier, Chaumet, Burberry, Valentino, Guerlain, Van Cleef & Arpels, Lacoste, Nike, Sotheby's, Yves Saint Laurent, Rabanne, Palm Angels, H&M, Swarovski, Tiffany & Co, Dior, Boucheron, Sephora, Jaeger-LeCoultre, Tommy Hilfiger. nodes.name: 2x4
nodes.bio: New York and Beijing-based brand strategy, content, and design systems studio. Works across branding, identity, campaigns, environments, exhibitions, and digital for clients spanning fashion, culture, and institutions. Part of The Independents group. Known for conceptually rigorous visual systems at the intersection of art, fashion, and culture.

nodes.bio: Paris and Dubai-based agency specialising in designing and producing exclusive fashion and luxury experiences, elevated scenography, and architectural projects. Founded 2009. Part of The Independents group. Covers events, retail, interior architecture, dinners, shows, showrooms, pop-ups, and window displays across fashion, fine jewellery, perfumery, watchmaking, and lifestyle.
nodes.cv: Part of The Independents group. nodes.bio: Dubai-based luxury multimedia and creative technology company founded 2022. Part of The Independents group. Specialises in immersive experience design, projection mapping, live shows, and digital content for luxury and lifestyle brands globally. Operates across UAE, KSA, GCC, France, and international markets.

nodes.name: Bureau Betak
nodes.bio: Global fashion show and event production agency founded in 1990 by Alexandre de Betak. One of the most influential show production houses in the industry, responsible for over 900 productions across fashion, beauty, and luxury. Part of The Independents group. Known for emotionally resonant, conceptually driven shows and a commitment to sustainable production practices.
nodes.cv: Founded 1990 by Alexandre de Betak. 900+ productions globally. Offices: Paris, New York, Los Angeles, Shanghai. Clients: Christian Dior, Jacquemus, Yves Saint Laurent, Kenzo, Michael Kors, Isabel Marant, Rodarte, Viktor & Rolf, Lacoste, The Row, Jason Wu, Carven, Helmut Lang, Diane von Furstenberg, Victoria's Secret, Donna Karan, Hussein Chalayan, Anthony Vaccarello, Tiffany & Co. nodes.name: CTZAR
nodes.bio: Paris-based social-first strategic and creative agency powered by an in-house production studio. Part of The Independents group. Specialises in social media strategy, content production, and influencer marketing for luxury and lifestyle brands — blending culture-driven storytelling with scroll-stopping content across all platforms.
nodes.cv: Part of The Independents group. Services: social media strategy and management, content production (hero films to bite-sized assets), influencer casting and activation. Clients: Louis Vuitton, Dior, Cartier, Loewe, Valentino, Guerlain, Rolex, Vogue, Christie's, Adidas, Breitling, Zadig & Voltaire, Clarins, La Samaritaine, Sofitel, Aubade.

nodes.name: Chaotic Good Projects
nodes.bio: Boutique digital marketing agency co-founded by former music managers Andrew Spelman and Jesse Coren. Specialises in social media narrative campaigns and 'trend simulation' for musicians — studying organic TikTok behaviour and recreating it at scale through curated networks of creators and UGC. Gained significant industry attention in 2026 following a viral Substack exposé and coverage in Wired, The Guardian, and Gizmodo examining its 'fake fan' and narrative manipulation tactics.
nodes.cv: Services: UGC (user-generated content), Fanpage management, Brands and Media, Narrative Campaigns. Clients include: Geese, Oklou, Mk.gee, Wet Leg, Dijon, Sombr, Alex Warren, Zara Larsson, Tame Impala, Coldplay, Justin Bieber, Dua Lipa. 2026 controversy sparked by Eliza McLamb Substack post, amplified by Wired and The Guardian.

notableWork.label: Geese campaign — centre of 2026 'fake fans' controversy (Wired, Guardian)
Oklou; Mk.gee; Wet Leg; Dijon — narrative and UGC campaigns
Zara Larsson; Tame Impala; Dua Lipa — established act campaigns

nodes.name: k2
nodes.bio: Global creative partner specialising in brand experiences, retail, and executive productions across cultures, formats, and continents. Part of The Independents group. nodes.name: Karla Otto
nodes.bio: Full-service brand-building agency founded in 1982 by German-born former model Karla Otto. Headquartered in Milan, with offices across 14 cities including Paris, London, Munich, New York, LA, Hong Kong, Beijing, Shanghai, Tokyo, Seoul, Dubai, Riyadh, and Jeddah. A founding member of The Independents group. Specialises in fashion, beauty, design, lifestyle, and arts & culture — spanning strategy, PR, creative, talent, experiences, and events.
nodes.cv: Founded 1982 by Karla Otto (former runway model, ex-press dept at Elio Fiorucci). Now handles PR for 70+ brands globally. Founding member of The Independents. PR Net 100, 2024. Offices: Milan, Paris, London, Munich, New York, LA, Hong Kong, Beijing, Shanghai, Tokyo, Seoul, Dubai, Riyadh, Jeddah.

notableWork.label: Long-term PR for Prada, Marni, Jean-Paul Gaultier, Jil Sander, Celine, Dior, Givenchy, Simone Rocha

nodes.name: Kennedy
nodes.bio: London-based creative agency founded by Jan Kennedy in 2000, specialising in the conceptualisation and realisation of brand, cultural, and entertainment projects. Known for creative agility and executional precision, working with preeminent creatives from fashion, art, music, architecture, and film. Part of The Independents group. Active across North America, Europe, the Middle East, and Asia for over two decades.
nodes.cv: Founded 2000 by Jan Kennedy (ex-board director Freud Communications; co-owner with Damien Hirst of Pharmacy restaurant and Quo Vadis; co-owner of We Love Space Ibiza). Acquired by The Independents. Global network of affiliate vendors across fashion, art, music, architecture, and film.

notableWork.label: Bottega Veneta, Burberry, Calvin Klein, Chloé, Gucci, Loewe, Loro Piana, Marc Jacobs, Marni, Miu Miu, Prada, Valentino, Fondazione Prada

nodes.name: Kitty Events
nodes.bio: Paris-based agency specialising in the production of fashion shows and events. The fashion show production arm of Kitten Production, founded in 2015 by Jonathan Boyer and Romain Blanquet-Ferrero. Acquired by The Independents in September 2024, with both founders remaining at the helm. Also active in stills, motion, and digital content production for luxury and fashion brands, with offices in Paris, Barcelona, and Milan.
nodes.cv: Founded 2015 by Jonathan Boyer and Romain Blanquet-Ferrero. Acquired by The Independents, September 2024. Active in fashion show production, events, stills, motion, and digital content. Offices: Paris, Barcelona, Milan.

nodes.name: M/M (Paris)
nodes.bio: Paris-based creative agency founded in 1992 by Mathias Augustyniak and Michael Amzalag, who met as students at the École Nationale Supérieure des Arts Décoratifs in 1988. Develops creative communication, image, design, and spatial strategies across music, fashion, visual art, film, graphic design, interiors, and furniture. Named creative directors of Harper's Bazaar Italia from September 2025.
nodes.cv: Founded 1992 by Mathias Augustyniak and Michael Amzalag. Key collaborators: Björk, Madonna, Kanye West, Yohji Yamamoto, Nicolas Ghesquière, Jonathan Anderson, Sarah Burton, Calvin Klein, Stella McCartney, Riccardo Tisci, Pierre Huyghe, Philippe Parreno, Inez van Lamsweerde & Vinoodh Matadin. Creative directors, Harper's Bazaar Italia from September 2025.

notableWork.label: Art direction: Björk, Madonna, Benjamin Biolay — album covers and visual identity
Fashion: Nicolas Ghesquière (Balenciaga/Louis Vuitton), Yohji Yamamoto, Jonathan Anderson, Riccardo Tisci

nodes.name: SHOWstudio
nodes.bio: Pioneer fashion film platform founded by photographer Nick Knight in November 2000 — predating YouTube and social media. Recognised as the leading force behind fashion film as a medium, offering a unique platform for fashion's engagement with moving image. First to livestream a fashion show: Alexander McQueen's 'Plato's Atlantis' in 2009. Celebrated its 25th anniversary in 2025 with a new Knight film and coverage in i-D, 10 Magazine, and Fashionista.
nodes.cv: Founded November 2000 by Nick Knight. Innovations: first fashion film platform; first fashion show livestream (McQueen, 2009); early experiments with 3D imaging, avatars, and AI. Collaborators: Alexander McQueen, John Galliano, Björk, Kate Moss, Lady Gaga, Rick Owens, A$AP Rocky, Rei Kawakubo, Travis Scott, Virgil Abloh, Kanye West. Named creative directors of Harper's Bazaar Italia from 2025 (M/M Paris).

notableWork.label: Alexander McQueen 'Plato's Atlantis' — first ever livestreamed fashion show (2009)
Fashion film platform — defining the medium since 2000; contributors include M/M Paris, Inez & Vinoodh, Nick Knight
25-year anniversary film, 2025 — covered by i-D, 10 Magazine, Fashionista, EE72

nodes.name: Cadence Films
nodes.bio: Global creative and production company founded 2014, representing an exclusive roster of directors trusted by the world's top advertising agencies for high-end commercial, music video, and branded content. Sister company Cadence Image and partnership with Management+Artists extends coverage into luxury stills and premium advertising. Works direct-to-brand on 360 campaigns from concept to delivery — broadcast, stills, social, experiences, and immersive VR.
nodes.cv: Founded 2014. Managing Partner/EP: Carole Guenebeaud. EP: Romain Nougaret. Director roster: Barbara Anastacio, Paul Herrmann, Quentin Jones, Mathieu Kassovitz, Damien Krisl, Ronan McKenzie, Barnaby Roper, Mitch Ryan, Amalia Ulman, Wim Wenders, NDVD, Anton Tammi. nodes.name: Iconoclast
nodes.bio: International award-winning production company founded 2011 in Paris and Los Angeles, specialising in film, TV, commercials, music videos, and print. Offices in Paris, LA, London, Berlin, São Paulo, Mexico City, Barcelona, Milan, and Dakar. Regular winners at Cannes, Ciclope, and D&AD — including Production Company of the Year at Ciclope 2022 and top-five Palme d'Or for production companies five years running.

Feature films: 'The World Is Yours' (Gavras), 'Don't Worry He Won't Get Far on Foot' (Van Sant), 'The Beach Bum' (Korine), 'At Eternity's Gate' (Schnabel). Ciclope Production Company of the Year 2022. Joint venture with StickUp Films for Germany (2022, via Variety).

nodes.name: The Sunshine Company
nodes.bio: Strategic consultancy, design studio, and creative agency specialised in the convergence of brand and entertainment. Offices in Culver City, LA and London (Shepherdess Walk, N1). Focuses on brand-led transformation, cultural strategy, entertainment development, and brand consultancy and design. Tagline: 'Don't interrupt the things people love, be the thing people love.'

nodes.name: August Barron / ALL–IN
nodes.bio: Creative partnership of Benjamin Barron (American photographer, b. NY) and Bror August Vestbø (Norwegian designer), based in Paris since 2021. Barron founded ALL–IN in 2015 after graduating from Bard College; Vestbø joined the project and they launched the womenswear label August Barron in 2019. The magazine is an annual limited-edition indie publication with no advertising, each issue printed in a different format and exploring themes around fashion, art, and culture. The clothing brand — built on upcycled and vintage-reconstructed pieces — has grown into a cult label stocked at Dover Street Market, 10 Corso Como, and Ssense, with runway shows styled by Lotta Volkova.
nodes.cv: ALL–IN magazine: founded 2015 by Benjamin Barron (Bard College); annual, limited-edition, no advertising. August Barron label: launched 2019; first show at Maryam Nassir Zadeh's NYC store. Based in Paris since 2021. Runway styled by Lotta Volkova. Collaborations: Guess U.S.A. archives, Bernadette Corporation, Miguel Adrover, Ann-Sofie Back, Romeo Gigli. Stockists: Dover Street Market, 10 Corso Como, Ssense. SS26 show: Montparnasse Tower (40th floor), 'Uptown Girl' theme.

notableWork.label: ALL–IN issue 1 cover: Willem Dafoe; latest cover: Lily-Rose Depp. Contributors: Haley Wollens, Natasha Stagg, Bianca Raggi, Torbjørn Rødland, BLESS, Aurel Schmidt, Elysia Crampton
W Magazine profile, March 2025 — 'How All–in Went from Indie Magazine to the Fashion Brand Everyone's Talking About'; i-D — '10 Radical and Romantic Years of Ben and August'
August Barron label — signature pieces: double pump shoe, level boot (ankle/knee/thigh), Carrie and Dolly vintage clasp bags, deconstructed denim. Lotta Volkova: customer → runway stylist

nodes.name: Figures
nodes.bio: Representation firm for 'a new class of cultural figures whose influence isn't measured by algorithms but by how their ideas move through taste communities, cultural leaders, peers, and institutions.' Founded by Jacqui Kavanagh and Leila McGlew. Profiled by the Wall Street Journal (March 2026) as a firm for 'the alternatively influential' — new-media writers, specialist founders, academics, community leaders, and multi-hyphenates with real cultural clout beyond follower counts.
nodes.cv: Founded by Jacqui Kavanagh and Leila McGlew. WSJ profile, 12 March 2026: 'A new representation firm for public thinkers and tastemakers who have real clout in their own demesnes despite only modest internet followings.' Contact: hello@figures.company.

notableWork.label: Roster: Sari Azout (Sublime.app, AI & creativity), Kim Lê Boutin (New Ways of Seeing Festival / Loadmo.re, ex-Kenzo digital AD), Kat Chan (Full Serving, nutrition — Vogue/HTSI), James Davis (Drawn Distant, gaming & style, ex-Highsnobiety)

Seb Emina (Read Me, ex-The Happy Reader editor — Fantastic Man, The Gentlewoman, Feeld/AFM), Severin Matusek (Co-Matter, digital communities), Daniel-Yaw Miller (Sportsverse, ex-BoF — fashion × sport), Lucy Kumara Moore (The Sensual World, ex-Claire de Rouen owner)

Jaime Perlman (More or Less Magazine; ex-British Vogue CD, American Vogue, Harper's Bazaar; VP Brand Image Tory Burch; CD Vyrao), Mindy Seu (Cyberfeminism Index; UCLA professor), Kristoffer Tjalve (Naive Weekly, Tiny Awards, Internet Phonebook)

nodes.name: Mitch Ryan
nodes.bio: Director and artist born in Baltimore, MD (1988), based in New York and Paris. Known for his colorful, otherworldly, playfully surreal music videos — most notably his long-running collaboration with Rosalía, whose videos have accumulated hundreds of millions of views. Represented globally by Cadence Films for commercials and music videos. Also works on high-polish brand campaigns and a long-form documentary chronicle of Rosalía's tour.
nodes.cv: b. Baltimore, 1988. Based New York / Paris. Represented by Cadence Films (global, commercial + MV). Known for: Rosalía music video collaboration (Grammy-winning artist). Brand work: Collina Strada, Glossier. Contact via Cadence: lorenzo@cadence-films.com.

notableWork.label: Rosalía — long-running music video collaboration; hundreds of millions of views; Grammy-winning context
Collina Strada and Glossier — high-polish contemporary brand campaigns
Long-form documentary chronicle of Rosalía's tour (in progress); first-ever commercial representation via Cadence Films

nodes.name: Thistles
nodes.bio: New York City eyewear line by New Zealand-born stylist and photographer Thistle Brown. Contemporary takes on traditional silhouettes — a heritage brand sensibility with a raw NYC edge. Frames handmade in Japan and China. Stocked at Café Forgot, Bona Drag, Dover Street Market, SSENSE, One of a Few, Recital, HAVN, Simon James (NZ), BOMI NYC, and others. Brown's first NYC job was assisting a Greenwich Village optometrist, cutting and dyeing frames.
nodes.cv: Founded by Thistle Brown (New Zealand-born, New York-based stylist and photographer). Brown represented by Total Management. Styling clients: Proenza Schouler, Acne Studios, Chanel, Calvin Klein, Marc Jacobs. Press: Harper's Bazaar, Vogue, Dazed, Interview, Arena Homme+, Purple, Document Journal. Cultured Mag profile, June 2023. Collaborated with Maryam Nassir Zadeh.

notableWork.label: Eyewear styles: The THISTLE (Wayfarer riff, hand-tooled silver), The AL, The ATLAS, The CRUZ, The JACK — all unisex
Stocked: Café Forgot, Bona Drag, SSENSE, One of a Few, Recital, HAVN, BOMI NYC, Simon James (NZ)
Cultured Mag launch profile June 2023; styling credits in Harper's Bazaar, Vogue, Dazed, Interview, Purple, Document Journal

nodes.name: Callan Malone
nodes.bio: New York-based writer and stylist. Pursuing an MA in Cinema Studies at NYU's Tisch School of the Arts. Writes across fashion, culture, and design for Cultured Magazine, i-D, GQ, Document Journal, and LaLa Magazine. Also maintains a Substack. Works across both editorial writing and fashion styling.
nodes.cv: Writer and stylist, New York. MA Cinema Studies, NYU Tisch. Publications: Cultured Magazine, i-D, GQ, Document Journal, LaLa Magazine, Substack.

notableWork.label: Writing: Cultured Magazine, Document Journal, i-D, GQ, LaLa Magazine
Styling work — portfolio at callanmalone.com/styling

nodes.name: SUB
nodes.bio: Berlin-based architecture office designing spaces that register and reframe contemporary culture. Founded in 2017 by Creative Director Niklas Bildstein Zaar (Swedish) and Andrea Faraguna. Organised into four departments: Architecture, Entertainment, Digital, and Research. Advances a synthetic approach integrating emerging technologies, semantic and behavioural research, and traditional architectural techniques. Global practice with projects across Europe, North America, and Asia-Pacific.
nodes.cv: Founded 2017, Berlin. Creative Director: Niklas Bildstein Zaar (Swedish; broad background in fashion and cultural production). Co-founder: Andrea Faraguna. COO: Ilaria Peloso. Principal Architect: Roula Assaf. Digital Director: Chris Blohm. Design Director: Sophia Kuhn. Departments: Architecture, Entertainment, Digital, Research.

notableWork.label: Balenciaga — comprehensive ongoing relationship: 10+ store designs (Aoyama, Collins St, George V, Madison Ave, New Bond St, Sloane St, Piazza di Spagna, Taikoo Li, Via Tornabuoni, Plaza 66, Kurfürstendamm, Paradise Plaza) + fashion shows (SS/FW 2020–2024)
Entertainment/cultural: Travis Scott Utopia; Travis Scott Circus Maximus Pompei; Ye Donda 2; Ye Free Larry Hoover; Anne Imhof (Natures Mortes, Youth, Sex, Sex 02)

Cultural institutions: Serpentine Galleries, La Biennale di Venezia, Palais de Tokyo, The Metropolitan Museum of Art, Tate Modern, Stedelijk Museum Amsterdam; Other clients: Gucci, Marni, Maison Margiela, Mugler, Kering, Tom Ford, Ferragamo, Ottolinger, Mercedes-Benz, Holly Herndon & Mat Dryhurst, Anonymous Club

nodes.name: Robin Peckham
nodes.bio: Arts writer, gallerist, and curator based in Taipei. Studied Modern Culture and Media at Brown University and Media Art Histories at Danube University Krems. Founded Saamlung Gallery in Hong Kong in 2011 (closed 2013). Former Editor-in-Chief of LEAP magazine. Co-Director of Taipei Dangdai Art & Ideas. Executive Director of the forthcoming JD Museum in Shenzhen. Co-curated the influential Art Post-Internet survey at UCCA Beijing (2014). Named in Apollo Magazine's 40 Under 40 Asia Pacific as a Thinker (2016). Featured on Blouin ArtInfo's 'China's 30 in Their 30s.'
nodes.cv: Brown University (Modern Culture and Media); Danube University Krems (Media Art Histories). Roles: director Boers-Li Gallery Beijing; curator Kunsthalle Kowloon; project associate Long March Space Beijing; founder Society for Experimental Cultural Production (2008); founder Saamlung Gallery HK (2011–2013); Editor-in-Chief LEAP; Co-Director Taipei Dangdai; Executive Director JD Museum Shenzhen (forthcoming). Apollo 40 Under 40 Asia Pacific, 2016.

notableWork.label: Art Post-Internet — co-curated survey exhibition, UCCA Beijing, 2014 (influential early survey of post-internet art)

Saamlung Gallery, Hong Kong (2011–2013): Charles LaBelle, Tsang Tsou Choi, Matt Hope, Nadim Abbas, João Vasco Paiva, Adrian Wong; youngest exhibitor, Art Futures, Hong Kong International Art Fair 2012
Writing: Artforum (incl. Cao Fei review 2024), Ran Dian, LEAP, Yishu, Pipeline, ArtSlant; books: monographs on Zhang Peili and MAP Office; conversation with Nicole Eisenman at Hauser & Wirth

nodes.name: New Museum
nodes.bio: Contemporary art museum founded in 1977 by Marcia Tucker, committed to new art and new ideas. Located on the Bowery in Manhattan. The only museum in New York City dedicated exclusively to contemporary art. Known for championing emerging and under-recognised artists and experimental curatorial approaches. Current Executive Director: Lisa Phillips (since 1999). Building by SANAA (Kazuyo Sejima and Ryue Nishizawa), opened 2007.
nodes.cv: Founded 1977 by Marcia Tucker. Director: Lisa Phillips. Building: SANAA, opened 2007, Bowery, NYC. Programmes: New Museum Triennial ('Generational'), IdeasCity, New Inc (cultural incubator). Known for: first US retrospectives and critical platforms for artists overlooked by major institutions.

notableWork.label: New Museum Triennial (Generational) — defining survey of emerging global artistic voices
New Inc — New York's first museum-led cultural incubator (art, tech, design)
IdeasCity — urban festival and think tank on the future of cities and culture

nodes.name: Whitney Museum of American Art
nodes.bio: Leading museum dedicated to American art, founded in 1930 by sculptor and patron Gertrude Vanderbilt Whitney. Located in the Meatpacking District / West Village, Manhattan, in a building designed by Renzo Piano (opened 2015). Home to one of the most significant collections of 20th and 21st century American art, with over 25,000 works. Hosts the biennial Whitney Biennial — America's most prominent survey of contemporary art.
nodes.cv: Founded 1930 by Gertrude Vanderbilt Whitney. Director: Scott Rothkopf (since 2021). Building: Renzo Piano, opened 2015, Gansevoort St, NYC. Collection: 25,000+ works, 20th–21st century American art. Flagship programme: Whitney Biennial (est. 1932).

notableWork.label: Whitney Biennial — est. 1932; America's most prominent and debated contemporary art survey, held every two years
Permanent collection highlights: Edward Hopper, Georgia O'Keeffe, Jasper Johns, Cy Twombly, Jean-Michel Basquiat, Kara Walker, Glenn Ligon
Building: Renzo Piano, opened 2015; prior home: Marcel Breuer building on Madison Ave (now the Met Breuer / Frick Madison)

nodes.name: MoMA
nodes.bio: The Museum of Modern Art, founded in 1929, is the world's most influential modern and contemporary art museum. Located in Midtown Manhattan. Holdings include painting, sculpture, drawing, prints, photography, film, media, performance, design, and architecture. Collection of over 200,000 works. The defining global institution for canonising modern art, with programming that shapes international critical discourse.
nodes.cv: Founded 1929 by Lillie P. Bliss, Mary Quinn Sullivan, and Abby Aldrich Rockefeller. Director: Glenn Lowry (since 1995). Collection: 200,000+ works. Expanded building: Diller Scofidio + Renfro, 2019. Key departments: Painting & Sculpture, Film, Architecture & Design, Photography, Media & Performance, Drawings & Prints.

notableWork.label: Permanent collection: Picasso, Matisse, Van Gogh (Starry Night), Warhol, Pollock, Richter, Koons, Bourgeois — defining the modern canon
MoMA PS1 partnership (2000); Departments of Film, Architecture & Design, Photography — establishing field-defining canons across media
Expanded and reopened 2019 (Diller Scofidio + Renfro); annual MoMA film programming among most prestigious globally

nodes.name: MoMA PS1
nodes.bio: New York's leading contemporary art centre, housed in a former public school building in Long Island City, Queens. Founded as PS1 Contemporary Art Center in 1971 by Alanna Heiss; affiliated with MoMA since 2000. One of the largest and oldest institutions in the US devoted to contemporary art. Known for experimental, site-specific, and process-oriented programming, the WarmUp summer music series, and residency programmes.
nodes.cv: Founded 1971 as PS1 by Alanna Heiss; affiliated with MoMA 2000. Director (of MoMA PS1): Kate Fowle. Building: 1890s public school, Long Island City, Queens. Known for: site-specific installations, artist residencies, WarmUp summer music series (est. 1997), Young Architects Program (annual outdoor installation).

notableWork.label: WarmUp — summer music series (est. 1997): electronic, experimental, and club music in PS1's courtyard; culturally defining
Young Architects Program (YAP) — annual competition for emerging architects to design a temporary outdoor installation
Site-specific and experimental exhibitions; artist residencies; long-term relationship with international emerging artists

nodes.name: Tate Modern / Tate

location.raw: London, UK (+ Liverpool, St Ives, Britain)

nodes.bio: UK's national collection of international modern and contemporary art, operating across four sites: Tate Modern (London, Bankside), Tate Britain (London, Millbank), Tate Liverpool, and Tate St Ives. Tate Modern, opened 2000 in the former Bankside Power Station (Herzog & de Meuron), is the world's most visited modern art gallery. The Switch House extension opened 2016. Free admission to permanent collection.
nodes.cv: Tate Modern founded 2000 (Bankside Power Station, Herzog & de Meuron). Director: Maria Balshaw (since 2017). Switch House extension: 2016 (Herzog & de Meuron). Tate Britain est. 1897. Tate Liverpool est. 1988. Tate St Ives est. 1993. Collection: 70,000+ works, Turner to present. Tate Modern: world's most visited modern art gallery.

notableWork.label: Tate Modern Turbine Hall commissions — annual large-scale installation (Olafur Eliasson, Ai Weiwei, Kara Walker, Miroslaw Balka, etc.)
Collection highlights: Rothko room, Giacometti, Picasso, Bourgeois (Maman), Warhol, Beuys, Hirst, Emin
Hyundai Commission (Turbine Hall annual); Tate Britain Turner Prize (est. 1984) — UK's most prominent contemporary art award

nodes.name: Serpentine Gallery
nodes.bio: Leading London contemporary art gallery operating across two sites in Kensington Gardens, Hyde Park: Serpentine South (est. 1970) and Serpentine North (Zaha Hadid Galleries, est. 2013). Free admission. Artistic Director: Bettina Korek (Executive Director) and Hans Ulrich Obrist (Artistic Director). Internationally known for the annual Serpentine Pavilion commission — one of the most prestigious architecture commissions in the world — and for ambitious solo exhibitions and performance programming.
nodes.cv: Serpentine South est. 1970. Serpentine North (Zaha Hadid Galleries) est. 2013. Artistic Director: Hans Ulrich Obrist. Executive Director: Bettina Korek. Annual Serpentine Pavilion: est. 2000, premier architecture commission (Zaha Hadid, SANAA, Peter Zumthor, Selgas Cano, Frida Escobedo, Counterspace, Theaster Gates, etc.). Free admission.

notableWork.label: Annual Serpentine Pavilion (est. 2000) — architects include Zaha Hadid, Oscar Niemeyer, SANAA, Peter Zumthor, Selgas Cano, Frida Escobedo, Counterspace, Theaster Gates
Hans Ulrich Obrist — The Interviews (multi-decade series); Park Nights — performance and music programme in the Pavilion
Serpentine Galleries online and digital programme; major solo exhibitions: Harun Farocki, Etel Adnan, Cao Fei, Arthur Jafa, Cecilia Vicuña

nodes.name: White Cube

location.raw: London, UK (+ Hong Kong, New York, Paris, Seoul, West Palm Beach)

nodes.bio: Influential commercial gallery founded in 1993 by Jay Jopling in a small space in St James's, London. Now one of the world's most prominent contemporary art galleries with spaces in London (Mason's Yard; Bermondsey), Hong Kong, New York, Paris, Seoul, and West Palm Beach. Represents major international artists and has played a defining role in the careers of the YBAs (Young British Artists) including Damien Hirst and Tracey Emin.
nodes.cv: Founded 1993 by Jay Jopling, London. Spaces: London (Mason's Yard; Bermondsey Street), Hong Kong, New York, Paris, Seoul, West Palm Beach. Artists: Damien Hirst, Tracey Emin, Antony Gormley, Georg Baselitz, Chuck Close, Cerith Wyn Evans, Theaster Gates, Ibrahim Mahama, Raqib Shaw, Jake & Dinos Chapman, Gilbert & George, Darren Almond.

notableWork.label: YBA representation — Damien Hirst, Tracey Emin, Jake & Dinos Chapman, Gilbert & George: defining role in British contemporary art 1990s–2000s
Theaster Gates, Ibrahim Mahama, Antony Gormley, Georg Baselitz — major international programming
Global expansion: Hong Kong (2012), New York, Paris, Seoul — building international contemporary art market presence

nodes.name: ICA Los Angeles
nodes.bio: Non-collecting contemporary art institute based in Hollywood, Los Angeles. Opened 2017 as ICA LA after operating as a satellite of ICA Boston; became fully independent in 2019. Focuses on emerging and under-recognised artists, with a particular emphasis on LA-based and West Coast practices. Free admission. Known for experimental, process-driven, and socially engaged programming.
nodes.cv: Opened 2017 as ICA LA (satellite of ICA Boston); independent since 2019. Location: Hollywood, LA. Free admission. Focus: emerging and under-recognised artists, LA and West Coast practices. Executive Director: Hamza Walker.

notableWork.label: Free admission — access-focused programming for LA's diverse communities
Platform for LA-based emerging artists; experimental, socially engaged, and process-driven exhibitions
Independent from ICA Boston since 2019; distinct identity focused on West Coast contemporary practice

nodes.name: ICA London
nodes.bio: Institute of Contemporary Arts, founded in London in 1946 by Herbert Read, Roland Penrose, and others as a space for avant-garde art outside the mainstream. Located on The Mall, adjacent to St James's Park. One of the UK's most radical cultural organisations, presenting art, cinema, music, performance, talks, and digital projects. Has premiered work by Francis Bacon, Richard Hamilton, and was integral to the Independent Group, British Pop Art, and punk culture.
nodes.cv: Founded 1946 by Herbert Read, Roland Penrose, E.L.T. Mesens. Location: The Mall, London SW1. Programmes: exhibitions, cinema (ICA Cinema), live music, talks, and digital projects. Historical significance: Independent Group, British Pop Art origins, early exhibitions of Francis Bacon, Richard Hamilton, Paolozzi. Integral to punk culture and counterculture movements. Director: Richard Birkett (recent).

notableWork.label: Independent Group (1952–55) — birthplace of British Pop Art (Richard Hamilton, Eduardo Paolozzi, Reyner Banham)
Francis Bacon early exhibitions; punk and counterculture programming; ICA Cinema — independent and avant-garde film
Live music and performance: historically integral to British avant-garde, electronic, and experimental music scenes

nodes.name: MOCA Los Angeles
nodes.bio: The Museum of Contemporary Art, Los Angeles — the only major museum in LA devoted exclusively to contemporary art created after 1940. Founded in 1979, with three locations: Grand Avenue (Arata Isozaki building, 1986), the Geffen Contemporary at MOCA (former warehouse in Little Tokyo), and MOCA Pacific Design Center. Has housed defining exhibitions of Abstract Expressionism, Minimalism, and LA's own art scenes. Director: Johanna Burton (since 2021).
nodes.cv: Founded 1979, Los Angeles. Director: Johanna Burton (since 2021). Locations: Grand Avenue (Arata Isozaki, 1986), Geffen Contemporary (Little Tokyo), MOCA PDC. Collection: 8,000+ works, post-1940. Endowment challenges in 2000s and 2010s; stabilised with Eli and Edythe Broad support. Defining for West Coast post-war and contemporary movements.

notableWork.label: Permanent collection: Abstract Expressionism, Minimalism, Pop, LA artists (Ed Ruscha, Chris Burden, Barbara Kruger, Mike Kelley, Mark Bradford)
Geffen Contemporary — large-scale exhibitions and installations; original temporary home when Isozaki building was under construction
Director Johanna Burton (since 2021) — renewed focus on equity, access, and contemporary practice; building stabilisation after decades of financial challenges

nodes.name: Walker Art Center
nodes.bio: Multidisciplinary contemporary arts centre in Minneapolis, Minnesota. One of the most respected contemporary art museums in the United States, with a collection of over 13,000 works and programming spanning visual art, film, performing arts, design, and digital art. Founded as a public art gallery in 1879; significantly expanded and relaunched under director Martin Friedman. Landmark building by Edward Larrabee Barnes (1971) expanded by Herzog & de Meuron (2005). Home to the Minneapolis Sculpture Garden (est. 1988).
nodes.cv: Founded 1879; current form developed under director Martin Friedman (1961–1990). Building: Edward Larrabee Barnes (1971), expanded Herzog & de Meuron (2005). Collection: 13,000+ works. Minneapolis Sculpture Garden est. 1988 (home to Claes Oldenburg's Spoonbridge and Cherry). Programmes: visual art, film/video, performing arts, design, digital art. Director: Mary Ceruti (since 2019).

notableWork.label: Minneapolis Sculpture Garden (est. 1988) — home to Claes Oldenburg & Coosje van Bruggen's Spoonbridge and Cherry; iconic public art landmark
Herzog & de Meuron expansion (2005); Edward Larrabee Barnes original building (1971) — defining examples of museum architecture
Multidisciplinary programming: visual art, film/video, performing arts, design, and digital art — one of the most ambitious programmes outside NYC/LA

nodes.name: Art Institute of Chicago
nodes.bio: One of the oldest and largest art museums in the United States, founded in 1879. Located on Michigan Avenue on the edge of Millennium Park. Collection of over 300,000 works spanning 5,000 years of human creativity — among the finest encyclopaedic collections in the world. Particularly renowned for its Impressionist and Post-Impressionist holdings, its architecture collection, and the Ryerson and Burnham Libraries. Also home to the School of the Art Institute of Chicago (SAIC), one of the most prestigious art schools in the US.
nodes.cv: Founded 1879, Chicago. Director: James Rondeau (since 2016). Collection: 300,000+ works, 5,000 years. Building: 1893 World's Columbian Exposition; Modern Wing by Renzo Piano (2009). School: SAIC (School of the Art Institute of Chicago) — one of the most prestigious US art schools. Ryerson & Burnham Libraries — major research resource.

notableWork.label: Impressionist/Post-Impressionist collection: Seurat (A Sunday on La Grande Jatte), Picasso, Monet (water lilies series), Renoir, Caillebotte (Paris Street, Rainy Day)
Architecture and Design collection — one of the most significant in the world; Thorne Miniature Rooms; Textiles collection
Modern Wing by Renzo Piano (2009); Griffin Court and Nichols Bridgeway connecting to Millennium Park; SAIC — alumni include Georgia O'Keeffe, Grant Wood, Cynthia Rowley, David Sedaris

nodes.name: JD Museum
nodes.bio: Forthcoming art and technology museum backed by Chinese e-commerce giant JD.com, set to open in late 2027 in Shenzhen Bay. Will occupy over 10,000 square metres within JD's Shenzhen headquarters tower, designed by Büro Ole Scheeren. The multidisciplinary space will include facilities for exhibitions, live performances, immersive installations, workshops, and retail. Pre-opening public programmes launching 2026. Executive Director: Robin Peckham (formerly co-director of Taipei Dangdai and editor-in-chief of LEAP).
nodes.cv: Backed by JD.com. Opening: late 2027. Building: Büro Ole Scheeren (JD Shenzhen HQ tower), Shenzhen Bay. Scale: 10,000+ sqm. Executive Director: Robin Peckham (ex-Taipei Dangdai co-director 2019–2025; ex-LEAP editor-in-chief 2014–2019). Pre-opening public programmes 2026. Platform: art and technology, multidisciplinary.

notableWork.label: Büro Ole Scheeren architecture — JD Shenzhen HQ tower; 10,000+ sqm museum space within a corporate tech headquarters
Robin Peckham (ED) — curatorial vision rooted in contemporary Chinese art, digital culture, post-internet practice, and Asia-Pacific contemporary scenes
Pre-opening public programmes 2026; full opening late 2027; exhibitions, live performances, immersive installations, workshops, retail

nodes.name: The Broad
nodes.bio: Contemporary art museum on Grand Avenue, Downtown Los Angeles, founded by philanthropists Eli and Edythe Broad. Opened September 2015. Building by Diller Scofidio + Renfro (approx. 120,000 sq ft), known for its distinctive 'veil and vault' structure. Free general admission to permanent collection. Collection of 2,000+ works — one of the world's leading post-war and contemporary art holdings. Attracts a strikingly young and diverse audience.
nodes.cv: Founded by Eli and Edythe Broad. Opened 20 September 2015. Building: Diller Scofidio + Renfro, 120,000 sq ft, $140M. Director: Joanne Heyler (founding director). Free general admission. Collection: 2,000+ post-war and contemporary works.

notableWork.label: Collection highlights: Jean-Michel Basquiat, Jeff Koons, Yayoi Kusama, Barbara Kruger, Kerry James Marshall, Julie Mehretu, Takashi Murakami, Cindy Sherman, Cy Twombly, Mark Bradford, Ed Ruscha, Robert Rauschenberg, Jasper Johns, Andy Warhol
Building: Diller Scofidio + Renfro — 'veil and vault' structure; oculus skylight; Grand Avenue cultural anchor alongside MOCA, Disney Concert Hall, Broad Stage
Free admission; young and diverse audience profile; strong position in LA's Grand Avenue cultural corridor

nodes.name: Marciano Art Foundation
nodes.bio: Non-profit contemporary art foundation on Wilshire Boulevard, Mid-Wilshire, Los Angeles. Founded by Guess co-founders Maurice Marciano and Paul Marciano. Opened May 2017 in a converted 1961 Scottish Rite Masonic Temple (designed by Millard Sheets; conversion by architect Kulapat Yantrasast). Collection of 1,500+ contemporary works, focused on art from the 1990s to present. Free with advance timed-entry reservation.
nodes.cv: Founded by Maurice Marciano and Paul Marciano (co-founders of Guess). Opened 25 May 2017. Building: former Scottish Rite Masonic Temple (1961, Millard Sheets); converted by Kulapat Yantrasast. Collection: 1,500+ works, post-1990. Free with advance reservation.

notableWork.label: Collection highlights: Mike Kelley, Paul McCarthy, Takashi Murakami — focus on post-1990s international contemporary art
Building: former Scottish Rite Masonic Temple (Millard Sheets, 1961) — architecturally significant conversion by Kulapat Yantrasast
Founded by Guess co-founders; free admission; Mid-Wilshire location adjacent to Miracle Mile museum corridor (LACMA, BCAM, Petersen Automotive)

nodes.name: Fellowship

location.raw: London, UK / Los Angeles, USA / Marrakech, Morocco / Sardinia, Italy (Global)

nodes.bio: Contemporary art gallery partnering with artists defining the digital age — specifically those working with algorithms and artificial intelligence. No fixed gallery by design; activates spaces that serve the work: a converted Notting Hill chapel, a boutique hotel in Marrakech, a Porto Cervo summer gallery, and a Sunset Blvd LA space. Fair presence: Art Basel Miami Beach, Art Basel Hong Kong, Paris Photo, Zona Maco. Has placed work in permanent collections of MoMA, LACMA, Buffalo AKG, ZKM, and SFMOMA.
nodes.cv: Gallery model: no fixed permanent space — activates contextual venues globally. Locations: London (Westbourne Park Rd, Notting Hill, converted chapel); Marrakech (IZZA boutique hotel, Medina); Sardinia (Porto Cervo, via Aga Khan, est. 2007); Los Angeles (7503 Sunset Blvd). Residency: IZZA Associate Artists Program (year-long, Moroccan-based artists). Twitter: @fellowshipai / @fellowshiptrust.

notableWork.label: Holly Herndon & Mat Dryhurst with SUB — The Call, The Hearth (2024), installed Serpentine London; Ix Shells — No Me Olvides (2025), Art Basel Miami; Harold Cohen — AARON at Tsukuba (1985), installed London
Permanent collection placements: MoMA, LACMA, Buffalo AKG, ZKM (Karlsruhe), SFMOMA
Art fairs: Art Basel Miami Beach, Art Basel Hong Kong, Paris Photo, Zona Maco; IZZA Associate Artists residency (Marrakech) — Moroccan artists incl. Mouchine Rahaoui, Ismail Zaidy

nodes.name: Nitty Gritty
nodes.bio: Independent multi-brand retailer, online store, and showroom founded in Stockholm in 1991 as a counter-reaction to mainstream department stores. Carries mens- and womenswear, footwear, accessories, skincare, and fragrance. Men's store on Krukmakargatan — a street shared with Our Legacy and Uniforms for the Dedicated. Ships worldwide. Known for a tightly curated brand mix that balances cult labels with hard-to-find international names.
nodes.cv: Founded 1991, Stockholm. Independent multi-brand retailer and showroom. Men's store: Krukmakargatan (alongside Our Legacy, Uniforms for the Dedicated). Online store ships worldwide.

notableWork.label: Menswear brands: Stone Island, APC, Levi's, New Balance, Filson, Mismo, Boglioli
Womenswear brands: Isabel Marant, APC, Comme des Garçons, Preen, Jerome Dreyfuss
Footwear, accessories, skincare, and fragrance — curated selection of hard-to-find international labels

nodes.name: Lea Boberg
nodes.bio: Danish-born, London-based womenswear and menswear designer. Graduated from the Royal College of Art; previously worked at Casely-Hayford. Founded her label in 2020. Makes in England — fabrics sourced locally in Britain or from Japan's advanced weavers. Operates primarily as made-to-order with a London atelier, producing a ready-to-wear offering stocked at a tight selection of global retailers. Known for intentionally roomy tailoring with frayed raw-edged lapels, voluminous blazers, and a quiet, functional sensibility outside of established seasons.
nodes.cv: Founded 2020. RCA graduate; ex-Casely-Hayford. Made in England (British or Japanese fabrics). Made-to-order model; London atelier. Ready-to-wear stocked at: Ven.Space (New York), Neighbour (Vancouver), Maiden Shop (Japan), Lakewood Clothing, Localers. Expanding 2026 with 'Issue' series — stable styles in leftover materials. SS27 collection (latest as of June 2026). Contact: studio@leaboberg.com.

notableWork.label: Signature pieces: RPL blazer (double-breasted, close-together buttons, raw edges); RNL blazer (single-breasted, oversized, handcrafted raw edges); slouchy matching trousers; wide-drape shirts; deadstock wool ties
'Issue 01' (2026) — capsule of stable styles in leftover/deadstock British and Japanese fabrics; available direct via leaboberg.com
Highsnobiety profile May 2026: 'How Lea Boberg, the Future of Tailoring, Is Slowly Expanding'; prior Highsnobiety feature on made-to-measure tailoring for a new generation

nodes.name: Ven.Space

location.raw: Carroll Gardens, Brooklyn, New York, USA

nodes.bio: Independent luxury menswear boutique at 369 Court Street, Carroll Gardens, Brooklyn — founded by Christopher Green, former GMM of Need Supply Co. and Totokaelo. A distinct-POV destination store with no e-commerce, no web shop; the site lists only its address and brand roster. Interior features Noguchi lamps, terracotta pots, Comme des Garçons books, a vintage Dieter Rams–designed Braun Atelier record player, visible stockrooms, an in-house ceramics line, and curated monthly playlists. Considered one of New York's best new menswear stores.
nodes.cv: Founded by Christopher Green (ex-GMM Need Supply Co. and Totokaelo). Location: 369 Court Street, Carroll Gardens, Brooklyn. No e-commerce — physical-only. Highsnobiety: 'Brooklyn's Next Great Menswear Store'; Highsnobiety 'Future of Retail Is Offline' feature; WWD profile.

notableWork.label: Brands stocked: Auralee, Casey Casey, Comme des Garçons Homme, Diemme, Dries Van Noten, Lemaire, Maison Margiela, Our Legacy, The Row, Lea Boberg
Interior: Noguchi lamps, terracotta pots, CDG books, vintage Dieter Rams Braun Atelier record player, visible stockrooms, in-house ceramics
No e-commerce by design — 'a self-sustainable business without too much pressure'; destination-only physical retail model

nodes.name: David McKendrick
nodes.bio: Graphic designer and art director running David McKendrick Studio, a UK-based practice specialising in intelligent, editorial communication for fashion, art, design and culture clients. Also creates Paperboy Magazine, a biannual publication combining established writers, photographers and artists with high school and undergraduate contributors. Visiting lecturer at Glasgow School of Art, Bath School of Art and Design, Manchester School of Art, Norwich School of the Arts and Nottingham Trent University; Honorary Doctor of Design, Southampton Solent University (2015).
nodes.cv: Clients include Hermès, Dunhill, Manolo Blahnik, Christie's, Mulberry, Mr Porter, Esquire, The Times, V&A, White Cube, Rizzoli, Hatje Cantz, Issey Miyake, Camper, Somerset House, Connolly, John Lobb, Studio Nicholson. Jury member, ECAL (Switzerland). Registered in England and Wales (David McKendrick Limited, No. 14083425).

notableWork.label: Paperboy Magazine — biannual independent publication combining world-renowned artists and writers with student contributors
North Exhibition at Somerset House; V&A Tartan project; photobook for Maxime Ballesteros

nodes.name: The Culture Journalist

location.raw: Philadelphia, PA / Los Angeles, CA, USA

nodes.bio: A biweekly podcast and Substack by journalists Emilie Friedlander and Andrea Domanick, exploring culture in the age of platforms. Each episode features a guest to uncover the technological, economic, and political forces lurking beneath 21st-century music, film, style, and media. Over 6,000 subscribers, with praise from The Guardian and NPR Music's Ann Powers.
nodes.cv: Emilie Friedlander: formerly Pitchfork, The FADER, and VICE (led electronic music vertical, lead story editor for culture content); co-founded Brooklyn concert promoter AdHoc and editorial studio Moon Phase; freelanced for NYT and The Guardian. Andrea Domanick: head of KCRW Digital Editorial; formerly Noisey/VICE West Coast Editor, KPCC, LA Weekly, Las Vegas Sun; published in LA Times, Billboard, Playboy, Rolling Stone; avid field recordist and sound artist.

notableWork.label: "Is counterculture still possible on the internet?" (ep. with Carly Busta); "Toward a unified theory of the girlboss" (ep. with Liz Franczak)
"Reality, fiction, and Luigi Mangione" (ep. with Joshua Citarella); "How groceries got gentrified" (with Snaxshot's Andrea Hernandez)
CUJOPLEX Discord community; monthly Cultural Weather Report series; Andrea Domanick's two-year VICE investigation into sexual misconduct in the music industry
